name: Laurenz V.  Singing Gruner Veltliner
desc_en_short: A very attractive fruit bouquet yields apple, peach and citrus aromas along with a typical Veltliner spiciness and a touch of white pepper. The soft and juicy palate is supported by fine acidity. It sings on the palate!
desc_en_full:
The Variety

Grüner Veltliner is Austria's showpiece grape. It yields concentrated, harmonious wines rich in finesse and with a long life potential. Veltliner wines are reliable in their development. They bring pure enjoyment in their youth, and guarantee utmost drinking pleasure in maturity.

Austria represents less than 1% of the world's wine production. Therefore, gaining recognition as a wine country means that a few points must be emphasized. Grüner Veltliner makes up a third of Austria's entire wine production - making it the ideal ambassador for a clear Austrian wine profile - both at home and abroad. Lenz Moser III - the grandfather of Laurenz Maria Moser - always propagated this variety, and found it to be an optimal grape for the Lenz Moser Hochkultur, or high culture, vine training system, which he developed.

name: Granmonte  Crémant Méthode Traditionnelle Extra Brut
desc_en_short: Our crémant has a subtle yet captivating nose of apple, pear, lime peel with hints of fresh dough and cashew. The bubbles are delicate on the tongue with crisp and drying acidity that cleanses you palate especially when enjoy with food. Our style of crémant is extra brut with only a balancing sweetness.
desc_en_full:
GranMonte is proud to be the first and only producer of crémant (sparkling wine) in Khao Yai, Thailand. Our crémant is crafted in limited number from 100% Chenin Blanc grapes by methode traditionelle. The grapes were hand-picked in the cold January for natural acidity and gently whole-bunch pressed to retain freshness. Secondary fermentation in individual bottles allowed the wine to naturally effervesce. Each bottle had its wine rested on lees for up to 18 months before being hand-riddled, disgorged, and corked in its original bottle. The long and enduring process resulted in a crémant with a soft, light bead, balanced fruit, and yeasty aroma. The textured finish is punctuated by crisp, drying acidity.

Our crémant has a subtle yet captivating nose of apple, pear, lime peel with hints of fresh dough and cashew. The bubbles are delicate on the tongue with crisp and drying acidity that cleanses you palate especially when enjoy with food. Our style of crémant is extra brut with only a balancing sweetness.

name: Bottega  Moscato Vino dell'Amore Spumante (200 ml)
desc_en_short: Bottega's Vino dell'Amore — 'Wine of Love' — is a delicate, sweet sparkling Moscato from Veneto in its iconic pink bottle, bursting with fresh peach, apricot, rose petal, and honey aromas with a light, frothy fizz and gentle sweetness.
desc_en_full:
Bottega's Vino dell'Amore, the 'Wine of Love', is as charming to look at as it is to drink. Presented in Bottega's signature decorated bottle, this sweet sparkling Moscato captures the essence of Italian romance: light, floral, and generously fragrant.

Made from Moscato Bianco grown in northeastern Italy's Veneto region, the wine is vinified using the Charmat method to preserve its fresh, aromatic character. Pale golden yellow with fine persistent bubbles, it opens on intense aromas of peach, apricot, orange blossom, rose petal, and a gentle hint of honey and fresh fruit candy. The palate is lightly sweet, soft, and ethereally light — just 7% alcohol — with a clean, refreshing finish.

The 200ml format makes it a perfect gift, a romantic gesture, or a solo celebratory treat. Ideal with desserts, fresh fruit tarts, macarons, light pastries, or served as a sweet aperitif.

name: Antinori  Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso IGT
desc_en_short: Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso is the entry-level red from the Antinori house — a smooth, fruit-forward Sangiovese blend that embodies the character of Tuscany in an accessible, everyday style with bright cherry, plum, and spice.
desc_en_full:
Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso is one of Antinori's most welcoming wines — an approachable, versatile Tuscan red that introduces the house style without demanding years of cellaring. Predominantly Sangiovese with a small addition of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, it is aged in both large and small oak to deliver a well-rounded, softly structured wine.

The colour is a vivid ruby-red. On the nose, fresh morello cherry, plum jam, and violets are lifted by subtle spice and a gentle hint of vanilla from the oak. The palate is smooth and round, with moderate tannins, good acidity, and a clean, fruit-driven finish that makes it effortlessly drinkable.

This is a reliable choice for the dinner table — excellent with pasta, pizza, grilled sausages, medium cheeses, and everyday Tuscan fare.

name: Antinori  Chianti Classico "Peppoli" DOC
desc_en_short: Peppoli is one of the most celebrated Chianti Classico estates, now managed by the Antinori family. This Sangiovese-dominant blend delivers the hallmark character of the appellation — sour cherry, dried herbs, and earthy depth — with an open, generous texture.
desc_en_full:
Peppoli is an historic estate in the Chianti Classico zone, acquired by the Antinori family and managed with the same dedication to quality that defines the entire Antinori portfolio. The vineyard sits at altitude in the hills between Florence and Siena, where the combination of galestro and alberese soils and cool nights preserves the elegance and freshness that Chianti Classico is celebrated for.

Peppoli Chianti Classico is a Sangiovese-dominant blend, complemented by a small percentage of Merlot and Syrah, aged in both barriques and large oak casks. The result is a wine of genuine charm: ruby-garnet in colour, with a nose of fresh cherry, dried violets, wild herbs, and a hint of tobacco. The palate is medium-bodied with vivid acidity, smooth tannins, and a clean, savoury finish.

A versatile food wine, equally at home with pizza, pasta al ragù, grilled meats, charcuterie, and aged Italian cheeses.

name: Antinori  Tignanello IGT
desc_en_short: Tignanello is one of Italy's most iconic Super Tuscans, crafted by the legendary Antinori family from their Santa Cristina estate in Chianti Classico. A bold Sangiovese-led blend with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, it pioneered the use of non-traditional varieties and French barriques in Tuscany.
desc_en_full:
Tignanello is not merely a wine — it is a landmark in the history of Italian viticulture. When Marchese Piero Antinori first released it in the early 1970s, he broke decisively from tradition: no white grapes, no large Slavonian oak casks, no DOC classification. What he created instead was a blend of Sangiovese with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, aged in small French barriques, that would go on to define an entire category — the Super Tuscan.

The wine comes from the Tignanello estate in Chianti Classico, where the unique combination of galestro and alberese soils, combined with altitude and optimal sun exposure, creates the conditions for extraordinary red wines. The Sangiovese provides the structural backbone and sour-cherry vivacity characteristic of the region, while the Cabernet grapes add depth, dark fruit, and cedar complexity.

In the glass, Tignanello presents a deep garnet with violet highlights. The nose is expressive and layered — plum, blackcurrant, violet, tobacco, and warm spice with hints of leather and graphite emerging as it opens. On the palate, it is full-bodied with firm but polished tannins, excellent acidity, and a long, mineral finish. This is a wine built for the cellar but deeply pleasurable with a decade's patience. Pair with bistecca alla Fiorentina, roast lamb, aged Parmigiano, or rich game dishes.

name: "Tormaresca" By Antinori  Torcicoda Salento IGT
desc_en_short: Torcicoda is Tormaresca's flagship Primitivo — a rich, full-bodied southern Italian red bursting with dark plum, blackberry, chocolate, and warm spice, crafted from old-vine Primitivo grown in the sun-baked soils of the Salento peninsula.
desc_en_full:
Torcicoda takes its name from the local word for the Primitivo grape — literally 'twisted tail', referencing the grape's distinctive bunch formation. Sourced from vineyards on the Salento peninsula in the deep south of Puglia, where Primitivo thrives in the intense heat and calcareous clay soils, this is one of Tormaresca's most acclaimed wines.

The grapes are harvested at full ripeness and fermented before ageing in French oak barriques. The result is a wine of considerable depth and warmth: deep ruby-purple, with a rich nose of blackberry, dark plum, dried fig, cocoa, cinnamon, and a subtle leather undercurrent. On the palate, it is full-bodied, velvety, and generous, with smooth, ripe tannins and a long, spiced finish.

Perfect alongside grilled lamb chops, beef short rib, aged hard cheeses, slow-cooked game, and hearty pasta dishes with meat-based sauces.

name: Antinori  Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
desc_en_short: Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva is one of the historic wines from Tuscany's most famous dynasty — a concentrated, structured Sangiovese from the heartland of Chianti Classico, aged to develop complexity while maintaining the grape's vivid cherry-fruit character.
desc_en_full:
The Antinori family has been making wine in Tuscany for over 600 years, and Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva represents one of their most traditional expressions. Sourced from estate vineyards in the Chianti Classico zone, this Riserva is dominated by Sangiovese with a small complement of Cabernet Sauvignon, aged in French and Hungarian oak barriques before extended bottle maturation.

Deep ruby in the glass, it offers an expressive nose of morello cherry, plum, dried rose petal, and Mediterranean scrubland, layered with cedar, tobacco, and a hint of leather. On the palate, it is structured and generous — full-bodied with Sangiovese's characteristic high acidity and firm but refined tannins, leading to a long, warming finish.

Classic accompaniments include bistecca alla Fiorentina, Chianina beef, wild boar ragu, aged Pecorino Toscano, and truffle-based dishes.

name: Tenuta Guado al Tasso By Antinori  COn't Ugo Bolgheri DOC
desc_en_short: Con't Ugo is the second wine of Antinori's prestigious Guado al Tasso estate in Bolgheri, offering Bordeaux-style depth and elegance at a more accessible level. Expect lush dark fruit, cedar, and Mediterranean spice with smooth, well-integrated tannins.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta Guado al Tasso is one of the jewels of the Antinori portfolio — a coastal Bolgheri estate where the proximity to the Tyrrhenian Sea and the unique terroir of gravelly alluvial soils create ideal conditions for Bordeaux varieties. Con't Ugo is the estate's second wine, drawing on the same approach and philosophy as the flagship but crafted for earlier accessibility.

The blend, typically Cabernet Sauvignon-led with Merlot and Syrah, offers a distinctly Mediterranean character: warm, ripe, and generously fruited, but never heavy. Aged in French oak barriques, it develops notes of dark plum, blackberry, dried herbs, cedar, and a whisper of tobacco — all framed by smooth, polished tannins and vibrant acidity that keeps it lively.

This is a wine that performs well young but rewards a few years' patience. Serve with roasted meats — lamb shoulder, beef tenderloin, wild boar — or rich pasta dishes with ragu.

name: Tenuta di Biserno  Insoglio del Cinghiale DOCG
grape_variety: Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Pecorino
desc_en_short: Insoglio del Cinghiale is the second wine of Biserno, the prestigious Tuscan estate co-founded by Lodovico Antinori — a bold, elegant Bolgheri-area red blending Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon with impressive depth, warmth, and Mediterranean character.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta di Biserno was established by Lodovico Antinori, one of the pioneering figures of the Super Tuscan movement, in the coastal hills of the Etruscan Coast near Bolgheri. Insoglio del Cinghiale — 'the boar's puddle' — is the estate's second wine, crafted from the same approach and terroir that produces the flagship Biserno, but designed for earlier drinking.

The blend, typically Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon, reflects the estate's continental-Mediterranean terroir: warm, generous, and complex. The wine is aged in French barriques to develop structure and nuance while preserving its fresh fruit core. Deep ruby-purple in colour, it offers an expressive nose of blackberry, cassis, warm spice, dried herbs, cedar, and earthy minerals. On the palate, it is medium-full bodied with polished tannins, vibrant acidity, and a long, savoury finish.

Best served with roasted lamb, beef short rib, wild boar ragu, aged Pecorino, and hearty pasta dishes with meat sauces.

name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut (200 ml)
desc_en_short: Bottega Gold is one of Italy's most recognisable sparkling wines — a dry, elegant Prosecco Brut in its iconic gold metallic bottle, delivering fresh pear, apple blossom, citrus, and delicate yeasty notes with fine persistent bubbles and a clean finish.
desc_en_full:
Bottega Gold Prosecco has become a global icon — as recognisable for its gleaming gold metallic bottle as for the quality inside. Made from Glera grapes grown in the Prosecco DOC zone of the Veneto and Friuli, it represents the Bottega family's commitment to both quality and presentation.

Vinified using the Charmat (tank) method to preserve its natural fruit character and freshness, the wine is pale straw yellow with a persistent perlage of fine bubbles. On the nose, it is delightfully fresh and aromatic — green apple, ripe pear, citrus zest, white flowers, and a subtle hint of bread — all characteristic of fine Prosecco. The palate is dry, light-bodied, and refreshing, with clean acidity and a pleasantly brief, fruity finish.

The 200ml format is ideal for individual servings, celebrations, and gifts. Perfect as an aperitif, with light antipasti, sushi, fresh salads, or seafood. A classic Prosecco Spritz starter.

name: Bottega  Spumante Rose Gold Brut (200 ml)
desc_en_short: Bottega Rose Gold is the rosé sibling of the iconic Gold Prosecco — a pale pink Italian sparkling wine in a shimmering rose-gold bottle, offering fresh red berry, rose petal, and citrus aromas with lively bubbles and a dry, elegant finish.
desc_en_full:
Bottega Rose Gold brings the same glamour and craft as the original Gold Prosecco but with a softer, rosé character — the instantly recognisable rose-gold metallic bottle now housing a delicate pink sparkling wine that is as striking to present as it is to drink.

Made from a blend of Glera and red varieties grown in northeastern Italy, this Spumante Brut Rosé displays a pale, onion-skin salmon hue with fine, lively bubbles. The nose is fresh and feminine — wild strawberry, raspberry, rose petal, and a hint of citrus — with a clean, yeasty background. The palate is dry, crisp, and refreshing, with the fruit character staying bright through to a clean, short finish.

The 200ml format is perfect for celebrations, as a gift, or as a stylish aperitif. Pairs beautifully with strawberries, light desserts, raw fish, smoked salmon, and cheese boards.

name: "Tormaresca" By Antinori  Chardonnay IGT
desc_en_short: Tormaresca Chardonnay is a fresh, approachable white from Antinori's southern Italian estate in Puglia — showing ripe tropical fruit, citrus zest, and a hint of toasty oak, with generous texture and a clean, lively finish.
desc_en_full:
Tormaresca is the Antinori family's venture into Puglia's sun-drenched heel of Italy, where the warm climate and maritime influence of the Adriatic and Ionian seas create conditions for rich, characterful wines. Their Chardonnay reflects both the warmth of the south and the winemaking precision that the Antinori name demands.

Vinified and partially aged in oak, the wine shows ripe yellow apple, mango, and citrus fruit alongside a subtle butterscotch and toasted almond note from the wood contact. The palate is broad and textured, with good weight and refreshing acidity that keeps the wine lively. It finishes clean and fruity with just enough length to satisfy.

An ideal partner for seafood pasta, grilled prawns, mild curries, roasted chicken, and soft fresh cheeses.

name: Elena Walch  Pinot Grigio Alto Adige D.O.C.
desc_en_short: Elena Walch's Pinot Grigio is one of Alto Adige's finest expressions of the variety — precise and Alpine in character with crisp green apple, pear, mineral slate notes, and a distinctive floral elegance that sets it apart from flatter interpretations of the grape.
desc_en_full:
Elena Walch is one of Alto Adige's most celebrated wine producers, farming steep mountain vineyards in the Caldaro and Tramin area where the combination of Alpine altitude, intense sun, and cool nights creates ideal conditions for aromatic, high-quality white wines.

Her Pinot Grigio is a benchmark for the Alto Adige style — a more precise, mountain-influenced interpretation than you find further south. Pale straw with green highlights, it opens on fresh pear, crisp Granny Smith apple, citrus zest, and white flowers, backed by a clean, stony mineral note that speaks clearly of the region. The palate is dry, taut, and refreshing, with vibrant acidity, moderate body, and a clean, mineral finish.

Excellent with grilled fish and seafood, light pasta dishes with cream sauces, chicken, goat's cheese, and charcuterie. Equally enjoyable as an aperitif.

name: Jermann  Pinot Grigio DOC
desc_en_short: Silvio Jermann's Pinot Grigio is a benchmark example from Friuli-Venezia Giulia — a fresh, elegant white with crisp green apple, pear, and delicate floral notes, underpinned by clean minerality and a pleasing, food-friendly finish.
desc_en_full:
Jermann is one of Friuli-Venezia Giulia's most celebrated estates, and their Pinot Grigio stands as a classic example of the region's ability to produce white wines with both character and immediacy. Unlike flatter versions of this grape grown further south, Jermann's Pinot Grigio benefits from the cooling Adriatic influence and the estate's careful canopy management, resulting in a wine with genuine freshness and definition.

Pale straw in colour with golden glints, the wine opens on fresh green apple, ripe pear, and subtle white peach, lifted by delicate floral notes of acacia. The palate is crisp and clean, with refreshing natural acidity, a lightly saline mineral thread, and a dry, lingering finish. This is a wine of understated elegance — not showy, but deeply satisfying.

Versatile at the table, it excels with grilled fish and seafood — particularly sole, sea bass, and langoustines. It also works beautifully with light pasta dishes, risotto with vegetables, and fresh goat's cheese.

name: Tequila Rose  Strawberry Cream (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Tequila Rose is the original strawberry cream liqueur. Combining tequila with strawberry cream was a bold innovation, the first drink of its kind to pair cream with anything other than Irish whiskey. Exotic. Unexpected. Delicious. Over twenty years later, Tequila Rose remains the world’s best-selling strawberry cream liqueur and was the inspiration that paved the way for the unique cream liqueurs that have followed.
desc_en_full:
Tequila Rose is a silky smooth pairing of rich strawberry cream and the thrilling bite of tequila. It hits the perfect note every time, whether you prefer sweet or seductive. Fearless or fun. Lush or light-hearted.

With its elegant design and playful details, Tequila Rose is the “little black dress” of cream liqueurs. Bold black glass keeps the light out to ensure the freshness and the flavor, while shimmery silver roses and bright pink strawberries make this bottle shine on the shelf. It’s a timeless and alluring classic.

name: Veuve du Vernay  Brut
wine_color: Pale-yellow colour with hints of green.
desc_en_short: "Fresh on the nose, vibrant aromas expressing a delicate blend of floral and fruit-driven notes. Rich and full on the palate, extended finish of fruity notes, very light and refreshing finish showing clean minerality and citrus."
desc_en_full: Veuve du Vernay sparkling wine has an intimate link to the history of French sparkling wine. Mr. Jean Eugène Charmat, the French scientist, who in 1907 invented the cuve close ("sealed vats") method of producing fine sparkling wine which has since been adopted worldwide, had a high regard for a widow in the village of Vernay who helped him to start his business. When Eugène Charmat's son Robert created a new sparkling wine of high quality in the 1960s, he named it in honor of the lady whom his father esteemed so highly. Today Veuve du Vernay is a major brand and is widely known internationally. Crisp and fruity , soft , balanced finish.A great value.

name: Havana Club  3 Years Rum (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Havana Club 3 Anos is the most prestigious of all white rums. It brings a touch of unparalleled class to all cocktails and blends. Havana Club 3 Anos is the result of blending aged and aromatic sugarcane aguardientes with extra light sugarcane distillates to produce a selection of young rums. This batch of rums is then allowed to rest in white oak barrels after which the Maestro Ronero chooses the best barrels and blends the final three-year-old rum, which is again set to rest, then filtered and bottled.

The perfect premium white rum for creating authentic Cuban cocktails like the Mojito and the Daiquiri. Distilled from Cuban sugarcane, the rum is naturally aged for at least 3 years in white oak barrels specially selected by the Maestros Roneros. This ageing process in a tropical climate gives Havana Club 3 Year Old its special balance of lightness and character for an unmistakable Cuban flavour.

Sod Chaeng Story

from a locally-owned small-batch distillery in Ubon Ratchathani Province, Thailand dedicated to pay homage to the hard work and wisdom of the local farmers

Sod Chaeng Distillery was started with the purpose of supporting local communities by adding value to their agricultural products and creating sustainable local employment for everyone from farmers, visual artists to musicians. This results not only in career development opportunities but also Thai-Isan culture promotion.

Sod Chaeng turned the local success of high-quality organic agricultural products, enabled by natural effective microorganisms (EM), into premium local spirits.

Combining over 10 years of experience in liquor creation with traditional Isan wisdom, Sod Chaeng has successfully developed premium spirits

Tasting Notes

Nose : Aromatic fresh Coconut

The Palate : Smooth, fresh and clean coconut flavor

The Finish : Sweet and crisp finishes

name: Chateau Leoville Poyferre Saint-Julien Grand Cru Classé
desc_en_short:
The blessing of a great terroir

As the devastating effects of frosts have a serious impact on the viticultural landscape of Bordeaux, Saint-Julien can give praise thanks to Saint Vincent, patron Saint of the vineyard workers, blessed by his protection from the worst of the exceptional frosts and safeguarded for the duration of the vintage.

2017, not too dissimilar to its companions of misfortune in sevens, started off on the wrong foot. A very dry winter and late severe spring frosts were then turned around with a warm pleasant summer.

This beautiful weather coupled with our unique terroir really raised the bar for our 2017 vintage. The result is a wine that is dense, marked with an exquisite aromatic expression, all wrapped in a vintage with surprising cellaring potential. An outsider, quite contrary to its reputation!

At 895 metres altitude you can almost touch the stars and shape the clouds. The dusk air is intense with fragrance. And the sun lights up the land in all its splendour. Here, at a height of 895 metres, Celeste is born, the Torres family's first Ribera del Duero.

name: Gilbey's  Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The recipe dates to the 1870s, when the Gilbey brothers, two veterans of the Crimean war, whose successful wine/spirits merchant business had thrived throughout the second half of the 19th century, expanded their business to include the eponymous gin (called Gilbey’s since 1895). Distilled from grain neutral spirits, it purportedly contains 12 botanicals. It was so popular that the rights to distill it were sold to the US in the 1930s and it has been distilled domestically since then.

name: De Kuyper  Blue Curacao (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The essence of this liqueur is the Curaçao or Lahara fruit, famous for being inedibly bitter. Luckily, it is also incredibly useful in creating a distinctive liqueur. Having covered the 'Curaçao', what about the 'blue'? Honestly, blue made this liqueur famous, but doesn't do anything for the taste. For all we care, this might as well have been Clear Curaçao – no one would taste the difference.
desc_en_full:
De Kuyper Royal Distillers ,founded in 1695 by Petrus De Kuyper, is a privately held Dutch company in the business of manufacturing and marketing distilled spirits and liqueurs.

In 1995, on the occasion of its 300th anniversary, the company received the title "Royal" from Queen Beatrix of the Netherlands. This led to the company changing its name from Johannes de Kuyper & Zoon to De Kuyper Royal Distillers. In the same year, Erven Warnink - the leading producer of advocat and cream liqueurs – was taken over by De Kuyper Royal Distillers.

The essence of this liqueur is the Curaçao or Lahara fruit, famous for being inedibly bitter. Luckily, it is also incredibly useful in creating a distinctive liqueur. Having covered the 'Curaçao', what about the 'blue'? Honestly, blue made this liqueur famous, but doesn't do anything for the taste. For all we care, this might as well have been Clear Curaçao – no one would taste the difference.

Signature cocktail

Bikini Martini

Blue Monday

name: Monsoon Valley  Shiraz
desc_en_short: Classic varietal characters of dark plum, cherries, toasted coffee and spices. Medium body is perfected with lovely fruity mid-palate and very ripen tannin. After being aged in oak barrel,the fruits and sweet spicy flavors become more round and leave the good body at the end.
desc_en_full:
Taste & Aroma

Made from carefully selected Shiraz grapes, Monsoon Valley Shiraz is slowly matured in German and French oak barrels to develop rich flavors with ripe plum and sour cherry aromas and coffee notes. The wine is rich, well-balanced, structured with medium body,and soft tannin level.

Food Recommendation

Can be enjoyed alone or to accompany grilled lamb,red sauce pastas, spicy grilled chicken, beef curry (Panang Nua), or duck with red curry (Kang Pet Phed Yang)

name: Fontanafredda  Langhe Nebbiolo Ebbio DOC
desc_en_short: When young, it features a fragrance which is wonderfully spontaneous and full of surprise. In its bouquet, colour, and generous, velvety body this langhe Nebbiolo brings out all the typical qualities of the magnificent vine it is made from. Later in life its colour becomes garnet-red with delicate orange shades, and its nose deepens, with traces of withered flowers, such as roses and, above-all, geraniums. Its warm, soft, well-bodied flavour is velvety and well-balanced, lingering in the mouth.
desc_en_full:
Vinification:

Obtained by traditional fermentation carried out in stainless steel containers floating, at a controlled temperature (28° C), for an average of 10-12 days.

Ageing:

The wine ages in wood for at least four months and then in the bottle for at least one or two months. The wine peaks immediately and remains constant for at least three to four years after harvest, varying considerably from vintage to vintage.

name: Cantina Terlano  Quarz Sauvignon Blanc DOC
desc_en_short:
Cantina Terlano Quarz

"Delicate as the fine inclusions of quartz in Terlano’s volcanic porphyry rock – that is our Sauvignon Selection, which does full justice to its name. Quarz offers a combination of fine texture, depth and salty aftertaste that has given this noble white an international reputation." - Rudi Kofler

Vinification

Manual harvest and selection of the grapes; gentle whole cluster pressing and clarification of the must by natural sedimentation; slow fermentation at a controlled temperature and aging on the lees for nine months partly in big wooden barrels (50%) and partly in stainless steel tanks (50%); blending three months before bottling.

name: Cantina Terlano  Winkl Sauvignon Blanc DOC
wine_color: Intensive straw yellow with a delicate green shimmer.
desc_en_short:
Intensive straw yellow with a delicate green shimmer. Ripe fruit aromas of apricot, mandarin and passion fruit mingle with elements of elderberry, nettle and mint. The fruitiness continues on the palate in combination with a fine acidity. The wine
scores with a good structure and a minerally but at the same time aromatic finish.
desc_en_full:
Winkl

"Winkl is a juicy, delicately fruity, minerally Sauvignon Blanc, which has been produced as a varietal wine since 1956. In the Terlano DOC area, this grape variety has long been grown with excellent results in the oldest vineyards." Rudi Kofler

Taste

The fruitiness continues on the palate in combination with a fine acidity. The wine scores with a good structure and a minerally but at the same time aromatic finish.

Simple pairings

An interesting combination in spring with boiled white asparagus in Bolzano sauce as well as with steamed green asparagus; also with tuna carpaccio, smoked salmon and raw langoustines; and a typical South Tyrolean choice with nettle dumplings, as well as poached trout with vegetables or young goat's cheese.

Detailed pairings

Foam of white Terlano asparagus with quail eggs and bitter almond - Giancarlo Perbellini (Ristorante Perbellini), Char on white asparagus, pea stock, mint and char roe - Norbert Niederkofler (Ristorante St. Hubertus)

name: Ron Zacapa  Centenario Sistema Solera 23 Years (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Wonderfully complex, generous and full-bodied, with a sweet honeyed viscosity atypical of an aged spirit; a great depth of raisined fruit and apricot preserves, building to an intense heart of savoury oak, nutmeg, leather and tobacco with notes of coffee and delicately sweet vanilla, balanced with a spicy touch of cinnamon and ginger on the pleasantly astringent finish; truly a rum for the discerning palate.
desc_en_full:
Ron Zacapa Centenario Sistema Solera 23

Appearance :

Light mahogany, with the tones of long barrel ageing at the rim and long, slow legs clinging to the glass.

Nose :

A soft start which develops complexity in the glass; sweet aromas of caramel, vanilla, cacao and butterscotch, combining with layers of flavour indicative of the different barrels in the solera process; sherried notes of caramelised, roasted brazil nuts and toasted hazelnut, and the characteristic rounded toffeed banana and dried pineapple of ex-American whiskey casks.

Palate :

Wonderfully complex, generous and full-bodied, with a sweet honeyed viscosity atypical of an aged spirit; a great depth of raisined fruit and apricot preserves, building to an intense heart of savoury oak, nutmeg, leather and tobacco with notes of coffee and delicately sweet vanilla, balanced with a spicy touch of cinnamon and ginger on the pleasantly astringent finish; truly a rum for the discerning palate.

Serving Suggestions :

Best enjoyed on a single large lump of pure, clear ice in a luxury rocks glass with a heavy base.

name: Havana Club  7 Years Rum (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Crafted for sipping, to be enjoyed on the rocks and ideal for rich cocktails: Havana Club 7 Años is highly versatile. name: Graham's  10 Year Tawny Port
wine_color: Very deep purple in colour with an intense garnet edge.
desc_en_short: Graham’s 10 Year Old is a tribute to craftsmanship, the passion for excellence, and unmatched precision, which is at the heart of every handmade Tawny Port. After a decade of care, this wine is a wonderful expression of Tawny Port. For those who appreciate quality in every detail.
desc_en_full:
"10 Years" indicates an average age – this Aged Tawny Port is a blend of older wines, which offer complexity and younger wines, which bring fresh fruit flavors and vibrancy. During their long maturing period in oak casks, Aged Tawnies undergo subtle color changes: the deep red hue which characterizes Port's youth gradually gives way to a paler reddish amber color. Graham's is known for its rich, elegant house style. This 10 Year has a full, nutty bouquet, the classic Graham's richness and a soft lingering finish.

Serving and pairing suggestions

Graham's 10 Year is bottled ready to drink after an average of at least ten years of cask aging. It has a t-cap closure, which means that you don't need a corkscrew to open it and that it will stay fresh for two to four months if stored in a cool, dark place or refrigerator. Serve it in a glass with at least a six ounce capacity so that you may appreciate the wine's aromas. Graham's 10 Year is delicious when paired with milk chocolate, creme brulee or fruit tarts. In warmer months, try it chilled for a refreshing dessert in a glass.

desc_en_full:
19 Crimes Description

Nineteen crimes turned criminal into colonists. Upon conviction, British rogues, guilty of at least one of the 19 crimes, were sentenced to live in Australia, rather than death. This punishment by 'transportation' began in 1788, and many of the lawless died at sea. For the rough-hewn prisoners who made it to shore, a new world awaited. As pioneers in a frontier penal colony, they forged a new country and new lives, brick by brick. This wine is borne of the rules they broke and the culture they built. And like our legendary rogues, it's a taste that'll live forever in infamy.

Tasting Notes

Bright red to crimson hues. Intense lifted vanilla aromatics balancedwith ripe red to dark berry fruits. Full and round on the palate with a distinct sweetness giving a rich and round mouth feel. The vanilla aromatics carry through on the palate and compliment subtle flavors of chocolate and a cedary spice. The palate finishes soft and fruity with a pleasant lingering freshness.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2019
grape_variety: Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: Château Clerc Milon's 2019 Pauillac is a fifth growth of uncommon elegance, delivering layered aromas of blackcurrant, violet, and licorice alongside creamy, close-knit tannins and a saline, minty finish that lingers with impressive persistence.
desc_en_full:
Château Clerc Milon occupies a privileged corner of Pauillac, its vineyards nestled between the legendary estates of Mouton Rothschild and Latour on the Left Bank's Médoc peninsula. A Fifth Growth in the 1855 Classification, the estate has been owned by the Baron Philippe de Rothschild family since 1970, and under their stewardship it has steadily punched well above its classified rank. The 2019 vintage is widely regarded as one of Bordeaux's finest in recent memory — a warm, dry growing season that delivered exceptional ripeness and natural concentration without sacrificing the freshness that defines great Pauillac.

The blend is anchored by a dominant 72% Cabernet Sauvignon, enriched by 22% Merlot for mid-palate generosity, with Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot providing aromatic lift and structural depth. In the glass, it pours a vivid crimson-garnet, immediately signalling its ambition. The nose opens with refined floral notes — violet and dried rose petal — before airing reveals classic Pauillac blackcurrant and a streak of licorice root. On the palate, a full, smooth entry gives way to a complex layering of ripe dark fruit, toasted cereal, and subtle oak spice, all framed by high-quality, creamy tannins that feel both generous and precise. The finish is long, saline, and faintly minty, with a whisper of dark chocolate.

This is a wine built for the cellar — ideally enjoyed from 2026 onwards — though it rewards patient decanting now alongside slow-roasted rack of lamb, a well-aged côte de boeuf, or a platter of hard aged cheeses like Comté or aged Manchego.

desc_en_short: This is one of the wines that has made Allegrini famous throughout the world. name: Glaetzer  Bishop
desc_en_short:
Bishop is the family name of Colin's wife' Judith. Fittingly the sign of Venus, a symbol which has come to represent women and feminine energy, is the centrepiece of the Bishop label.

In mythology, the symbol meant the Roman goddess Venus or her Greek equivalent Aphrodite. But the sign has also been linked back to ancient Egypt – a nod to Bishop’s link to Amon-Ra and Anaperenna.
desc_en_full:
The Glaetzer History

The first Glaetzers settled in the Barossa Valley in 1888 after emigrating from Brandenburg, Germany. The family left Germany on board the Nord-Deutscher Lloyd steamer “Habsburg” and took seven weeks to reach their final destination of Port Adelaide. From here they settled in a country town called Nuriootpa in the Barossa Valley where they started their new life in Australia. The family were some of the earliest recorded viticulturalists in the Barossa Valley and Clare Valley.

Over a century later, winemaking patriarch Colin Glaetzer established Glaetzer Wines, a boutique family owned winery producing premium Barossa Valley wines. Today Colin’s son Ben carries on the wine making traditions at Glaetzer Wines. Indeed it was the birth of Glaetzer Wines that signalled a new era for Colin’s family which boasts more than its fair share of winemakers.

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

JACOB CREEK (เจค็อบ ครีก) เป็นไวน์สัญชาติ ถือกำเนิดในปี 1847 ณ เนินดินของ JACOB’S CREEK - JOHANN GRAMP ได้ปักชำกิ่งลงบนไร่องุ่นของเขา วิศัยทัศน์ของเขา คือการสร้างไวน์รสเยี่ยม ที่สะท้อนถึงตัวตนขององุ่นและผืนดิน จวบจนปัจจุบัน JACOB’S CREEK ยังสานต่อการคงอยู่และลมหายใจนั้น ด้วยการผลิตไวน์ที่จะแสดงเอกลักษณ์อันหลากหลายขององุ่น ที่สร้างขึ้นเป็นรูปเป็นร่าง ด้วยผืนดินและดวงตะวันของ AUSTRALIA

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci   1502 Da Vinci in Romagna Sangiovese Appassimento
wine_color: It shows an intense red color with hues that verge on ruby.
desc_en_short: During his journey across the region of Romagna in 1502, Leonardo observed and then painted the grapes of Cesena, which dried thanks to an ingenious hanging system. This same technique helps the grapes to achieve the concentration of flavour which is at the heart of the exceptional richness of this wine. Sangiovese grapes for drying are picked by hand in crates in mid-September, after a careful selection and a quality check. The grapes are allowed to rest in special fruit warehouses at controlled temperature and humidity where they are left to dry for at least sixty days. Wine-making starts with soft pressing followed by temperature-controlled fermentation on the skins at 25°C. Pumping-over and punching-down are performed to extract color and allow aromatic components to develop. Later on, racking is carried out to separate the skins from the must. Ageing includes malolactic fermentation to make wine smoother and rounder on the palate, after which one part of the wine is aged in barriques for a minimum period of nine months.
desc_en_full: Leonardo in 1502 spent six months of his life in Romagna. Following his journey, 1502 Da Vinci in Romagna is the line dedicated to the wines of the best Romagna vineyards produced following the indications of the Metodo Leonardo. A special tour in five cities, which touches places full of beauty: the one that Leonardo captured in the drawings made at each stage of his journey and shown on the labels.

desc_en_short: " Veuve du Vernay is a reflection of the French way of life; sophisticated, spontaneous, romantic, elegant and chic. A range of sparkling wines that are produced in Bordeaux, the heart of the French wine country. Sparkling wine has long been a part of everyday French life; an aperitif, a wine with food, a wine for celebrations, a wine that encompasses the French ‘joie de vivre’ in every glass, Veuve du Vernay is a reflection of this French way of life. "
desc_en_full: Veuve du Vernay sparkling wine has an intimate link to the history of French sparkling wine. Mr. Jean Eugène Charmat, the French scientist, who in 1907 invented the cuve close ("sealed vats") method of producing fine sparkling wine which has since been adopted worldwide, had a high regard for a widow in the village of Vernay who helped him to start his business. When Eugène Charmat's son Robert created a new sparkling wine of high quality in the 1960s, he named it in honor of the lady whom his father esteemed so highly. Today Veuve du Vernay is a major brand and is widely known internationally. Crisp and fruity , soft , balanced finish.A great value.

name: Tanqueray  Gin (750 ml)
desc_en_short: The vibrant juniper and licorice flavor and a pretty, slightly floral, rosewater finish is braced up with a peppery spine. Give this gin some time to open up for optimal aromatics—the reward is a citrusy zing. Good value.
desc_en_full:
"Based on a 180 year old recipe, Tanqueray® London Dry is widely acknowledged as the benchmark gin."

It starts with the man. Charles Tanqueray. The bold spirit who traded the church for the still and in the process, created one of the world's most awarded gins. Tonight we toast the bold spirit worthy of its seal and 180 years of distilling excellence

A blend of the purest triple-distilled spirit and a hand picked selection of four botanicals, London Dry Gin offers uniquely balanced gin experience. Said to have been Frank Sinatra’s preferred gin, London Dry’s edge and sophistication have long made it a favourite with the stars in fashion, film and music. Tanqueray’s iconic bottle design is a contemporary update of the original 1948 design, which in turn, was inspired by the shape of a classic cocktail shaker. The famous Tanqueray family crest embossed on the glass of each bottle features a pineapple, a historic symbol of hospitality and warm welcome.

"Tanqueray No. TEN is the only gin to be inducted to the San Francisco World Spirits Competition Hall Of Fame"

Passion and attention to detail in perfect measure, is a recipe for exquiste results.

desc_en_full: Charles Tanqueray created the world’s finest gin in 1830 and it is made today to the same classic, timeless recipe. The base of the bottle for Tanqueray No. Ten resembles a citrus press, symbolic of the fresh Mexican limes, Florida oranges and pink grapefruits that are distilled in every small batch of the gin.

name: Saint James  Royal Amber "Rhum Agricole" (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
St James Royal Ambre Rum is a rhum agricole from St James Plantation on Martinique. Matured in oak for 18 to 24 months, this has notes of spice and figs. This is fantastic in a Planter's Punch with mango and pineapple.

Rhum agricole is the French term for sugarcane juice rum, a style of rum originally distilled in the French Caribbean islands from freshly squeezed sugarcane juice rather than molasses. Rhum is the term that typically distinguishes it in French-speaking locales from the rum made with molasses in other parts of the West Indies.
desc_en_full:
Tasting notes :

Color : Warm, amber colour.

Nose: Round and elegant, with floral notes combined with hints of wood and spice typical of the ageing process.

Taste: Round and harmonious, very present in the mouth.

name: Kah  Tequila Reposado (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
This tequila has surprisingly subtle flavours of butter, vanilla and cinnamon. Exceptionally smooth and balanced like the blanco so easy to sip neat or as a base for classic cocktails.

Awards :

Gold Super Premium Reposado - 2018 : The Tequila & Mezcal Masters (The Spirits Business)
desc_en_full:
Information Product

Kah Day of the Dead Tequila Reposado Kah "Day of the Dead" Tequila Reposado KAH Tequila Reposado is an ultra smooth, and ultra high-proof Tequila with a full body, and intense Agave favor. It is aged for ten months in French limousine casks, where it acquires subtle hints of vanilla and caramel. The KAH Tequila Reposado bottle is painted as "El Diablo". The use of devil imagery was inspired by Day of the Dead celebrations in Peru. The festival takes place the week before November 5th on the banks of Lake Titicaca, The traditional dances (complete with dangling red crucifixes) are rumored to be led by the devil himself. Traditional dances are dedicated to Satan. This region of Peru is mostly a mining area and workers spend much time underground. According to ancient Incan beliefs, Satan lives underground making the miners vulnerable to his powers due to their daily proximity. The festival is designed to honor Satan, thus keeping the workers safe. The even pour Tequila on the ground for the devil to drink.

name: Aviva  Red Rose
desc_en_short:
SHAKE UP YOUR LIFE!!!

Tired of the routine, of the same plans, of the same people? Tired of taking your life so seriously? Why not stir up your life with a completely different drink?

We present you AVIVA, the most original sparkling wine drink in the world.

THE COLOURS

AVIVA has different colours inspired by our moods and in the colours of nature, just like when the life and soul of the party arrives and reaches a group and revolutioni- zes everything, AVIVA has been conceived to always be the protagonist.

FLAVOR

A fine bubble sparkling wine with a delicious taste on the palate, fresh, fruity and easy to drink. AVIVA reminds you of all of the flavors and aromas of the Mediterra- nean. Elaborated through natural processes and with a low alcohol content. Fo- llowing in the footsteps of the worlds wine experts and their know how.

A drink that shows the nuances inside and outside, the way we are, the taste of our grapes and our land, elements that make the difference with everything we have seen until that point.

We did not want it to make a typical prosecco, a cava or a moscato, we wanted it to be something unique, special and new.

The taste and the Mediterranean way, in never before seen format.
desc_en_full:
THE PRODUCT

The Sparkling Wine Drink

At Bodegas Torre Oria we think that there is no point in having a drink that is the same as all the others. AVIVA was created with the intention of being something completely different and original.

We dreamed of creating a wine that was surprising both inside and out, the result of a long process of work between winemakers, expert bartenders and product designers, has resulted in a unique product in the world.

It was not enough to develop an attractive label and bottle. Not just a delicious product. We suggested innovating with the texture of the product itself. A drink conceived from the point of view of design and with its content as its value. We needed to go further.

As a result, a new sparkling wine drink concept was born.

ORIGIN

Born in the Mediterranean. Shaking the World.

Like our climate, the character of our people, our Mediterranean customs and our parties … this is how we wanted AVIVA to be. A casual drink, for any time of the day, for anyone and to be enjoyed anywhere in the world.

A fun and eye-catching sparkling wine that encourages you to live experiences from the moment you hold it in your hands for the first time. Its effect when shaken and its unique texture make it a unique product in the market.

name: Laurenz V.  Charming Gruner Veltliner
desc_en_short: Grüner Veltliner is Austria's showpiece grape. It yields concentrated, harmonious wines rich in finesse and with a long life potential. Veltliner wines are reliable in their development. They bring pure enjoyment in their youth, and guarantee utmost drinking pleasure in maturity.
desc_en_full:
The Terroir

The grapes for this very charming wine are grown in the Kamptal region of Austria. Here, the Grüner Veltliner ripens in granite, gneiss, mica-slate as well as loess soils. And from the neighboring region to the north, the rugged Waldviertel , blows cool air that substantially lowers night temperatures in the Kamptal; this in turn slows up the ripening process of the grapes. But these conditions are exactly why the wines from this region taste so fresh and fruity. The New World calls the phenomenon "Cool Climate Viticulture". And wines from these cooler regions are highly respected and treasured.

The Potential

The Kamptal has nearly 4, 000 hectares of vineyards. Of these, more than 3/4 is planted with white wine vines, with 2, 000 hectares reserved for Grüner Veltliner alone. However, the fantastic potential of the Kamptal is still largely unknown internationally.

The Variety

Grüner Veltliner is Austria's showpiece grape. It yields concentrated, harmonious wines rich in finesse and with a long life potential. Veltliner wines are reliable in their development. They bring pure enjoyment in their youth, and guarantee utmost drinking pleasure in maturity.

Austria represents less than 1% of the world's wine production. Therefore, gaining recognition as a wine country means that a few points must be emphasized. Grüner Veltliner makes up a third of Austria's entire wine production - making it the ideal ambassador for a clear Austrian wine profile - both at home and abroad. Lenz Moser III - the grandfather of Lenz M. Moser - always propagated this variety, and found it to be an optimal grape for the Lenz Moser Hochkultur, or high culture, vine training system, which he developed.

name: Malfy  Con Arancia (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Malfy Con Arancia’s key botanical additions are Blood Orange peels sourced from Sicily.Blood Oranges are a prized variety in Sicily – and harvested in November – their rich red color develops from the cool Mediterranean nights.
desc_en_full:
Malfy Con Arancia's key botanical additions are Blood Orange peels sourced from Sicily.Blood Oranges are a prized variety in Sicily – and harvested in November – their rich red color develops from the cool Mediterranean nights.

The Blood Orange peels are steeped in alcohol and pressed in a basket press. The infusion is then blended with juniper and other botanicals before being distilled in a stainless steel vacuum still.

Our distillery

Our distillery is in Moncalieri, just outside the city of Torino - An area famed for its production of fine wines and spirits. Established in 1906, Torino Distillati is run by the Vergnano family, Carlo, his wife Piera and their children Rita and Valter. Beppe Ronco and Denis Muni are our Master Distillers.

name: De Kuyper  Butterscotch (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Butter, brown sugar and a quarter of treacle. That’s all you need to make some good old-fashioned butterscotch. But if you now think it should be evenly easy to create a butterscotch liqueur, think again. For just adding spirits simply won’t do. You want to keep that buttery caramel sensation, but leave out the sticky sauce part. Distil the taste, not the waste. Some challenge, but so we did.
desc_en_full:
MET MANHATTAN

Ingredients: 15 ml De Kuyper Butterscotch 60 ml Bourbon 30 ml Mandarine Napoleon 2 dashes Angostura orange bitters

Glass: martini Garnish: orange zest twist SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.

name: Castello Banfi  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
wine_color: Intense ruby red with garnet reflections.
desc_en_short:
WINEMAKER’S NOTES

A "classic" of our production, this Brunello di Montalcino is obtained with clones of Sangiovese selected after 20 years of research on this unique grape and is aged in French oak barrels of 350 lt as well as in 60-120 hl Slavonian barrels. The most aristocratic combination of Montalcino tradition with winemaking modernity.

PRODUCTION TECHNIQUE

The meticulous grape selection (yield not exceeding 6 metric tons/ha) is followed by vinification in temperature-controlled Horizon hybrid stainless steel & wood tanks, with skin contact for 10-12 days. The wine ages for 2 years in various sizes oak barrels, 350-liter barriques, and 60- and 120 hl barrels; the wine is subsequently aged in bottle for an additional 8-12 months before release.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Beyer Ranch Zinfandel
desc_en_short: “Our Beyer Ranch Zinfandel comes from three different estate vineyards in the Livermore Valley. Each vineyard is picked ripe to accentuate the deep jammy fruit flavors, yet the alcohol remains balanced. The wine features all of Zinfandel’s classic aromatics and full body.” - Karl D. Wente, Fifth Generation Winemaker
desc_en_full:
Vineyard

Since 1883, five generations of our family have handcrafted fine wines that express the very best of our vineyards and winemaking expertise. Our Estate Grown Beyer Ranch has historically been a premier Livermore Valley location for Zinfandel. The estate vineyards are planted on carefully selected sites on the sloping benches that lead to the banks of the Arroyo del Valle and Arroyo Mocho streams. Grapes from these Zinfandel vineyards produce luscious wines with deep richness.

Winemaking

Fermentation : Each variety was fermented separately in stainless steel fermenters and were pumped over two to three times daily to maximize extraction of flavors and colors.

Aging : The wine was aged in a combination of stainless steel tanks and neutral oak barrels to maintain the fruitiness and freshness of the wine.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Corton Grand Cru
desc_en_short: "Its colour is rather deep, its nose powerful and verging on candied fruit and spices when maturing. It has good structure in the mouth, without harshness. A good Corton must have "gras" (a velvety texture) to counterbalance tannin and acidity. On the palate, it recalls the candied fruit present in the nose. Very long and persistent, it ends on a very complex note".
desc_en_full:
Vineyard :

Site: Corton and the other Corton vineyards (such as Corton Bressandes) are the only red Grand Crus of the Côte de Beaune. They are located on the hill of Corton, clearly visible from miles around and capped by low-growing oak trees. The vines are on a rather steep slope with a good south-east exposure.

History & tradition: the name comes from "curtis" (the word for an estate in Gallo-Roman times) and Othon, the Emperor who owned this vineyard. Since the wine travels well, it soon acquired great fame around the world.

Soil: a mixture of clay and small rocks create a well-drained soil where the roots of the pinot noir grape have to dig very deep in order to find water and nutrients.

desc_en_short:
Bright red with purple hues. A round and vibrant Cabernet Sauvignon with plum and strawberry flavours combined with hints of red pepper and spices. The palate is smooth and well balanced, with a fruity and long lasting finish.

Sustainable Winery

ISO 9001-HACCP
desc_en_full:
ORIGIN : Chile Curicó Valley - Molina

GRAPE VARIETY : Cabernet Sauvignon proprietor's single vineyard grapes, from ungrafted selected clones of pre-phylloxera French rootstock.

HARVEST : April 28 to May 9, 2011 Hand-picked in 18 Kg. boxes and carried to the winery immediately after cutting. Carefully selected before processing.

VINIFICATION :

Fermented in 15.000 and 25.000 liter stainledd steel tanks for 7-10 days at 25-28ºC controlled temperature to dryness with selected and native yeasts (80/20).

Extended maceration for additional 3 - 5 days.

100% malolactic fermentation.

Stored in temperature controlled stainless steel tanks through bottling.

Serve between 18° and 20°C.

name: Feudo Maccari  Saia
desc_en_short:
Saia is bold yet balanced, showcasing black cherry, plum, cocoa, and tobacco leaf, framed by silky tannins and a mineral-driven freshness. The oak aging is well-integrated, giving a polished structure while preserving the earthy and sun-kissed nature of the grape.

Ideal for pairing with grilled meats, roasted vegetables, or aged cheeses, this wine offers a Mediterranean expression that’s both robust and refined.
desc_en_full:
Feudo Maccari is a Sicilian wine project by Antonio Moretti Cuseri, owner of Tuscany’s renowned Tenuta Sette Ponti. Located in Noto, in southeastern Sicily, the estate benefits from intense sun, volcanic soils, and coastal breezes from the Ionian Sea—ideal conditions for crafting powerful, expressive wines.

Saia is made from 100% Nero d’Avola, sourced from old alberello-trained vines (some over 30 years old), producing a wine of intensity, richness, and authenticity that reflects the rugged soul of Sicily.

name: Torbreck  Run Rig
desc_en_short:
Aubergine core with a deep purple rim. Aromatics leap from the glass with great finesse and power yet with precision and focus. The lifted plum, black currant and dark cherry along with Turkish delight, star anise, earl grey tea and black olive combine with gorgeous plumes of subtle vanilla pod and cardamom aromas. The palate is dense, firm and powerful yet holdsincredibly balanced fruit intermingled with voluminous structure. One of the better RunRig’s from lower than normal yields. A wine for the cellar best after 2025-2050.
Serve at 16C with delicious rich flavored cuisines.
desc_en_full:
Torbreck Run Rig

The RunRig has often drawn comparison with the beautifully fragrant & tautly structured wines produced from the steep slopes of the Northern Rhône Valley's Appellation of Côte Rôtie.

RunRig, although wonderfully aromatic, is a wine that exhibits so much power and latent richness that it could easily be mistaken for the hugely concentrated wines sourced from the sun drenched hill of Hermitage (the historic home of Syrah and some of the worlds most powerful and longest living wines).

Possessing a dense, saturated, almost ink like hue, the aromas of black raspberries, crème de cassis, smoke, graphite and melted tar soar from the glass. Full bodied with great intensity, amazing freshness and extraordinary concentration, the multi-layered palate displays the fruit purity that only the most fastidious farming can achieve.

desc_en_full:
Montgras Antu Ninquen

ANTU - "Sun" in the Mapuche native language-, is MontGras' new exploratory range of wines.

With the eagerness and curiosity to explore new places and grape varieties, ANTU represents the adventurous spirit of our Winemakers. In these wines, the identity and character of a very special terroirs, along with a signature winemaking style, is transferred to each bottle.

Our detailed vineyard mapping has enabled us to carefully select specific vineyard Blocks and lots within them, that give us limited yields of world-class grapes.

We carry out segmented harvests, hand-picking grapes at their optimum moment of ripeness to create wines that are full bodied, robust with vibrant fruit.

name: Tenuta delle Terre Nere  Etna Rosso Feudo Di Mezzo Il Quadro delle Rose DOC
desc_en_short: Feudo di Mezzo is the largest cru in the appellation, a homogenous character, but with clear qualitative variations – much like one could say of Clos de Vougeot in Burgundy. From the best parcels – such as our “Quadro delle Rose” - it betrays a dusky character, autumnal tones in its cedary-spicy bouquet. Always the fist to be harvested, Feudo di Mezzo is also always the one readiest to open up and generously deliver itself. Of all crus it is supremely silky, its tannins suave, its bouquet darkly debonair. I love it as it ages effortlessly, its crepuscular nature picking up light as it ripens. Poultry, pork, game, lasagne, braised meats.
desc_en_full: Feudo di Mezzo is the largest cru in the appellation, a homogenous character, but with clear qualitative variations – much like one could say of Clos de Vougeot in Burgundy. From the best parcels – such as our “Quadro delle Rose” - it betrays a dusky character, autumnal tones in its cedary-spicy bouquet. Always the fist to be harvested, Feudo di Mezzo is also always the one readiest to open up and generously deliver itself. Of all crus it is supremely silky, its tannins suave, its bouquet darkly debonair. I love it as it ages effortlessly, its crepuscular nature picking up light as it ripens. Poultry, pork, game, lasagne, braised meats.

desc_en_short: This full and round bright-red wine with crimson hues give way to a subtle sweetness on the palate. The criminally intense vanilla aromatics are balanced with ripe raspberry and plum fruits. The vanilla aromatics carry through on the palate and complement subtle flavors of raspberries, dark plums and chocolate. desc_en_full:
19 Crimes Description

Nineteen crimes turned criminal into colonists. Upon conviction, British rogues, guilty of at least one of the 19 crimes, were sentenced to live in Australia, rather than death. This punishment by 'transportation' began in 1788, and many of the lawless died at sea. For the rough-hewn prisoners who made it to shore, a new world awaited. As pioneers in a frontier penal colony, they forged a new country and new lives, brick by brick. This wine is borne of the rules they broke and the culture they built. And like our legendary rogues, it's a taste that'll live forever in infamy.

Tasting Notes

This full and round bright-red wine with crimson hues give way to a subtle sweetness on the palate. The criminally intense vanilla aromatics are balanced with ripe raspberry and plum fruits. The vanilla aromatics carry through on the palate and complement subtle flavors of raspberries, dark plums and chocolate. Red berry fruit and plush tannins provide a lingering finish perfect for any evening.

name: 19 Crimes  Malbec
desc_en_short: 19 Crimes Malbec delivers a richly textured, full-bodied pour of dark cherry, red currant, and chocolate, rounded out with a whisper of vanilla sweetness and soft, lingering tannins — a bold red built for the table and the story behind it.
desc_en_full:
Few wine labels carry a backstory as audacious as 19 Crimes. The brand takes its name from the nineteen offences under British law that, in the late 18th century, sentenced convicted criminals not to death but to transportation — a one-way voyage to the penal colony of Australia beginning in 1788. Many never survived the crossing. Those who did became unlikely pioneers, laying the foundations of a nation with their own roughened hands. Every bottle is a nod to that defiant, hard-won resilience.

This Malbec brings the grape's Argentine heartland character to the label's distinctly antipodean spirit. In the glass it presents as a deep, inky red, immediately inviting with aromas of ripe dark cherry, red currant, and a warm undercurrent of baking spice and vanilla oak. On the palate it is firm and full-bodied yet approachable, with dark fruit taking centre stage alongside hints of dark chocolate and a subtle sweetness that rounds out the mid-palate beautifully. Soft, medium tannins guide the wine to a smooth, lingering finish.

This is a crowd-pleasing red that punches above its price point and pairs effortlessly with grilled ribeye, slow-roasted lamb shoulder, or a smoky barbecue spread. It holds its own alongside aged hard cheeses as well. Pour it at any gathering where bold flavours and good conversation are guaranteed — because like the rogues who inspired it, this wine refuses to be forgettable.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Merlot
desc_en_short:
Intense aromas of autumn berry fruits combine nicely with notes of black pepper and dried fruits. An easy-drinking and juicy wine that persists on the fruity finish.

Sustainable Winery

ISO 9001-HACCP
desc_en_full:
Vinification:

Fermented in 15.000 and 25.000 liter stainless steel tanks for 7-10 days at 25-28ºC controlled temperature to dryness with selected and native yeasts (80/20). 100% malolactic fermentation. Aged in 225 liter French used barrels for about 4-5 months. Stored in temperature controlled stainless steel tanks through bottling.

Roberto Echeverría Jr has been the Chief Winemaker at Viña Echeverría since 2001. Inspired by his French heritage, Roberto’s signature style produces wines with a combination of Old World elegance and New World energy. More European viticultural techniques include timely harvesting to avoid over ripeness and therefore good balance and control of alcohol levels. Focus is on French varietals, barrels and yeasts, as well as longer fermentation and aging in barrels and diverse blending.

Their Primitivo di Puglia showcases the best of this ancient southern grape — believed to be genetically identical to California's Zinfandel — in a generous, approachable style.

Vinified in stainless steel to preserve fruit purity with brief oak ageing to add roundness, the wine displays a deep ruby colour and a nose of dark plum, blackberry jam, wild cherry, and cocoa, with hints of cinnamon and dried herbs. An honest, hearty wine for grilled meats, lamb, mature hard cheeses, beef burgers, and pasta with meat ragù.

name: Penfolds  Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz (750 ml) Limited Relese Lunar New Year Snake Edition
desc_en_short:
Celebrate the Year of the Snake with an iconic Bin 389 Magnum presented in a Lunar New Year-inspired gift box.

Bin 389 was often referred to as ‘Baby Grange’, in part because components of the wine are matured in the same barrels that held the previous vintage of Grange. First made in 1960 by the legendary Max Schubert, this was the wine that helped forge Penfolds reputation with red wine drinkers by combining the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon with the richness of Shiraz. Exemplifying the judicious balance of fruit and oak, Bin 389 highlights the generous mid-palate Penfolds is known for.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Note

Colour

Black core, purple rim.

Nose

A kaleidoscope of aromas. First noted, sweet pastries. Blueberry danish and chocolate croissant. Sugar dusting. The fruits of the forest are quick to emerge, fresh blackberries and mulberries dominant. There is the faintest suggestion of formic acid, Max Schubert would approve. Dried bay leaf, pink peppercorns and black cardamom add spicy intrigue. The imagination conjures thoughts of rare roast beef with black pepper crust, seared venison loin, coal dust, cocoa powder and graphite. With a swirl, aniseed/licorice notes become pronounced.

Palate

A flawless integration of the two varietals, neither dominating but working together in harmony. The aroma descriptors deftly match the palate. Flavours of black fruit and red fruit compote abound. Cocoa powder sweetness adds weight to the substantial mid-palate. There are plenty of savoury flavours to balance out the sweetness, with cold sliced salted beef, roast lamb cooking juices and cured pastrami. The tannins are fine, reminiscent of oolong tea, providing plenty of structure. The delicious fruit is married seamlessly with the creamy American oak. A fine seam of acid provides a refreshing lift on the finish.

desc_en_full:
Brut Vintage Reserva from Freixenet The refined sparkling companion for vintage celebrations introduces an elegant new look to complement its distinctive Cava characteristics.

VINTAGE MOMENTS Brut Vintage is crafted for those special moments that call for celebration. Vintage moments, like an intimate candlelight dinner with close friends or private celebrations, when the occasion demands an elegant sparkling wine in a beautiful bottle. With its classic taste characteristics and striking presentation, Brut Vintage is the perfect companion for those vintage occasions.

CLASSIC PRESENTATION Our new packaging offers classic signals of quality recognized as the unique signature of Freixenet. The smaller label hints at exclusivity, the detailing at craftsmanship and the contrasting textures at sophistication. Subtlety and style are the hallmarks of this design, destined to grace the tables of dinner parties around the world.

TRADITIONAL CAVA What does it take to be worthy of the Brut Vintage appellation? This distinctive sparkling wine is crafted with grapes from a single harvest and matured in our underground caves for at least 24 months. The long méthode champenoise (also known as método tradicionale) aging process produces a sparkling wine of exceptional quality. Completely natural in composition with no added sugar, Brut Vintage is classic in flavor and texture.

TIMELESS CHARACTER Complex and full of character, our Brut Vintage has a delicate pale yellow hue that's very dry on the palate but with an intense richness in flavor — combining hints of honey, citrus and dried fruits. Our special cava has a fine and persistent mousse, just right for those vintage celebrations.

ELEGANT TASTE Brut Vintage is crafted from Macabeo and Paralleda grapes hand-harvested in 25kg containers, carefully crushed with pneumatic presses and fermented with custom yeast strains. The elegant wine is much appreciated by lovers of unsweetened cavas for its optimal aging and fine balance of acidity and body.

Alcohol: 12% Total acidity: 3.60 g/l pH: 3.01 Dosage: No dosage. Residual sugar ‹ 3 g/l

name: Opera Prima  Gold Moscato
desc_en_short: This distinctive sparkling wine boasts beautiful rose petal and lychee fruit aromas and luscious, silky-sweet flavors balanced by a crisp, refreshing finish. Lovely to sip on its own, it’s also delicious with a wide range of desserts. Serve well- chilled.
desc_en_full:
The Vineyards : La Mancha is the largest vineyard in the world, occupying 50% of Spain's Vineyard. There is more "land under vine" here than in the entire countries of Australia and Chile combined. Included among our prolific vineyards are 50 hectares of experimental vineyards for research and development works with different grape varieties, both for young wines as Crianzas and Reservas. La Mancha is located on large plain and it is a dry area which often suffers from long drought periods. Soil is rich in limestone rock, original from miocene sediments. The combination of these conditions put stress on the vines which results in high character and quality.

desc_en_full:
Wine marking :

After a careful selection process, grapes undergo a gentle pressing to obtain only the highest quality must. Fermentation takes place in stainless steel tanks at very low temperatures in order to maintain the fresh, fruity character of a young wine. The white Moscato and red Tempranillo still wines are then blended and macerated in contact with Tempranillo skins for eight hours to obtain color and complexity. The still wine is then transferred to isothermal stainless steel tanks where secondary fermentation takes place using the Charmat method. This process last approximately one month.

desc_en_full:
Light and refreshingly sweet wine, perfect for any social occasions. Fragrant and refreshing, crisp with red summer berries.

Aroma

Strawberry, Raspberry, Lychee

Taste

Medium sweet with almost medium acidity, light to medium body and medium to long finish

Complimentary

Spicy salad, Dessert, Creamy cheese

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob's Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. His vision was to create great tasting wines that reflect the true character of the grapes and the land. To this day we live and breathe this vision by producing wines that showcase how each grape variety expresses itself when shaped by the soil and the sun of Australia.

Awarded with 3 gold medals from international wine and spirits competition. Such as, 2014 WSA Wine Challenge, 2013 China Wine & Spirits Awards and 2014 Pacific Rim International Wine Compettition.

name: Charles Heidsieck  The Brut Reserve (750 ml)
wine_color: The Brut Réserve boasts a deep golden hue. Delicate, vivacious and long-lasting bubbles are the result of a long, slow maturing process in chalk cellars.
desc_en_short:
IT IS WITH BRUT RÉSERVE that the House has expresses the quintessence of its style. Particularly recognisable, this cuvée is the fruit of a unique blend that can be summarised with one equation: 60/40/10.

60: The number of crus gone into a blend that is precise, stringent and tailor-made. The wines produced that year, a third for each varietal (Meunier, Chardonnay and Pinot Noir), are vinified cru by cru, varietal by varietal, in stainless steel vats.

40: A high proportion of 40% reserve wines, equally divided between Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, to ensure the fine complexity and indulgence of the Brut Réserve.

10: The average age of the reserve wines, an exception in Champagne. Taken from the best crus from the best years, the wines placed in reserve are kept in vats for between 5 and 15 years, if not more, to develop finesse and their aromatic range.

wine_color: Bright yellow with light green hues.
desc_en_short:
Bright yellow with light green hues. This vibrant Sauvignon Blanc combines aromas of fresh herbs, green pepper and tomato leaves, with citric notes of grapefruit and lime. The palate is fresh and crisp, with a balanced natural acidity and a fruity finish.

Sustainable Winery

ISO 9001-HACCP
desc_en_full:
ORIGIN : Chile Curicó Valley Molina

GRAPE VARIETY : 100% Sauvignon Blanc proprietor's single vineyard grapes, from ungrafted selected clones of pre-phylloxera French rootstock.

HARVEST : February 23rd to 29th and March 7th to 11th, 2012, during the morning Hand-picked in 18 Kg. boxes and carried to the winery immediately after cutting

VINIFICATION :

Careful selection of the grapes before crushing.

Destemmed and chilled to 8ºC. Direct pneumatic pressing.

24 to 36 hours of static sedimentation at 8ºC prior to fermentation with selected yeasts.

Fermented in stainless steel tanks at 14-17ºC constant temperature for 25 days.

Stored in temperature controlled stainless steel tanks through bottling.

Serve between 10° and 12°C.

name: De Bortoli  Sacred Hill Semillon Sauvignon Blanc
desc_en_short:
*Minimum Qty Allowed in Shopping Cart 3 Bottles.

This Wine is Very pale straw. Ripe tropical flavours and a defining green apple backbone. Fresh early January rains prepared vines for what was to be a mild Riverina vintage anyway. The cooler temperatures allowed for great depth in fruit aromas especially in the Colombard and it also allowed for good natural acid in the Chardonnay.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING

Semillon & Sauvignon Blanc vineyards from the Riverina are winemaker select for their fresh fruit notes. The Semillon was pressed quickly and floated to produce very clean juice for a cool flavour-retaining ferment in stainless steel while the Sauvignon blanc was crushed and sat on skins for 3 hours before pressing hard to extract as much flavour as possible. Juice was cold settled for 3 days to allow the juice to again absorb as much flavour from the pulp as possible before racking. All juice is fermented cool to retain intense varietal characters. Wine was left on fine lees for a 4 month period before blending and bottling.

name: Camus  Cognac VSOP Borderies (700 ml)
desc_en_short: CAMUS Borderies VSOP presents a lively, mineral and floral character. The wine used for this Cognac came mostly from the Domaines CAMUS estates, and was exclusively distilled on the lees. The long ageing process in seasoned casks has sublimated the mineral character of this CAMUS Borderies VSOP into a harmonious, smooth and coherent array of aromas.
desc_en_full:
Camus VSOP Borderies

The Borderies cru is not just the smallest, but it is above all, the most sought-after of all the Cognac AOC growth areas.

Home to the CAMUS family and very close to its heart, the Borderies cru provides the vines with the perfect amount of water, thanks to the chalky soil and ideal exposure.

As a tribute to the CAMUS family's chosen land, the Borderies cru is expressed through magnificently balanced grapes, capturing the characteristic aroma of violets that denote eaux-de-vie from the area.

Tasting Notes

With its elegant, floral and mineral style, it is easy to identify the aromatic notes of CAMUS VSOP Borderies: creamy milk chocolate, orange zest and a touch of spice.

The finish is pure magic – reminiscent of fruity, plump fresh grapes.

name: Hendrick's Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: A superb, and utterly unique, pink-tinged gin from William Grant's distillery in Scotland. Handcrafted gin with traditional botanicals, juniper, coriander, and citrus peel but the special infusion of cucumber and rose petals creates a unique and unexpected flavour.
desc_en_full: First launched in 2001, Hendrick’s Gin has been the success story that has set the template for modern gin brands to follow. Hendrick’s Gin even goes one step further, as once the two spirits are blended, there is a further addition of cucumber and Bulgarian rose petal essence before the spirit is cut with water and finally bottled at 41.4% ABV. We’d recommend that if you don’t like gin or don’t know where to start, this is the place to do so as Hendrick’s is not a big juniper gin. That said, the juniper may be lighter, but it’s certainly there creating the base for a clean, floral and refreshing gin. Regardless of whether the cucumber flavour is distinct of the other twelve botanicals, the addition of it definitely adds a fresh quality to the ensemble helped along by the rose which can definitely be picked up on the aroma. Cubeb berries, elderflower, chamomile, and yarrow are some other botanicals that bring a more unusual offering to the mix, giving the floral edge some depth while also complimenting the more commonly used coriander, angelica root, lemon & orange peel, and orris root – who work to provide a solid backbone to the overall gin. Juniper is present all the way through, if framed by all the other botanicals so that it sits as part of a group as opposed to being the single botanical determinable at any given point.

name: De Kuyper  Triple Sec (700 ml)
desc_en_short: In bartending Triple Sec is a household name. The recipe dates back to the early 19th century and has been copied and altered ever since. We also took a shot at the infamous cocktail seasoning, pronouncing the citrus and prolonging the taste. By distilling the dried peels of sweet and bitter oranges, and mixing the two flavours meticulously together, we came up with this one of a kind liqueur.
desc_en_full:
COSMOPOLITAN

Ingredients: 25 ml De Kuyper Triple Sec liqueur 25 ml Lemon flavoured vodka 35 ml Cranberry juice 15 ml Freshly squeezed lime juice

Glass: martini Garnish: Flamed orange zest & a rose on a cocktail stick Shake all ingredients in the shaker & fine strain in a chilled glass
