name: Georges Duboeuf  Syrah Rose (VDF D'OC)
wine_color: Brilliant appearance with a lovely rose colour.
desc_en_short: Brilliant appearance with a lovely rose colour. Intense and fine white fruit aromas of peach livened up with notes of citrus fruits. Enticing in the mouth, tender, generous with an incredible freshness and a round finish.
desc_en_full:
History and terroir

In 1951, at the age of eighteen, Georges Duboeuf began delivering wine to local restaurants on his bicycle. Since then he has become one of the most important and influential wine producers in France. Often described as the King of Beaujolais, Duboeuf is also its most tireless ambassador, though his range now extends far beyond that region. Duboeuf still tastes hundreds of wines each day, and marshals his enormous production almost single-handed. While flower-label Beaujolais remains his empire’s most recognisable creation, one must remember he is a merchant as well as a winemaker: the company distributes numerous high-quality domaine wines not only from Beaujolais, but also white burgundies, Vins de Pays, and even a Côtes-du-Rhône.

Tasting Notes

Brilliant appearance with a lovely rose colour. Intense and fine white fruit aromas of peach livened up with notes of citrus fruits. Enticing in the mouth, tender, generous with an incredible freshness and a round finish.

name: Vigneti del Salento  I Muri` Negroamaro
desc_en_short: Ruby red with purple hues. This wine is intense with notes of black berries (blackcurrants and berries). It is medium bodied, velvety, with good balance. It has savoury notes on the long, fresh finish.
desc_en_full: This is the new solo venture in Puglia of the Farnese group (responsible for Gran Sasso, Zabu, Vesevo, Tufarello and others). The wines were made at two wineries in the province of Taranto on the western flank of Salento. The style of the wines - fresh whites, soft and generous reds - will be familiar to those who buy their other wines, as winemaker Filippo Baccalaro is the driving force behind this new label. Filippo, a native of Piemonte, has been working in Puglia for almost two decades. He was attracted by the rich, ripe flavours of the fruit, and the ability to capture these flavours with minimal use of technology and a lot of know-how. Filippo has the latter, as is illustrated by these wines. The star wines are, of course, those made from native grapes like Verdeca, Negroamaro and, of course, Primitivo.

name: Zonin  Chianti DOCG
wine_color: Bright ruby-red color of an attractive intensity.
desc_en_short: Bright ruby-red color of an attractive intensity. In its youth, it is richly marked by violet reflections that tend to become garnet with age. Characteristically intense,fine and elegant and distinguished by an elegant scent of violets and tenuous perfumes of wild berries. Dry, full, properly balanced and extremely enticing with lingering acidity and flavors of cherry and spice.
desc_en_full:
VINIFICATION AND MATURATION

The stems are separated from the grapes, which are softly pressed in cylinders under pressure gently breaking their skins and releasing the juice. The juice is left to macerate on the skins for about a week in temperature controlled stainless steel vinifiers at a tempe- rature of about 82-36°F. After the alcoholic fermentation is over, the wine undergoes also the malolactic fermentation, a process that lowers total acidity and makes wines seem rounder and fuller in flavor

name: Argento  Malbec
desc_en_short: Argento Malbec from Mendoza delivers a deep violet pour bursting with blackberry, plum, and black currant, layered with chocolate, sweet spice, and a whisper of vanilla from oak ageing — all wrapped up in a long, velvety finish.
desc_en_full:
Argento is one of Mendoza's most recognisable names, a producer whose identity is inseparable from the sun-drenched vineyards and high-altitude terroir that have made Argentina's flagship grape famous around the world. Rooted in the Mendoza appellation — home to some of the finest Malbec on the planet — Argento channels the fruit purity and natural vibrancy that the region's volcanic soils, intense sunshine, and cool Andean nights reliably deliver vintage after vintage.

In the glass, this Malbec opens with a striking deep violet, almost inky hue that signals density and concentration before a single sip is taken. The nose is generously expressive: ripe blackstone fruit, fresh blackberry, and black currant leap forward, underpinned by floral lift and a seductive thread of dark chocolate. Time in oak introduces gentle vanilla and sweet spice, adding complexity without overshadowing the wine's essential fruit character.

On the palate, the full body unfolds with plush, ripe tannins that feel genuinely velvety rather than grippy — a hallmark of well-handled Mendoza Malbec. The finish is long and satisfying, leaving impressions of dark fruit, cocoa, and a subtle earthy mineral note.

Serve with confidence alongside a well-seasoned Argentine asado, a grilled ribeye, or slow-roasted lamb shoulder. It also holds its own next to smoky barbecue ribs or a hard aged cheese board. A reliable, crowd-pleasing Malbec that over-delivers for its approachable style.

name: Catena Zapata  Alamos Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: From Catena Zapata's accessible Alamos label, this Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon delivers ripe red currant and cassis, layered with cedar, sweet spice, and a whisper of tobacco — finishing soft and structured with finely grained tannins. Rated 87 points by Wine Advocate.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Argentine wine as Catena Zapata, and the Alamos label represents the family's commitment to bringing high-altitude Mendoza quality within everyday reach. Sourced from vineyards in Mendoza — Argentina's premier red wine region, where the Andes cast long shadows and cool nights preserve freshness — this Cabernet Sauvignon exemplifies the generous, fruit-forward style that has made the region famous worldwide.

The wine opens with a striking dark purple colour and ruby red tones, immediately signalling its depth and concentration. On the nose, ripe red fruits lead the way, joined by sweet baking spice and an intriguing thread of tobacco that adds complexity beyond its approachable price point. The palate delivers red currant and cassis in abundance, with well-integrated oak contributing notes of cedar and espresso bean — classic hallmarks of Mendoza Cab aged with care. The finish is soft and satisfying, with finely grained tannins providing just enough structure and length to make this a genuinely rewarding glass.

Awarded 87 points by Wine Advocate, Alamos Cabernet Sauvignon is a go-to bottle for weeknight dinners and casual gatherings alike. Pour it alongside a grilled ribeye, asado-style barbecue, or a charcuterie board of cured meats — South American classics that mirror the wine's warm, spice-driven character perfectly.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Southern Hill Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: “With fruit sourced from our estate vineyards, our Southern Hills Cabernet Sauvignon is expressive of the Livermore Valley. The fruit comes from the valley floor and southern hills of Livermore Valley with its gravelly alluvial fans. Our soil and climate help to give us a Cabernet that is rich in flavor with an appealing structure.” - Karl D. Wente, Fifth Generation Winemaker
desc_en_full:
Vineyard

Since 1883, five generations of our family have handcrafted fine wines that express the very best of our vineyards and winemaking expertise. Our Estate Grown Southern Hills Cabernet Sauvignon is named for our vineyards located along the southern hills of the Livermore Valley. The Wente family farms approximately 400 acres of Cabernet Sauvignon vineyards using the two clones originating from California wine pioneer Charles Wetmore's Bordeaux cuttings in the 19th century. With Livermore Valley's unique East-West orientation, well-drained rocky soils, and unique mesoclimates, the Southern Hills vineyards are located in the most desirable settings with exposures ideal for growing Cabernet Sauvignon of distinct character, depth and intensity.

Winemaking

Fermentation :The various components were fermented separately in upright stainless steel tanks and gently pumped over twice daily. This process of aeration helps to increase the extraction of the aromas and textures within the grape.

Aging : This wine was aged for 16 months in French, Eastern European, and neutral oak barrels as well as stainless steel tanks. Aging in stainless steel helps preserve fruit vibrancy in the wine.

name: Antinori  Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso IGT
wine_color: An intense ruby red in color.
style: Principally Sangiovese, With Smaller Percentages Of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, And Syrah
desc_en_short: Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso is the entry-level red from the Antinori house — a smooth, fruit-forward Sangiovese blend that embodies the character of Tuscany in an accessible, everyday style with bright cherry, plum, and spice.
desc_en_full:
Villa Antinori Toscana Rosso is one of Antinori's most welcoming wines — an approachable, versatile Tuscan red that introduces the house style without demanding years of cellaring. Predominantly Sangiovese with a small addition of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, it is aged in both large and small oak to deliver a well-rounded, softly structured wine.

The colour is a vivid ruby-red. On the nose, fresh morello cherry, plum jam, and violets are lifted by subtle spice and a gentle hint of vanilla from the oak. The palate is smooth and round, with moderate tannins, good acidity, and a clean, fruit-driven finish that makes it effortlessly drinkable.

This is a reliable choice for the dinner table — excellent with pasta, pizza, grilled sausages, medium cheeses, and everyday Tuscan fare.

name: Torres  Mas La Plana
desc_en_short: It is more than three decades since this mysterious Spanish wine was born. The label: Black. The bottle: Burgundy style. The variety: Cabernet Sauvignon. Few had much faith in this project. Certainly, nobody back in 1979, could have dreamed that it would triumph in a Parisian blind tasting against some of the world's most famous wines. Yet it marked the start of a new era in wine making: a time in which to stop dreaming and embark on a new journey, year after year, harvest after harvest.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes :

Intense aroma showing all the classic characteristics of the native land of Mas La Plana, together with notes of truffle, toast and wild red berry conserve. Great body and breadth on the palate, with juicy tannins that become balanced and elegant.

Serving suggestions :

This wine's noble and healthy tannins will complement the best meat and game dishes. Serve at 17 - 18 ºC.

name: Masseria Altemura  Sasseo Primitivo
desc_en_short:
Sasseo Primitivo is rich, velvety, and fruit-forward, with flavors of black cherry, plum, blackberry, and hints of dark chocolate, vanilla, and exotic spice from oak aging.

This is a wine for those who enjoy power with finesse—perfectly suited for grilled meats, robust pastas, or sipping on its own in cool evening air.
desc_en_full:
Masseria Altemura is a project from the esteemed Zonin Family, focusing on showcasing the full potential of southern Italy's native varietals. Located in the Salento peninsula of Puglia, the estate benefits from intense sunlight, sea breezes from the Adriatic, and mineral-rich red soils.

Sasseo is made from 100% Primitivo grapes, hand-harvested from stony vineyards—the name derives from “Sasso,” meaning “stone,” a tribute to the rugged terroir that gives this wine its unique structure and depth.

name: Catena Zapata  Catena Malbec
desc_en_short: Crafted by fourth-generation vintner Laura Catena and winemaker Alejandro Vigil, this Mendoza Malbec draws from high-altitude estate vineyards to deliver a wine of remarkable concentration, natural balance, and distinctly Andean character — equally compelling young or after a decade in the cellar.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry more weight in Argentine wine than Catena Zapata, and the Catena Malbec stands as one of Mendoza's most eloquent expressions of what the high Andes can do with this adopted French variety. The wine is a collaboration between fourth-generation vintner Laura Catena and celebrated chief winemaker Alejandro Vigil, whose combined vision has helped place Mendoza on the world's fine-wine map.

The grapes are sourced from Catena Zapata's own High Mountain Estate Vineyards — plots selected through decades of rigorous terroir study at elevations where cool nights preserve natural acidity and slow, even ripening intensifies flavour without sacrificing freshness. The assemblage of these historic vineyards produces a wine greater than any single site: layered, harmonious, and unmistakably Andean in its personality.

In the glass, expect a deep, inky violet with aromas of ripe plum, dark cherry, and blackcurrant, threaded through with hints of violet, dark chocolate, and a subtle whisper of oak-derived spice. On the palate the wine is full-bodied yet polished, with medium-grained tannins and a long, savoury finish that speaks to genuine terroir rather than winemaking artifice.

Drink now alongside a grass-fed Argentine asado, a slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a wood-fired rib-eye, and the wine will reward immediately. Patience, however, is well rewarded — bottles cellared for ten to twenty years develop remarkable complexity and elegance. An outstanding everyday-luxury choice and a confident gift for any red-wine lover.

name: Catena Zapata  Nicasia Malbec
desc_en_short:
Nicasia Malbec is powerful yet polished, with bold dark fruit flavors like blackberry and blueberry, layered with violets, licorice, and cocoa, framed by elegant French oak. It offers an ideal expression of high-altitude terroir, showcasing balance between natural strength and graceful structure.

This is a benchmark Malbec—worthy of comparison to top wines from Bordeaux or Napa, yet rooted in the soul of the Andes.
desc_en_full:
Catena Zapata is widely recognized as Argentina’s leading fine wine producer. Dr. Nicolás Catena Zapata redefined South American winemaking by planting vineyards at high altitudes across Mendoza, pushing Malbec to new heights—both literally and figuratively.

The Nicasia Malbec comes from the Nicasia Vineyard in the Altamira district of Uco Valley, located over 900 meters above sea level. This single-vineyard site features stony soils and intense day-night temperature swings, resulting in grapes of exceptional intensity, purity, and fine tannins.

name: Masi  Amarone Riserva "Costasera" Classico DOC
desc_en_short:
Proud, majestic, complex and exuberant: this is a special cru version of Masi's gentle giant, Costasera.

A benchmark for the Amarone category, which, together with Barolo and Brunello, makes up the aristocracy of the Italian wine world. 

Here Masi's unrivalled expertise in the Appassimento technique is used to give the grapes a long period of further ripening on bamboo racks and an ageing period for the wine of at least three years in casks made from the finest wood types. 

The expert use of indigenous grapes for the Valpolicella Classica area - Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara - is enriched by the addition of the unique Oseleta, a grape variety rediscovered by Masi.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
desc_en_short: From one of Barolo's most respected estates, this Langhe Nebbiolo from Cordero di Montezemolo delivers the signature charm of Piedmont's noble grape — vibrant raspberry and violet aromas, fresh acidity, and supple tannins in an approachable, food-friendly style.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the heart of La Morra, one of the Barolo zone's most celebrated communes, Cordero di Montezemolo has been crafting wines from the Piedmontese hills for generations. Their Langhe Nebbiolo DOC offers a compelling introduction to the house's mastery of Nebbiolo — the great, temperamental grape behind both Barolo and Barbaresco — in a more accessible, earlier-drinking expression that still carries all the varietal character the appellation demands.

In the glass, the wine shows a bright violet of medium intensity, immediately signalling its youthful freshness. The nose is inviting and expressive: ripe raspberry and wild strawberry weave together with a lifted floral note of violet, a signature flourish of Nebbiolo grown in the Langhe hills. On the palate it is fresh and robust, with full flavour, balanced acidity, and tannins that are notably polished — approachable far earlier than the estate's flagship Barolo yet still possessing genuine structure.

This is quintessentially a food wine. At the table it excels alongside Piedmontese classics: agnolotti al ragù, saffron risotto, or ribbon pasta with a rich meat sauce. It holds its own against veal chops, braised lamb, roasted goat, or fowl with mushrooms, and makes an equally memorable pairing with rabbit and peppers — a dish straight from the Langhe farmhouse tradition. A reliable, satisfying choice for any occasion calling for an honest, characterful Italian red.

desc_en_short:
92 points in James Suckling "A young malbec that shows blackberry, flowers and minerals. Full body, fresh acidity and a bright, focused finish that's subtle and savory. Drink or hold."

89 points in Wine Enthusiast "Strapping currant and berry aromas come with a full dosage of toasty oak. This Malbec feels sinewy, with good depth. Oaky notes of toast and blackened spice lend a chocolaty essence to berry and plum flavors, while this finishes with an herbal, spicy aftertaste."

TASTING NOTES

name: Echeverria  Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Bright ruby red with purple hues.
desc_en_short: Echeverría Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon is a sustainably produced Chilean red delivering vivid aromas of dark berries, coffee, and tobacco on a round, full-bodied palate with high tannins and a long, satisfying finish.
desc_en_full:
The Echeverría family has been crafting wine in Chile's Curicó Valley since 1992, building a reputation for expressive, terroir-driven Cabernet Sauvignon that punches well above its price point. As a certified sustainable winery holding ISO 9001 and HACCP accreditation, Echeverría combines old-world winemaking discipline with the sun-drenched intensity that Central Valley Chile does so well.

The Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon pours a vivid ruby red shot through with deep purple hues — a visual promise of the concentration within. On the nose, ripe strawberries, blackberries, and dark cherries lead the charge, before warming notes of coffee and tobacco emerge from careful oak maturation. The palate is generous and full-bodied, with velvety tannins that give structure without austerity, and a finish that lingers with real purpose.

This is a bottle built for the table. Pull the cork alongside a slow-braised beef short rib, a rosemary-crusted leg of lamb, or a platter of cured meats and aged hard cheese. It's equally at home next to a steak and kidney pie or umami-rich game dishes. Adventurous pairers will find it holds its own against boldly flavoured Chinese braised pork or a blue cheese board. Certified vegan and vegetarian — no compromises required.

name: Echeverria  Founder's Selection
wine_color: Ruby red with deep amber gleams.
desc_en_short:
Ruby red with deep amber gleams. Cherries, plums, strawberries and blackberries combine nicely with eucalyptus and menthol aromas. A fulfilling and soft palate with black pepper and bitter chocolate flavours create a well balanced wine, owner of a distinctive personality.

Sustainable Winery

ISO 9001-HACCP
desc_en_full:
ORIGIN : Chile Maipo Valley

GRAPE VARIETY : 85% Cabernet Sauvignon - 10% Syrah - 5% Carménere Grapes from ungrafted selected clones of pre-phylloxera French rootstock.

HARVEST : Cabernet Sauvignon April 16, 2009. Syrah April 16, 2009. Carménère April 30, 2009. 100% Hand-picked in 18 Kg. boxes and carried to the winery immediately after cutting. Carefully selected before processing.

VINIFICATION :

Fermented in 12.000 liter stainless steel vats for 10 days at 25- 28ºC controlled temperature, with selected yeasts.

Extended skin maceration for additional 10 – 15 days.

100% malolactic fermentation.

Aged in 225 liter new French oak barrels for 18 months. The wine is blended and returns to barrels for 6 additional months.

Serve between 18° and 20°C.

name: Glaetzer  Amon-Ra
desc_en_short: Amon-Ra Shiraz is an iconic wine that epitomises Ben Glaetzer's passion to create wines that are full flavoured, multi layered and intense but still elegant, stylish and finely balanced. This wine is a balanced between the fruit and the tannin extraction. The fruit has such expression that we aim to preserve the caracteristics of the vineyard rather than dominate them. Deep, dark purple in colour. The palate is full bodied and multidimensional. Black fruits with hints of charcoal and graphite sit in equilibrium with balanced savoury tannin.
desc_en_full:
Glaetzer Amon-Ra

Deep garnet-purple colored, Amon-Ra Shiraz has a youthfully mute nose, offering glimpses at the blackberry, blackcurrant and licorice core, plus suggestions of dark chocolate, cigar boxes, fertile earth and baking spices. The full-bodied palate delivers concentrated, muscular blackberry and earth flavors with plenty of freshness and a beautiful backbone of finely grained tannins, finishing with commendable persistence and layers.

The Glaetzer History

The first Glaetzers settled in the Barossa Valley in 1888 after emigrating from Brandenburg, Germany. The family left Germany on board the Nord-Deutscher Lloyd steamer "Habsburg" and took seven weeks to reach their final destination of Port Adelaide. From here they settled in a country town called Nuriootpa in the Barossa Valley where they started their new life in Australia. The family were some of the earliest recorded viticulturalists in the Barossa Valley and Clare Valley.

Over a century later, winemaking patriarch Colin Glaetzer established Glaetzer Wines, a boutique family owned winery producing premium Barossa Valley wines. Today Colin's son Ben carries on the wine making traditions at Glaetzer Wines. Indeed it was the birth of Glaetzer Wines that signalled a new era for Colin's family which boasts more than its fair share of winemakers.

name: Torbreck  Shiraz Woodcutter's
desc_en_short: This wine reflects the up & coming Shiraz vineyards of the Barossa, rather than the battle hardened old vines which make up the core of our other cuvee’s. Full of dense, savoury fruit, rich and complex wine. A dark cherry, central core of fruit gives way to an intense textural mid palate full of blueberry, cocoa, spice and dark chocolate.
desc_en_full:
Torbreck Shiraz Woodcutter's

The name Woodcutter's stems from when Torbreck founder David Powell spent several years working in the Scottish Highlands as a lumberjack or woodcutter in the Torbreck forest. Like all Torbreck wines it's sourced from hand harvested and hand tended, low yielding vines. It is then open fermented and gently basket pressed, and aged one fine lees for 12 months in large format seasoned barrels and foudres.

Although this wine is constantly praised for being succulent and rich - perhaps the best feature of this wine is it's complexity and texture which are rarely found at this price.

name: Torbreck  The Steading
wine_color: Deep ruby with flickers of violet
desc_en_short: Deep ruby with flickers of violet, its delicate aromas of truffle, five spice and spring flowers are supported by a rich core of licorice, saddle leather and Provencal herbs. Very ‘Burgundian’ in style, the palate is elegant and pure with subtle notes of crushed cherries, earth and cedar all neatly wound by a taut spine of acidity and ripe supple tannins.
desc_en_full:
Torbreck The Steading

The Steading is perhaps the most important wine within the Torbreck portfolio, the one that best exemplifies what Torbreck is all about. It is sourced from vines that survived the vine-pull scheme in the early 1980s, cultivated on their own roots that were unaffected by phylloxera. It also best explains the serendipitous discovery of some withered ancient vines, some well over a century old, that have been carefully nurtured back to life. Protecting and nurturing the old vine resources of the Barossa Valley is paramount to Torbreck and this wine, more than any other is an indication of what is possible from the now protected ancient Barossa cultivars - Grenache, Mataro and Shiraz.

The Steading is sourced from our own vineyard estates as well as from growers on a share-farming basis, totalling 45 different sources of fruit, all vinified separately and blended once their individual virtues have been assessed. The Steading's generous and supple flavours combined with its silken texture are a rewarding and very versatile choice with all types of cuisine.

name: Torbreck  The Factor
desc_en_short: 98 points in James Suckling. " Dark fruit with iodine, muscle shell and hints of coal on the palate. Full-bodied, very tight and structured with steely tannins that give incredible length and poise. The greatest Factor I have ever tasted. Driven and brilliant. Better after 2021 "
desc_en_full:
Torbreck The Factor

Our homage to the great old Shiraz vines of the Barossa Valley and the growers who painstakingly tend and nurture them each year. The Factor displays the concentration & fruit purity that only the most meticulous farming can achieve. Delicate scents of wild blackberries, pipe smoke and pepper are supported by a dark core of espresso roast, black currants and coal. The palate offers tremendous texture and combines great richness with subtle notes of olive tapenade, saddle leather and minerals.

Brooding and densely packed, this majestic wine has the constitution to cellar for many years where it will slowly unravel its extravagant riches. The reputation of the Barossa as the pre-eminent red wine growing region of Australia rests firmly on wines such as this.

name: Mcguigan  Private Bin Shiraz
desc_en_short: McGuigan Private Bin Shiraz delivers classic Australian warmth — dark cherry and plum fruit layered with smoky vanillin and toasty oak, finishing with a satisfying grip of firm tannins that makes it an ideal companion to bold, hearty dishes.
desc_en_full:
McGuigan Wines has been crafting approachable, flavour-forward Australian wines for decades, and the Private Bin Shiraz stands as one of the range's most reliably enjoyable expressions. Sourced from sun-ripened Shiraz vineyards across South Eastern Australia, this is a bottle that delivers the warm, generous character the country does so well — bold fruit, generous oak, and structure to spare.

In the glass, expect a deep, inky ruby that signals the richness within. The nose opens with dark cherry and ripe plum, entwined with inviting notes of smoky vanillin and toasty American oak — a hallmark signature of the classic Aussie Shiraz style. On the palate, those same plum and cherry flavours roll through in full-bodied fashion, plush and smooth, before the oak asserts itself with a toasty, slightly spiced warmth. A firm tannin grip provides the backbone, giving the wine shape and a clean, persistent finish that calls for food.

This is an ideal pour for a weekend dinner centred on grilled ribeye or lamb chops, where the wine's tannin structure is softened by rich, fatty proteins. It also shines alongside a hearty tomato-based pasta — think slow-cooked beef ragù — where the wine's fruit and oak complement the sauce's acidity and depth. A touch of spice in the dish only adds to the harmony.

Reliable, expressive, and genuinely crowd-pleasing, McGuigan Private Bin Shiraz is a bottle worth keeping stocked for any occasion that demands a bold red.

name: Luccarelli  Campo Marina Primitivo di Manduria DOP
desc_en_short: Luccarelli Campo Marina delivers the sun-drenched soul of Primitivo di Manduria in a bold, fleshy Italian red bursting with black plum, ripe purple fruit, and a generous, warming finish — tailor-made for grilled meats and open-fire feasts.
desc_en_full:
Primitivo di Manduria is one of southern Italy's most characterful appellations, carved out of the sun-scorched heel of Puglia in the Taranto province. Here, ancient Primitivo vines — genetically identical to California's Zinfandel — push deep roots into the red clay-limestone soils, producing grapes of remarkable concentration and natural sweetness that have earned the zone its own DOP status.

Luccarelli's Campo Marina is a textbook expression of what Puglia does best. The name evokes the coastal fields of Manduria, and the wine delivers that warm, Mediterranean generosity in every glass. Vinification honours the grape's extroverted character: careful temperature-controlled fermentation preserves the vivid aromatics, while time on the skins extracts the deep colour and firm tannic structure that define the variety.

In the glass, expect an inky ruby-purple hue and an immediately inviting nose of fleshy black plum, ripe damson, and sun-warmed blackberry, layered with hints of dried fig, dark chocolate, and a whisper of vanilla spice. The palate is full-bodied and lush, with velvety fruit weight balanced by a grippy tannic backbone and a low-acid, warming finish that lingers satisfyingly.

Campo Marina is made for the table and the fire. Pour it alongside chargrilled ribeye, smoky BBQ ribs, slow-braised lamb ragù over rigatoni, or a platter of aged Pecorino. It's equally at home around a campfire or anchoring a weekend feast — a crowd-pleasing, deeply satisfying red from one of Italy's most exciting wine regions.

name: Vesevo  Aglianico del Beneventano IGT
desc_en_short: Vesevo's Aglianico del Beneventano IGT is a southern Italian red from Campania's volcanic heartland, delivering vivid red fruit, warming spice, and a silky-smooth finish that belies Aglianico's typically austere character.
desc_en_full:
Vesevo is a respected estate rooted in the volcanic soils of Campania, drawing on the ancient Aglianico grape — one of southern Italy's most noble and historically significant varieties. The designation Aglianico del Beneventano IGT places this wine in the province of Benevento, an inland stretch of Campania where the combination of volcanic mineral-rich soils, warm summers, and cooling elevations coaxes remarkable depth and complexity from the grape.

In the glass, this wine pours a deep ruby red threaded with captivating violet hues — a hallmark of young Aglianico. The bouquet is immediately expressive: ripe red cherries and wild berries lead the way, wrapped in enticing layers of black pepper, warm spice, and a whisper of vanilla that hints at careful oak maturation. Floral notes add lift and elegance to what is an impressively complex nose for an IGT-level bottling.

On the palate, Vesevo shows a gentler side of Aglianico than the variety's reputation might suggest — the tannins are polished and approachable, the fruit is generous and juicy, and the finish is clean, dry, and satisfying. This is a wine that bridges everyday drinking pleasure with genuine varietal character.

Pour this alongside a slow-braised lamb ragù over rigatoni, wood-fired pizza with cured meats, or a board of aged Pecorino and cured salumi. It also shines as a table companion to grilled pork sausages or hearty vegetable stews.

name: Casanova di Neri  Brunello di Montalcino Tenuta Nuova DOCG 2017
wine_color: Deep garnet-ruby, reflecting concentration and maturity.
desc_en_short:
Tenuta Nuova 2017 is a Brunello of both power and grace—crafted from 100% Sangiovese carefully selected from the estate's finest plots.

This wine displays a well-defined character: robust yet refined, intensely flavored yet harmonious. Its structure is firm without harshness, offering remarkable aging potential—easily 20+ years. Ideal for connoisseurs who seek world-class Brunello with both strength and precision.
desc_en_full:
Casanova di Neri is one of the most acclaimed producers of Brunello di Montalcino. Founded in 1971 by Giovanni Neri and now led by Giacomo Neri, the estate is known for merging modern winemaking techniques with the deep traditions of the region.

The label Tenuta Nuova, meaning “New Estate,” was born from Giacomo Neri’s bold decision to plant Sangiovese in a previously unexplored southeastern part of Montalcino with distinct terroir. The result is a Brunello of strong character, structural depth, and aromatic complexity—made to evolve beautifully over time.

name: Little Beauty  Marlborough 'Limited Edition' Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: A seductively aromatic Marlborough Pinot Noir from Little Beauty, layering raspberry, dark cherry, and violet perfume over a silky, medium-ruby palate with gentle smoky oak and soft, ripe tannins — an elegant expression of New Zealand's cool-climate finesse.
desc_en_full:
Little Beauty has carved out a well-deserved reputation in Marlborough for coaxing genuine character from New Zealand's most expressive cool-climate varieties, and this Limited Edition Pinot Noir stands as one of their most compelling releases. Sourced from Marlborough's sun-drenched yet cool vine rows, the wine captures everything that makes this South Island region increasingly compelling for Pinot Noir — bright natural acidity, crystalline fruit purity, and a delicate structural elegance that sets it apart from warmer-world expressions.

The nose is immediately captivating: intensely aromatic waves of fresh raspberry, black cherry, and dark plum are lifted by a floral breath of violet and layered with savoury depth — compote-like red fruit richness, a whisper of coffee mocha, and the subtle complexity that comes from careful barrel fermentation. It is the kind of bouquet that rewards patience in the glass.

On the palate, the wine delivers beautifully. Silky, near-weightless tannins carry flavours of blueberry and ripe blackberry alongside that signature smoky, barrel-ferment character, all underpinned by the variety's hallmark lively acidity. The finish is clean, refined, and lingering.

This is a superb choice at the dinner table alongside roasted lamb rack with herb crust, a seared beef tenderloin, or a slow-braised short rib. Its lighter body and high acidity also make it a natural companion to duck confit or a charcuterie board. Serve lightly chilled at around 14–16 °C to let the aromatics truly shine.

name: Jacob's Creek  Double Barrel Shiraz (750 ml)
wine_color: Deep purple with a hint of ruby
desc_en_short:
Double Barrel is finished in aged whisky barrels for a richer, deeper, and smoother taste. Double Barrel Shiraz has a full and generous palate with a beguiling mix of sweet red fruits and dark chocolate indicative of its origins as Barossa Shiraz. Subtle toasty vanilla drives from aging in traditional French and American oak wine barrels. 

Wine Score : 93 POINTS, Huon Hooke - The Real Review (Feb-18)

Award : SILVER, Sakura Awards Japan, 2018
desc_en_full:
Double Barrel is finished in aged whisky barrels for a richer, deeper, and smoother taste. Double Barrel Shiraz has a full and generous palate with a beguiling mix of sweet red fruits and dark chocolate indicative of its origins as Barossa Shiraz. Subtle toasty vanilla drives from aging in traditional French and American oak wine barrels. The careful use of aged Scotch whisky barrels for finishing has added complex nuances of almond-husks and also softened the dense tannins to yield an incredible smoothness.

Aroma

Plum, Licorice, Vanilla, Smoke

Taste

Dry with medium to high tannin, full body with long finish

Complimentary

Grilled Beef, Lamb, Hard Cheese

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob’s Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. His vision was to create great tasting wines that reflect the true character of the grapes and the land. To this day we live and breathe this vision by producing wines that showcase how each grape variety expresses itself when shaped by the soil and the sun of Australia.

Awarded with 3 gold medals from international wine and spirits competition. Such as, 2014 WSA Wine Challenge, 2013 China Wine & Spirits Awards and 2014 Pacific Rim International Wine Compettition.

name: George Wyndham  Bin 999 Merlot (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Rooted in Australia's winemaking heritage since 1830, George Wyndham Bin 999 Merlot is a generous, fruit-forward red blended from premium viticultural regions — soft in tannin, rich in plum and dark berry character, and effortlessly easy to enjoy.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Australian wine history as George Wyndham. When Wyndham planted his first commercial vineyard in the Hunter Valley in 1830, he helped lay the foundations of an entire industry. That pioneering spirit lives on in the Bin range — a celebrated lineup of multiregional blends drawing fruit from some of Australia's most respected viticultural regions, crafted to deliver the bold, full-flavoured generosity that has defined the Wyndham name for nearly two centuries.

Bin 999 is the Merlot expression of this legacy, and it delivers everything the variety does best. Expect a plush, approachable palate built around ripe plum, dark cherry, and a hint of chocolate, supported by subtle notes of spice and soft earthiness. The tannins are gentle and supple — a hallmark of well-handled Merlot — making this an immediately pleasurable pour without the need for extended cellaring.

The wine earned a Silver Medal at the Sydney Royal Wine Show 2018, a mark of quality that reflects the consistency and care behind the Wyndham winemaking programme.

Bin 999 is a natural match for slow-braised beef, pork ribs glazed with smoky sauce, or a hearty Bolognese. It also holds its own alongside aged cheddar or a simple charcuterie spread. Whether it's a midweek dinner or a relaxed weekend gathering, this is a bottle that reliably over-delivers for its place in the market.

name: Penfolds  Bin 28 Shiraz
desc_en_short: Bin 28 offers a showcase of warm climate Australian Shiraz – ripe, robust and generously flavoured. First made in 1959, Bin 28 was originally named after the famous Barossa Valley Kalimna vineyard purchased by Penfolds in 1945 and from which the wine was originally sourced. Today, Bin 28 is a multi-region, multi-vineyard blend, with the Barossa Valley always well represented.
desc_en_full:
Taste Description

Colour

Purple with a black core.

Nose

The aromatic spectrum presents an array of scents that point to varietal origin. The nose is distinguished by a subtle formic character, closely followed by savoury notes of blood pudding, aniseed/fennel and black liquorice. A hint of milk chocolate adds a soft, sweet touch to the predominantly dark fruit profile, where red, blue, and black fruits are all present. Ground cinnamon, toasted fennel seeds, clove, mace and sumac contribute to an overall impression of liveliness.

Palate

The palate texture is suggestive of cocoa powder while being framed by just enough savoury oak to add structure without dominating. Leading with black fruit characters, it progresses with dark plum and hints of gamey richness (roasted venison). Baking spice notes and a delicate icing sugar dusting add nuance without tipping into overt sweetness. Lively acidity carries through from start to finish, perfectly accompanied by fine structural tannins that contribute to an enduring balance. Despite its depth and layers, it carries itself with typical Bin 28 ease; its complexity is apparent yet approachable.

name: Penfolds  Bin 407
desc_en_short: Vibrant crimson red color. The aromas soar brazenly, immediately cabernet – tomato relish/chutney, cassis. Other fruits edge forward for shelf space, like yellow-fleshed plum (El Dorado?) and ripe persimmon. Intriguingly, other less assertive aromatics offer more complexity: sage soaked in butter/oil (pre-saltimbocca preparation); olive leaf/turmeric spices, and caper brine. Reticent oak (cedar) noted – manifested as a seasoned/maturation character. On the palate, the wine is medium-bodied and elegantly framed. No mid-palate deficiencies! Blackcurrant and plum fruits sit alongside mocha/chocolate/walnut flavors. Oak? A genteel (washed, older oak) character – affording a maturation, not a flavor imprint. Mouth-watering with fine, rounded/polished tannins. In sharp palate contrast – a finessed, varietally-driven alternative to the muscular and Grange-like Bin 707 Cabernet.
desc_en_full:
Launched with the 1990 vintage in 1993, Penfolds Bin 407 was developed in response to the increasing availability of high quality Cabernet Sauvignon fruit. Inspired by Penfolds Bin 707, Bin 407 offers varietal definition and approachability, yet with structure and depth of flavour.

A textbook Cabernet Sauvignon, the varietally expressive Bin 407 highlights the rewards of Penfolds multi-region, multi-vineyard blending, with a core of ripe fruit supported by a sensitive use of French and American oak.

name: Penfolds  Bin 707 Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: A purple rim and dense black core.
subregion: Coonawarra / McLaren Vale / Barossa Valley / Adelaide Hills
desc_en_short: Penfolds Bin 707 Cabernet Sauvignon is Australia's benchmark Cabernet — a multi-region masterpiece drawing fruit from Coonawarra, McLaren Vale, and the Barossa Valley, aged in new American oak to deliver extraordinary depth, velvety richness, and two decades of cellaring potential.
desc_en_full:
Few wines carry the weight of history and expectation that Penfolds Bin 707 does. First produced in 1964 and conceived as the Cabernet Sauvignon counterpart to the legendary Grange, Bin 707 is built on the same founding philosophy: uncompromising fruit selection across multiple South Australian regions, completed fermentation in new American oak, and a winemaking vision that transcends any single vineyard or vintage. So seriously does Penfolds guard its standards that Bin 707 was simply not made in years — including 1981, 1995, 2003, and 2011 — when fruit of the required quality was unavailable.

The wine draws on estate and grower fruit from Coonawarra, McLaren Vale, Barossa Valley, and Adelaide Hills, each appellation contributing structural backbone, dark-fruited generosity, or aromatic complexity in turn. The result is a Cabernet of rare completeness: a dense, ink-dark core rimmed with purple, offering a cascade of blueberry, mulberry, blackberry, and crème de cassis on the nose, lifted by violet, wild flowers, and a whisper of anise. With air, candied fig, roasted beetroot, and warming spice emerge from beneath a fine dusting of oak.

On the palate, Bin 707 delivers glycerolic density with squid-ink concentration — texturally lush and seamless, with no hard edges. Ripe, overt tannins and new oak provide structure, yet the finish refuses to be curtailed, lingering with remarkable persistence. Rated 98 points by Wine Advocate, this is a wine built equally for tonight's celebratory table and the cellar shelf through 2040 and beyond. Serve alongside confit lamb breast, slow-roasted beef, or aged hard cheese.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2012 (Premiers Crus)
wine_color: A dense and opaque, almost black colour with a very attractive glint.
desc_en_short: This is a beautifully opulent wine, great Mouton in its richness and succulent fruits. It’s combines structure and obvious new-wood aging with hugely ripe black plum and currant flavors. While it is a pleasure to taste now, there is a great tannic structure in the background to give the sense of power and aging potential.
desc_en_full:
Château Mouton Rothschild 2012

2012 was a year of contrasts. The winter was cold and very dry. The average temperature in February was the lowest for fifty years.

April brought cool weather and abundant rainfall, while the weather in May and June was in-between. The flowering and veraison dates for Château Mouton Rothschild Cabernet Sauvignon were the same to the day as the average dates for all the vintages since 1962.

Exceptionally dry conditions prevailed from 20 July to 20 September, concentrating sugar and tannins in the grapes. The month of August was the fifth driest in the last fifty years. By the end of September, the aggregate amount of rainfall was 26% lower than the average since 1962 (435 mm compared with 589 mm).

Picking at Château Mouton Rothschild began on 1 October and ended on 15 October. 2012 will be a landmark year, since after almost three years of work the new vat room welcomed its first harvest.

Rich, deeply coloured, well-structured and very full-bodied, the 2012 vintage promises to be a great success.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Casillero del Diablo Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Concha y Toro delivers ripe blackberry and dark fruit on a medium-bodied frame with silky, polished tannins — a reliably satisfying Chilean Cab with legendary heritage and everyday versatility.
desc_en_full:
The legend of Casillero del Diablo begins in the cellar. Don Melchor Concha y Toro, founder of Chile's most iconic wine house, reportedly spread rumours that the devil himself guarded his finest barrels — a story that kept thieves away and gave this beloved label its unforgettable name. Today, Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon carries that heritage into every bottle, sourcing Cabernet Sauvignon fruit from Chile's Central Valley, where the Andes and Pacific Ocean create a climate tailor-made for expressive, fruit-driven reds.

In the glass, expect deep ruby hues and an inviting nose of ripe blackberry, dark cherry, and a hint of cassis, with subtle cedar and dried herb notes adding complexity. The palate is medium-bodied with a satisfying concentration of dark fruit, carried by smooth, silky tannins that speak to careful vineyard selection and measured oak maturation. The finish is long and clean, with a lingering berry warmth that keeps things elegant rather than heavy.

This is a wine built for the table. It shines alongside a chargrilled ribeye or slow-braised short ribs, and handles bold flavours with ease — think smoky barbecue, spiced lamb, or peppery charcuterie. Cheese lovers will find it pairs beautifully with aged Gruyère or a creamy blue. For an everyday bottle that consistently over-delivers for its price, Casillero del Diablo Reserva remains one of Chile's most trusted names on the global stage.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2015
wine_color: An intense garnet red, the wine has a bright and glittering aspect.
desc_en_short:
Climatic conditions

In terms of the weather, although 2015 was slightly warmer than average, it was above all very dry (706 mm of rain, compared with an annual average of 862 mm), especially between February and July, from budbreak to veraison. As a result the berries remained small, which explains why the yield was rather low even though we had hopes of a normal crop after flowering.

Rainfall in August and September was higher than average, which helped to prevent the ripening process from coming to a halt in a vineyard already stressed by the lack of water. As a result, the grapes matured at rather different rates from one plot to another, according to the variety, the age of the vines and the terroir.

In order to ensure that each grape variety and each plot was picked at optimum maturity, the harvest period was the longest in living memory. It lasted 23 days between our three estates, from the first Merlot clusters on 14 September to the last Cabernet Sauvignon grapes on 6 October.

The grapes were magnificent and fermentation took place quickly. The wines immediately showed plenty of colour and a complete array of very intense aromas ranging from red and black fruit to spice and incense.

The tannins are generally very well-rounded, dense and seamlessly smooth. After blending, the wines still have the same aromatic intensity and complex, full-bodied structures which barrel-maturing should further enhance.

name: Damilano  Barolo "Liste" DOCG
wine_color: garnet ruby red with fleeting orange reflections
desc_en_short: At the eye, Liste appears in its intense ruby red color. At the nose, it expresses notes of wild berries, liquorish, tobacco, and cocoa. At the palate, it is dry, full-bodied, has a persistent flavor, and is austere and velvety.
desc_en_full:
Damilano is one of the oldest wineries in Barolo. The family business dates back to 1890 when Giuseppe Borgogno started cultivating and producing wine from the grapes from his own vineyards. Giuseppe improved the general quality of the vinification system and of the wines, turning the winery into a "jewel" of the Langhe area. In 1997 Giuseppe passed the winery on to his grandchildren Paolo, Mario and Guido. Together, they are working to keep and improve the level of the quality of the Damilano wines.

Tasting Notes

At the eye, Liste appears in its intense ruby red color. At the nose, it expresses notes of wild berries, liquorish, tobacco, and cocoa. At the palate, it is dry, full-bodied, has a persistent flavor, and is austere and velvety.

name: Damilano  Barolo "Cannubi" DOCG
wine_color: Garnet ruby red with orange reflections
desc_en_short: Damilano is one of the oldest wineries in Barolo. The family business dates back to 1890 when Giuseppe Borgogno started cultivating and producing wine from the grapes from his own vineyards. Giuseppe  improved the general quality of the vinification system and of the wines, turning the winery into a “jewel” of the Langhe area. In 1997 Giuseppe passed the winery on to his grandchildren Paolo, Mario and Guido. Together, they are working to keep and improve the level of the quality of the Damilano wines.
desc_en_full:
About Wine

Cannubi is considered the Grand Cru vineyard for the Barolo, an extraordinary wine in terms of style and elegance.

The absolute high quality of the grapes picked on the Cannubi hill is the result of a unique land composition: an unevolved soil with clay-rich marl, grey and white on the surface. The soil in which the Damilano vines are found features a high percentage of sand (up to 45%) that gives the grapes intense aromas of cherry, plum and tobacco, rose and violet in sequence. Its low potassium and high calcium/magnesium content offer the wine a fine and polished touch. Its color is intense and bright, and it has a high polyphenol concentration.

At first sight, what is most striking is its ruby red color with hints of maroon and orange reflections, and an ample, enveloping scent, with clear fruit notes of cherry and plum, and herbal notes of tobacco, liquorish, and cocoa.

The wine embraces the palate with a harmonic, pleasantly dry flavor, with soft tannins and a perceivable body and completeness.

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci  Da Vinci I Capolavori Chianti Riserva DOCG
desc_en_short: The landscape in this Leonardian masterpiece is typically Tuscan and nuanced, just like the fruit of this famous Reserve wine. Ripe grapes are left on the skins for about 10-12 days. Fermentation takes place at controlled temperatures of 27-28°C. Pumping-over is performed frequently for a short while, and the saignée or "bleeding off" technique is used to increase the richness of noble polyphenols. Once removed from the skins, wine is kept in steel containers to complete malolactic fermentation. Then wine is aged in French and American oak barrels for 10 months.

wine_color: A cherry red colour with plum-coloured tints
desc_en_short: A cherry red colour with plum-coloured tints, this wine has a harmonious nose that reveals kirsch and red berry aromas. Full-bodied and aromatic, with fine, fruity tannins. A pleasing wine that won’t disappoint, even after it has just been opened. Young, well-balanced and made with very typical grapes. It will gain in length and smoothness as it ages.
desc_en_full:
History and terroir

Since Antiquity, Merlot has been grown in the Gironde and Languedoc Roussillon, where it has become increasingly popular. In fact, it is here that Merlot finds the conditions most favourable to higher quality. The surface area planted in the French vineyards is now in excess of 100,000 hectares, approximately 30,000 of which is in Languedoc Roussillon, from where this Merlot Vin de Pays d'Oc comes.

Tasting Notes

A cherry red colour with plum-coloured tints, this wine has a harmonious nose that reveals kirsch and red berry aromas. Full-bodied and aromatic, with fine, fruity tannins. A pleasing wine that won’t disappoint, even after it has just been opened. Young, well-balanced and made with very typical grapes. It will gain in length and smoothness as it ages.

name: Feudi di San Gregorio  Primitivo di Manduria DOC
desc_en_short: A richly structured Primitivo di Manduria DOC from Feudi di San Gregorio, delivering wild cherry, prune, and warming spice alongside a velvety finish of cocoa and coffee — a deeply satisfying expression of Puglia's sun-drenched southern terroir.
desc_en_full:
Feudi di San Gregorio is one of southern Italy's most celebrated estates, originally rooted in Campania's ancient Irpinia hills but reaching across the Mezzogiorno to champion indigenous varieties. This Primitivo di Manduria DOC hails from Puglia's Taranto province, where the Primitivo grape — a close relative of California's Zinfandel — thrives in the deep, iron-rich soils baked by Mediterranean sun, producing wines of remarkable concentration and warmth.

The nose opens with generous aromas of wild cherry and sun-dried prune, building depth with notes of clove, black pepper, and subtle woody undertones from careful oak maturation. On the palate, the wine is full-bodied yet beautifully balanced, with Primitivo's characteristic ripeness held in check by medium acidity and supple tannins that carry the wine to a long, satisfying finish of cocoa and espresso coffee.

This is a wine built for the table. Serve it alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder seasoned with rosemary and garlic, a Puglian ragù tossed through handmade orecchiette, or a char-grilled ribeye steak. Its spiced complexity also makes it a natural match for aged pecorino or a hearty beef stew on cooler evenings. Decant for 30 minutes to allow its generous fruit and structure to fully open up.

name: Colina  Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Deep ruby (รูบี้เข้ม)
desc_en_short: Colina Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Central Valley delivers a structured, fruit-forward red with dark berry intensity, subtle spice, and firm tannins — a satisfying everyday bottle with real character for the price.
desc_en_full:
Colina is a Chilean wine brand focused on expressing the honest, sun-drenched character of the Central Valley — one of Chile's most productive and approachable wine regions, where warm days and cool nights conspire to ripen Cabernet Sauvignon to generous, consistent flavour. Sheltered by the Andes to the east and the Coastal Range to the west, the valley provides ideal conditions for cultivating this internationally beloved variety with both depth and everyday drinkability.

In the glass, Colina Cabernet Sauvignon pours a deep ruby with violet edges. The nose opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum, layered with hints of black pepper and a whisper of dried herbs. Time in oak adds a gentle vanilla note and rounds out the fruit without overpowering it. On the palate, the wine is medium-bodied with firm but approachable tannins and a steady core of dark fruit that carries through to a clean, moderately long finish. It's structured enough to feel serious, yet balanced enough to pour freely mid-week.

This is a reliable, crowd-pleasing Cab that over-delivers for its accessible price point. Serve it slightly below room temperature alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a generous beef stew. Ideal for casual dinner parties, weekend barbecues, or any occasion calling for a dependable red that won't require much deliberation — just pour and enjoy.

name: Louis Jadot  Coteaux Bourguignons Rouge
desc_en_short: Louis Jadot's Coteaux Bourguignons is an approachable, elegant entry into the world of Burgundy Pinot Noir — light-bodied and silky with fresh red cherry, raspberry, violet, and subtle earthy depth, showing the gentle finesse that makes Burgundy so distinctive.
desc_en_full:
Louis Jadot is one of Burgundy's most celebrated and trusted négociants, producing wines across every appellation in the Côte d'Or and beyond with consistent quality and true Burgundian character. The Coteaux Bourguignons Rouge is their most accessible red, made from 100% Pinot Noir sourced from vineyards across the broader Burgundy region.

Unfined and unfiltered for maximum character, the wine is fermented in temperature-controlled tanks and aged in oak to add gentle complexity without overwhelming the delicate Pinot Noir fruit. In the glass it is a translucent ruby-red. The nose is immediately Burgundian — fresh morello cherry, raspberry, violets, and a subtle earthy, almost forest-floor quality that is the hallmark of the region. On the palate it is light-bodied and silky, with lively acidity, soft tannins, and a clean, fruity finish.

Ideal lightly chilled as an everyday Burgundy: serve with roast chicken, duck, grilled salmon, mushroom dishes, charcuterie, or mild soft cheeses.

name: Chateau Pericou  AOP Bordeaux Rouge
desc_en_short: Intense garnet red in color, it shows hints of plum, blackberry and cassis on the nose, with pleasant hints of rose. In the mouth it is soft and mature, complex and evolved. Excellent to combine with all meat recipes, both white and red.
desc_en_full:
Château Pericou takes its name from the homonymous estate located in the most peripheral part of the Côtes de Blaye, in Bordeaux. A terroir that boasts gravelly, clayey and calcareous soils that provide excellent drainage and promote the concentration of substances in the grapes.

It is produced for the most part with Merlot grapes joined by Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, all from vineyards with at least 30 years of age. A traditional vinification allows to highlight the enchanting depth of the fruit and the tannins that support it.

name: Vigneti del Salento  Zolla Primitivo Di Manduria Riserva
wine_color: Ruby red colour with violet reflections.
desc_en_short: Ruby red colour with violet reflections. Offers a complex bouquet with notes of cherry, blackberries, black currant combined with scent of spice and vanilla, coffee and toffee. A red with great body, velvet tannins and long persistence.
desc_en_full: This is the new solo venture in Puglia of the Farnese group (responsible for Gran Sasso, Zabu, Vesevo, Tufarello and others). The wines were made at two wineries in the province of Taranto on the western flank of Salento. The style of the wines - fresh whites, soft and generous reds - will be familiar to those who buy their other wines, as winemaker Filippo Baccalaro is the driving force behind this new label. Filippo, a native of Piemonte, has been working in Puglia for almost two decades. He was attracted by the rich, ripe flavours of the fruit, and the ability to capture these flavours with minimal use of technology and a lot of know-how. Filippo has the latter, as is illustrated by these wines. The star wines are, of course, those made from native grapes like Verdeca, Negroamaro and, of course, Primitivo.

wine_color: Brilliant, with a rich and persistent foam and fine perlage Pale straw yellow
desc_en_short: Brilliant, with a rich and persistent foam and fine perlage Pale straw yellow. Fruity, flowery, with scents of acacia flowers, apple, white peach and citrus fruits on aromas. The palate is fresh, delicate, fragrant and well-balanced. Fabulous as an aperitif and in cocktails as well as with starters, first courses, fish and poultry.
desc_en_full: "Vino dei Poeti" is a prestigious name that evokes the way poets, artists and art lovers raise their glasses to toast the joy of being alive and drink their beloved light, fragrant Italian sparkling wine. The grapes are generally hand picked from mid-September in the vineyards cultivated with the sylvoz growing system, which is typical of this zone. The grapes are pressed and then vinified in white, where the must is separated from the skins. The juice obtained is put in special steel containers, where it is fermented with the addition of selected yeasts. The second fermentation then starts according to the Charmat method, at a controlled temperature of 14° C in order to preserve the typical fragrances of the original grapes. It lasts nearly 40 days. During this period carbon dioxide is released naturally so that the wine becomes sparkling. The wine is then cold stabilized, filtered and bottled.

wine_color: Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow.
desc_en_short: Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow. Clear, characteristic, fruity and flowery with scents of Golden apple, Williams pear, acacia flowers and lily of the valley. Quite tasty, harmonious, elegant and fresh. As aperitif, in cocktails, with starters, first courses, fish and poultry.
desc_en_full:
Bottega Gold guarantees rare and elegant fragrances, thanks to the fermentation process which allows to obtain the sparkling wine directly from the must. A packaging with a great emotional effect makes it a really prestigious present. Bottega Gold is obtained by the vinification in white of the Glera grapes, grown in the Conegliano and Valdobbiadene hills. These grapes are hand picked and taken directly into the winery where the stalks are eliminated and the grapes are softly pressed. The must obtained is maintained in stainless steel containers at 0° degrees and then fermented in autoclave for nearly 40 days, at a controlled temperature of 14-15°C, with the addition of selected yeasts (Charmat method).

Brilliant, fine and persistent perlage. Light straw yellow. Clear, characteristic, fruity and flowery with scents of Golden apple, Williams pear, acacia flowers and lily of the valley. Quite tasty, harmonious, elegant and fresh. As aperitif, in cocktails, with starters, first courses, fish and poultry.

name: Bottega  Moscato Manzoni Rosé
wine_color: Brilliant, with fine and lasting perlage .Rose-colored.
desc_en_short:
This rosé sparkling wine is characterized by its distinctive sweet taste. The bright pink painted bottle is fully part of the collection of glamour Bottega sparkling wines. The label, which depicts a rose in full bloom, evokes the aroma of the wine.

Awards

Silver Medal - The Asian Rosé Masters - HK (2018)

Commended - DWWA Decanter World Wine Awards - UK (2017)

Gold Medal - The Global Rosé Masters - Drink Business - UK (2014)

Silver Medal - Cathay Pacific Hong Kong International Wine and Spirit Competition HKIWSC - HK (2014)
desc_en_full:
Characteristics and production process

The long and valuable work of selection performed in the first years of '900 by prof. Luigi Manzoni at the old School of Enology of Conegliano led to the cultivation of a series of crosses, including this cross Manzoni 13.0.25. The curious acronym is tied to the cataloging system in the field found in the notebooks of the professor. It is a cross between the Raboso and Moscato.This is a sparkling wine that originates from grapes grown in the Province of Treviso, in the Prosecco wine area, famous for its great wine tradition. The skins of the grapes are subjected to a process of maceration with the must that lasts for 24 hours. The sparkling process starts after the addition of selected yeasts directly to the must. The resulting wine has the fresh and fragrant character of the original grapes, a low alcohol content and a pleasantly sweet taste.

name: Perrier-Jouet  Grand Brut Champagne (750 ml)
wine_color: A pale and sparkling yellow robe, suggestive of vivacity.
desc_en_short: The Perrier-Jouët Grand Brut is delicate, elegant and balanced. The striking freshness and vivacity of the floral and fruity fragrances (yellow fruits and fresh fruits) take root, before giving way to subtle notes of vanilla and butter, lending the wine a fruity and consistent character. The notes of cherry plum, lemon and bergamot orange emerge first, then make way for the flowers of fruit trees, such as lime and honeysuckle. These are followed by notes of butter, madeleine cakes and vanilla sugar. Lingering notes of grapefruit, white peaches, green pears, apple trees and green hazelnuts round off the taste. Ampleness and vivacity balance out in the mouth, developing into a flavour of round and vinous elegance. A fresh, balanced champagne with a long finish. Its flavours are best revealed between 7 and 9°. Can be matched with dishes such as carpaccio of fish or white meats. Its lively vivacity works well with crisp vegetables and tofu.
desc_en_full:
Wine Composition

A blend of fifty different Crus, Perrier-Jouët Grand Brut expresses the quality and diversity of the House winemaking tradition. Its assemblage creates a balance between the consistency of the Perrier-Jouët style and the effects of the climate of the year. With 12 to 20% of reserve wines added, Perrier-Jouët Grand Brut is dominated by the elegance and finesse of the Chardonnay (20%) from the best vineyards in the Côte des Blancs. The delicate structure is brought by the Pinot Noir (40%) primarily sourced from the Montagne de Reims and the Vallée de la Marne, but also from the best Crus in the Aube region such as Les Riceys. Pinot Meunier (40%) from the Vallée de la Marne brings harmony, fruit and roundness to the wine.

name: Cloudy Bay  Sauvignon Blanc
desc_en_short: Cloudy Bay Vineyards, established in 1985, is today a partnership between champagne house Veuve Clicquot Ponsardin and co-founder of Cape Mentelle Vineyards in Western Australia, David Hohnen. The Cloudy Bay team is committed to producing 'wines of region' and strives to enhance the pure, bracing flavors naturally afforded by the climate and soils of Marlborough. The winery and vineyards are situated in the Wairau Valley in Marlborough at the northern end of New Zealand's South Island. This unique and cool wine region enjoys a maritime climate with the longest hours of sunshine of any place in New Zealand. Cloudy Bay has estate vineyards located at prime sites within the Wairau Valley and long-term supply agreements with five Wairau Valley growers. The main varieties grown are Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay and Pinot Noir.

The Cloudy Bay team is committed to producing 'wines of region' and strives to enhance the pure, bracing flavors naturally afforded by the climate and soils of Marlborough. The winery and vineyards are situated in the Wairau Valley in Marlborough at the northern end of New Zealand's South Island. This unique and cool wine region enjoys a maritime climate with the longest hours of sunshine of any place in New Zealand. Cloudy Bay has estate vineyards located at prime sites within the Wairau Valley and long-term supply agreements with five Wairau Valley growers. The main varieties grown are Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay and Pinot Noir.

Straw-yellow in color, aromas of green apples and pitted fruits complement undertones of pears and grapefruits. On the palate, crisp acidity and a rich body make for an easy-to-drink white wine that is particularly refreshing on a hot summer day.

This wine is great as a sipping wine, but it also pairs well with antipasti, ceviche, pulpo gallego, and Asian cuisine.

name: Albert Bichot  Domaine Long-Depaquit  Chablis
wine_color: pale yellow colour with light green
desc_en_short:
This Chablis' crystalline limpidity and its pale yellow colour with light green highlights tell a great deal about its character which is bursting with mineral precision and finesse. Very pure nose that is typical of Chablis (white flowers, flint) opens up to a palate that boasts an elegant combination of almond notes and lovely vivacity.

In northern Burgundy, not far from Auxerre, the Chablis appellation covers an approximately fifteen-kilometre radius around the village of the same name. It was the monks of the Cistercian abbey of Pontigny who began growing vines in the region. Chablis is located at the south-eastern extremity of the Paris Basin. The grapes used to craft this Chablis at the Domaine Long-Depaquit estate come from a mosaic of terroirs, which lends remarkable complexity to the wine.

name: Nik Weis  Urban Riesling
desc_en_short: Urban Riesling from Nik Weis is a fresh, vibrant off-dry Mosel Riesling — light on its feet with crisp green apple, citrus zest, and a delicate floral lift, balanced by characteristic Mosel slate minerality and racy acidity.
desc_en_full:
Nik Weis is a fifth-generation winemaker based in the village of Leiwen in the Mosel, and his Urban Riesling is designed as an accessible, expressive introduction to the Mosel style — lively, refreshing, and deeply food-friendly.

Made from estate Riesling grown on the region's legendary steep slate slopes, the wine is fermented slowly at cool temperatures to preserve its vibrant aromatics. It is light in body, off-dry in style, and brilliantly balanced — green apple, lime zest, white peach, and peach blossom on the nose, followed by a palate that dances with racy acidity, a delicate sweetness, and a clean, mineral slate finish that is pure Mosel.

Exceptionally versatile at the table: excellent with Thai cuisine, Vietnamese food, sushi, grilled salmon, lightly spiced chicken, and fresh cheeses. Also a wonderful aperitif.

name: Tenuta Lunelli  Pietragrande
desc_en_short: Pietragrande is an intriguing and attractive Chardonnay - made even more precious by a dash of Sauvignon - that charms one with the elegance of its aromas and its delicate persistence. It is the result of careful vinification, designed to highlight Trentino’s vocation for healthy, sustainable mountain viticulture.
desc_en_full: The Lunelli family, third generation of winemakers at Ferrari Trento DOC, has pursued a passion for wine with the creation of Tenute Lunelli, comprised of three wine estates located in three different regions of Italy, Umbria, Tuscany and Trentino. In 2001, fascinated by the ancient and mystical land of Umbria and by the powerful Sagrantino wines, the Lunelli family acquired Tenuta Castelbuono. The property encompasses 30 hectares of vineyards in the areas of Bevagna and Montefalco. The Lunellis have focused on converting the vineyards to organic agriculture and replanting based on extensive research of specific Sagrantino clones. At Castelbuono, old vines are preserved and new vines are added through a structured cloning selection project called the Patriarchs Project. The amazing winery, Il Carapace, is the realization of a collaboration between the Lunelli family, the imaginative sculptor Arnaldo Pomodoro, and the technical architectural expert Giorgio Pedrotti. The huge dome covered with copper, marked with a pattern of grooves and cracks, is inspired by a carapace, a tortoise shell. An accompanying sculptural element in the shape of a red arrow (called the Lampante) piercing the earth highlights the structure in the surrounding landscape. A typical expression of a grape variety that is absolutely unique in terms of power and longevity, the Sagrantino from Tenuta Castelbuono amazes one with its mellowness, the result of skillful vineyard management and long ageing in large oak casks.

desc_en_full:
Created in the 1990s as part of the evolving Penfolds white wine development program, Bin 51 Eden Valley Riesling captures the attributes of Eden Valley. The region's high altitude and cool climate induces Riesling with great finesse, elegance with a capacity for long term cellaring. South Australia has gained worldwide recognition for producing definitive world-class Riesling, with the Eden Valley at the very head of the field. In their youth, the wines possess focused apple/lime aromas, with a mineral edge and marked natural acidity, contributing significantly to the palate texture and flavour. The evolution of Penfolds Bin 51 Eden Valley Riesling opens a new chapter in a long history of Penfolds involvement in winemaking from this variety.

Testing Notes.

name: Nik Weis  Mosel Dry Riesling VDP.ESTATE WINES (GUTSWEINE)
desc_en_short: Nik Weis Mosel Dry Riesling is a VDP-classified estate wine of exceptional precision — a bone-dry expression of Mosel Riesling from slate slopes, delivering intense citrus, green apple, and white peach character with razor-sharp acidity and a long, stony mineral finish.
desc_en_full:
The VDP (Verband Deutscher Prädikatsweingüter) classification is Germany's most respected wine quality hierarchy, and Nik Weis's Gutswein (estate wine) classification marks this as a serious, terroir-driven expression from one of the Mosel's finest producers.

Made from Riesling grown on the legendary steep slate slopes of the Mosel Valley, this dry Riesling is vinified in stainless steel to full dryness — a style that places the terroir at the centre of the experience rather than sweetness. The result is a wine of crystalline clarity: pale lemon-green, with an intense nose of citrus zest, Granny Smith apple, grapefruit, green herbs, and the distinctive petrol note that marks fine aged Riesling. The palate is sleek, precise, and electrifying — dry and nervy with soaring acidity, perfectly balanced by the ripe fruit concentration, and a long, laser-sharp finish that leaves a wash of slate minerality.

A superb match for oysters, scallops, crab, sashimi, or any seafood preparation. Also excellent with spicy Asian cuisine where its dry mineral character offers refreshing contrast.

desc_en_short:
A harmonious, beautifully balanced Riesling, made in a dry style that is extraordinarily versatile with food.

This well-rounded Riesling is the dry counterpart to the lightly sweet Dr. L Riesling. Together, these two wines provide a glimpse into the unique stylistic diversity of Riesling, capturing the elegant and racy characteristics of steep, slate-soil Mosel vineyards at a very reasonable price. They are produced with grapes from contracted growers throughout the Mosel River valley who work closely with brothers Ernst and Thomas Loosen to achieve excellent quality in every vintage.
desc_en_full: Loosen Bros. (“Gebrüder Loosen” in German), is Ernst Loosen, of the renowned Dr. Loosen estate, and his younger brother, Thomas. Together, the brothers work with small growers throughout the Mosel region to source fruit for the wines, which are vinified by the same highly skilled cellar team that produces the Dr. Loosen estate wines. Very strict quality standards require that the growers improve their viticulture in order to sustainably grow the healthy, perfectly ripe fruit that Ernst and Thomas want for these wines.

name: Louis Latour  Genevrieres Meursault 1er Cru
desc_en_short: Situated just south of Volnay and Pommard in the Côte de Beaune, Meursault is the most extensive southerly white wine commune of the Côte-d'Or. The perfect south-easterly exposure and the rocky, stony soils contribute greatly to the remarkable finesse of which the wines of Meursault are capable. 'Genevrières' comes from 'genévrier' (juniper), a shrub with prickly needles and purple berries which enjoys the limestone soils of Burgundy. Many junipers were replaced by vines, hence the name of the juniper forms part of the name of many parcels of vines. The Meursault 1er Cru "Genevrières" is a great example of a fine Burgundy Chardonnay.

desc_en_full:
Massenez fruits Eaux-de-vie are renowned for their quality and refinement on the palate – high quality product from 40° to 46° certainly , but ones that bring pleasure to every palate : the French "elegance" for sure!

Each year , this quality is celebrated with gold medals at world spirits competitions : a real pride for the whole Massenez team. From luxury bottles to traditional flutes , Massenez has remained authentic both in its packaging and its distilling method. It is a world leader in its sector.

Flagship of the MASSENEZ Brand, Prisonere Pear captivates with its olfactory and gustative qualities and the pear-shaped bottle with a whole pear inside. The bottle is simply attached to the pear tree in flower...so the fruit develops inside the bottle. When it ripens , the fruit and the bottle are carefully detached

Taste Like

Subtle , elegant , Pear flavour , freshness.,spicy liquorice , harmonious

Smell Like

Pear / Fresh / Cleanliness

Body

Light-Medium Bodied

Goes well with

Chicken

Liquor Fact

- Brand : Massenez - Region/Country : Alsace/France - Alcohol (%) : 40% - Bottle Size : 700 ml

Awards

- Gold Medal , the WorlConcour of Spirit in Canada, June 2011

Food Pairing: Spiced orange roast chicken

Ingredients

1.2kg chicken thigh fillets 80ml (1/3 cup) fresh orange juice 80ml (1/3 cup) honey 2 teaspoons olive oil 2 garlic cloves, crushed 2 teaspoons ground coriander 1 teaspoon ground cumin 1 teaspoon turmeric 1 orange, thinly sliced Finely chopped fresh chives, to serve

Preparation

1. Place the chicken in an ovenproof dish. Add the orange juice, honey, oil, garlic, coriander, cumin and turmeric and gently toss until combined. Season with salt and pepper. Cover with plastic wrap and place in the fridge for 1 hour to marinate.

2. Preheat oven to 180°C. Place the orange over the top of the chicken. Roast in the oven, basting with pan juices, for 45 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Place on a serving platter and top with chives to serve

name: The Botanist  Islay Dry Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
The botanist is a gin of layered complexity. A progressive exploration of the botanical heritage of our isle of islay. Nine classic gin botanicals are augmented by 22 local herbs and flowers, foraged responsibly and by hand from the hills, shores and bogs of this fertile hebridean island by our own team of botanical scientists. The result is a gin as much for the mind as the palate; a thinking person’s gin. a roller coaster botanical odyssey in a glass.

Awards :

Silver - Gin - 2019 : San Francisco World Spirits Competition
desc_en_full:
Tasting Note :

Nose : The aromas explode like an olfactory Aurora Borealis filling the senses with meteorites of smell sensations as they explode from the glass. Sweet delicate menthol, apple mint, spring woodlands, juniper, coriander with aniseed undertones, lemon and orange peel, a bouquet of summer flowers on the machir, honey from thistle, coconut from gorse, wild mint and summer meadows. It's a magical melody of Islay's' natural bounty from the Atlantic washed beaches to the summit of heather covered hills. Inhale and you're there on the "Queen of the Hebrides"!

Palate : The taste is rich and mellow; cool on entry then as it reaches the back palate you can feel the warmth and absolute purity of slow unhurried distillation. This is a bewitching, delectable and luxurious gin; its citrus freshness excites and stimulates the taste buds allowing you to experience a starburst of flavours as they explode across the palate.

Finish : All this from a beaten up old pot still, operated by beaten up distillers on the coast of heaven.

Mood : Carefree. The electric buzz of illicit anticipation.
