name: Bombay Sapphire Gin (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Bombay Sapphire Gin is one of the more popular premium gins on the market for a very good reason. This London Dry gin is one of those that is distinctly gin but not overwhelming on any of the botanicals, which makes it ideal for mixing into any gin cocktail. Beyond the mixable, aromatic flavor, Bombay Sapphire is also reasonably priced and readily available so it can (and should) become a reliable staple in any bar. This is also an ideal gin to introduce people to gin because it is not overpowering but very appealing to any taste
desc_en_full:
Bombay Sapphire is a brand of gin owned by Bacardi that was first launched in 1987 by IDV. In 1997 Diageo sold the brand to Bacardi. Its name originates from gin's popularity in India during the British Raj and the sapphire in question is the Star of Bombay on display at the Smithsonian Institution. Bombay Sapphire is marketed in a flat-sided, sapphire-coloured bottle that bears a picture of Queen Victoria on the label.

The flavouring of the drink comes from a recipe of ten ingredients (which the bottle's label boasts as "10 exotic botanicals"): almond, lemon peel, liquorice, juniper berries, orris root, angelica, coriander, cassia, cubeb, and grains of paradise. The spirit is triple distilled using a carterhead still, and the alcohol vapours are passed through a mesh/basket containing the ten botanicals, in order to gain flavour and aroma. This gives a lighter, more floral gin rather than the more-common 'punchy' gins that are distilled using a copper pot still. Water from Lake Vyrnwy is added to bring the strength of Bombay Sapphire down to 40.0% (UK, Australia).

In 2011 it was announced that the company is planning to move the distillation process to a new facility in Laverstoke, Hampshire. The plans include the restoration of the former Portal's paper mill, and the construction of a visitor centre. In February 2012, they received planning permission for the site and the centre is scheduled to be opened in Spring 2014. Production and bottling of the drink is contracted out by Bacardi to G&J Greenall.

name: Gin Mare  Mediterranean Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
The finest botanicals are selected from across the Mediterranean, coming together to create a carefully hand-crafted gin that stands out as being savory and unique. Herbaceous on the nose, with thyme and juniper dominating, but also a presence of olives, and a wonderful explosion of rosemary, basil & coriander when tasted.

Awards :

Gold - Super Premium - 2016 : The Gin Masters (The Spirits Business)
desc_en_full:
Gin Mare Mediterranean Gin (700 ml)

Gin Mare also elicits the generational evolution of this category via a family with great weight in this work. Gin Mare has long been the standard for gins that push the boundaries of flavour. Using botanicals like juniper, olives, thyme, citrus, rosemary and basil illicit fascinating flavour profiles for this Mediterranean gin. The Gin Mare distillery is located in an ancient fishing village between the Costa Brava and the Costa Dorada. They use a premium barley base, through delicate maceration and distillation which is independent for each botanical and use authentic blending which expresses all the characteristics of each botanical in the final product. The citrus is macerated once a year into a neutral spirit with an ABV of 50%. The Giro Ribot family have been making wine and spirits since 1835 and after extensive flavour testing of over 45 botanicals, created the recipe that is now the world-famous Gin Mare.

Tasting Notes.

Nose: Herbal nose with berry fruits. Hints of soapiness and perfume.

Palate: Juniper with bursts of basil, rosemary, and thyme. Just a hint of coriander.

Finish: Zesty, spiced finish.

name: Sakurao  Japanese Dry Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
A craft gin that utilizes 9 kinds of botanicals, including fresh citrus from Hiroshima, and is distilled using a combination of steeping and vapor methods.

Besides 9 kinds of botanicals from Hiroshima, 5 kinds of imported botanicals such as juniper berry and coriander seed are also used for SAKURAO GIN ORIGINAL.

The fusion of a citrus-like aroma with the traditional taste of gin is the most distinctive feature of our original gin.
desc_en_full:
Innovation from Tradition From Hiroshima to the World

Our distilling master uses 100-years of distilling techniques and know-how to select only the best ingredients from Hiroshima to create SAKURAO GIN. Our botanicals produce a wide variety of aromatic notes, including citrus such as the florid-smelling lemon and the rich fragrance of yuzu, as well as aromas from Japanese cypress and oyster shells. Our gin also uses cherry blossoms, known as sakura in Japanese, which is the symbol of Sakurao, where our distillery is located. And our LIMITED gin, in addition to juniper berries, also utilizes a total of 17 specially selected botanicals from Hiroshima and has been distilled using traditional methods from England, the home of dry gin. Craft gins proudly made in Hiroshima presented from SAKURAO DISTILLERY to the world.

name: Drumshanbo Gunpowder  Irish Gin (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Here at the edge of a lake in a shed in a small Irish town, the ordinary is made extraordinary. Within is the laboratory of PJ Rigney, boundary-pushing begetter of hand-made spirits who slow distills gin with natures finest oriental botanicals and gunpowder tea.

WIN – In celebration of a very different Pride this year, we are giving you the chance to win one of our Limited Edition Collectors’ Bottles and 6 minis to celebrate Pride 2020. To enter simply follow us, like this post & retweet. Good luck. T&C's. #drinkresponsibly #PRIDE2020
desc_en_full:
Testing Notes

The nose is light and citrus-forward. The more delicate handling of the citrus ingredients is quite evident here. Fresh lemon zest highlights a subtle herbaceous, sagey juniper with the slightest hint of green tea and meadowsweet.

Drumshanbo Gunpowder Gin explodes with flavor on the palate. Citrus once again dominates. This time its grapefruit. Juniper and green tea are present mid-palate: the juniper herbaceous and bright, the green tea calling to mind Jasmine green tea, as you might get before sushi at a Japanese restaurant.

Towards the end though, it turns sweet and spicy/herbal. Anise and cardamom are subtly and expertly played in here. Meadowsweet, especially late and towards the finish lends its a honeyed, vanilla-like nuance.

name: Amaro Montenegro (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Amaro Montenegro was born in 1885 with the name Elisir Lungavita and immediately received notable recognition. 11 years later, marking the marriage of the charming Princess Elena of Montenegro to Prince Vittorio Emanuele III, Stanislao decided to dedicate his storied elixir to her royal highness, changing it’s name to Amaro Montenegro. 40 Botanicals create a unique taste as bitter and herbaceous, citrusy and sweet with 7 discovery notes.

It can be enjoyed straight, with soda water and lemon rind, or hot, as well as in various combinations in modern cocktails.
desc_en_full:

It can be enjoyed straight, with soda water and lemon rind, or hot, as well as in various combinations in modern cocktails.

name: Baileys  Original (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Baileys Irish Cream is a classic combination of Irish whisky, fresh Irish dairy cream and the finest spirits, infused together to create a silky velvety liqueur. Best enjoyed poured over ice, added to cocktails or even to coffee for an indulgent twist.
desc_en_full:
Baileys :

Baileys Irish cream is a perfect marriage of fresh, premium quality Irish dairy cream, finest spirits, Irish whiskey and a proprietary recipe of chocolate flavors. Over 80% of the raw ingredients and packaging used to make and present Baileys Irish cream is sourced from the island of Ireland. The cream is fresh, dairy cream that comes from two dairy suppliers. The whiskey is a triple pot still blend, made to a proprietary specification and supplied from The Old Midleton Distillery, Co Cork. The sugar comes from sugar cane and sugar beet. The distinct style of Baileys Irish cream is derived from a unique proprietary recipe of natural cocoa extracts, giving Baileys Irish cream its chocolate character and essence. 275 million litres of fresh Irish milk are required annually to produce the fresh cream used in the manufacture of Baileys Irish cream. 40,000 of the top-bred Irish dairy cows produced the daily cream requirement for Baileys Irish cream grazing on approximately 1500 selected and accredited Irish farms mainly on the East coast of Ireland. This affords full traceability.

Drinking :

As is the case with milk, cream will curdle whenever it comes into contact with a weak acid. Milk and cream contain casein, which coagulates, when mixed with weak acids such as lemon, tonic water, or traces of wine. While this outcome is undesirable in most situations, some cocktails specifically encourage coagulation.

Awards :

International spirit ratings competitions, including at the San Francisco World Spiritis Competition, the Beverage Testing Institute, and others, have assessed Baileys offerings. In general, Baileys has performed well, with the classic Irish Cream generally receiving the highest awards.

name: Benedictine D.O.M Liqueur (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Nose : Those who have failed to note this liqueur ways in at 40% abv are soon reminded when they plunge their nose into the glass. After the initial alcohol hit subsides, a delightfully understated aroma of lavender honey and complex herbal spice dotted by a range of botanicals that intermingle to create a complex and enjoyable aroma.

Neat : This is not liqueur that does anything by halves; Its high (for a liqueur) abv, intense sweetness and robust herbal palate are all big on the palate. A few ice cubes helps to loosen things up a little, revealing the true beauty of this liqueur; And what a beauty it is. As the initial honeyed sweetness fades, a mix of Christmas spice, summer fruits and gin-like aromatics offer up a deliciously complex and herbal profile. There is not a hint of the ‘medicinal’ taste that often is present in herbal spirits, and as such this is a liqueur with wide appeal. The finish ends with a lovely marzipan note that will keep you coming back for more.

name: Plantation  Original Dark Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The essence of Plantation Rum Original Dark is the wealth of its palate and its technique of double aging. Plantation Original Dark comprises the elegant rums of Trinidad which are distilled then aged in their tropical climate in the Caribbean in American oak barrels under high heat before traveling to France. Once in Europe, the House cellar master decides on less “high esters” for the Trinidadian rum and blends in old rums from Jamaica (15 - 20 yrs.) This end result finishes like sweet nectar but also adapts beautifully to classic cocktails made with dark rum such as the Mai Tai or Daiquiri.
desc_en_full:
Distillation

Column and pot for the small quantity of Bonificateur

Tropical and continental ageing

Special process: 3 years old Trinidad blended with our Plantation proprietory Bonificateur. Bonificateur is very old (+/15 to 20 Years) pot still high esters and petites eaux aged rum refined with Ferrand technic.

Taste Profile

"Dynamic aromatic activity going on here in the opening fragrance, which includes hints of black peppercorn, clove, cinnamon, baked cherry, prune, Danish, molasses; later sniffs encounter vegetation/forest, leaves, black tea, dry stone/slate. Entry is fruity with white raisins, dried pineapple, and is off-dry, brown sugar-like; mid-palate is spicy/peppery, piquant, minerally, and significantly drier than the entry stage. Ends, fruity, spicy, charming."

name: Plantation  O.F.T.D Overproof Dark Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Plantation O.F.T.D. (Old Fashioned Traditional Dark) Overproof is a group effort. Alexandre Gabriel, Master Blender for Plantation Rum, brought together six rum luminaries--Jeff "Beachbum" Berry from Latitude 29, Martin Cate from Smuggler's Cove, Paul McFadyen from Trailer Happiness, Paul McGee from Lost Lake, Scotty Schuder from Dirty Dick, and Dave Wondrich, cocktail historian extraordinaire. Over many tasting and blending sessions, they formulated a rum ideally suited for use in top-shelf Tiki and tropical cocktails. The O.F.T.D. is a blend of rums from Barbados, Guyana, and Jamaica in the overproof dark style, and replaces Plantation’s Trinidad-sourced Original Dark Overproof Rum.

Column and pot for the small quantity of Bonificateur

Tropical and continental ageing

Taste Profile

"First inhalations are not attacked by the stratospheric alcohol degree, but rather are engaged by supple, off-dry scents of brown sugar and gentle spices, clove nutmeg, maces; further time in the glace allows for the spices to develop, especially the nutmeg, but now also cinnamon and vanilla bean are evident. name: The Colonist  Black Spiced Rum (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Rum tasting in the Finnish wilderness with my good old friend continued with The Colonist Spiced Black Rum. Unique bottle and label design. Dark brown color. Plenty of oak, vanilla and a massive amount of unaged alcohol to the nose and palate when neat. Quite a kick at the end. Harsh even as a mixer rum.
desc_en_full:
The first association of a colonist comes to us as from Columbus times when Europeans started to explore the seas and the first ones landed on America's shores. However the word "Colonist" can be perceived as 'the first' in any rough, habited or inhabited land, sea, planet... It is an adventure, the beginning of many promising things, fortunes, opportunities.

We wanted to create a strong emotional, full in details, eye catching rum both in design and taste. This particular spirit will challenge the usual perception of rum. In flavour it will be rounded, sweet and smooth. However it still demonstrates a punch you would expect from a Caribbean aged rum. The Colonist - the word itself is used to describe adventurous, fearless, strong and smart people. As our rum comes from the Caribbean, we enriched our design with plenty of colourful birds and plants from the area.

Our basic rums -white and dark- would be a perfect companion for cocktails. It's distinctive design would be an easy option for Cuba Libre lovers. The Spiced one is for more complex cocktails. And The Reserva will be enjoyed pure in a small spirit glass.

desc_en_full:
Tequila Blanco — also known as Silver or Plata — represents the purest expression of agave spirit: unaged or only briefly rested, allowing the character of the Blue Weber agave plant to shine without the influence of wood. Dos Companeros Silver embodies this philosophy with a clean, vibrant profile suited equally to cocktails and straight sipping.

The nose leads with fresh raw agave, lime zest, and a touch of green pepper, backed by subtle floral notes and white pepper. On the palate it is clean and lively, with a smooth texture, refreshing citrus acidity, and a long, slightly peppery finish. The agave character stays present throughout, marking it as an authentic expression of its category.

Use in Margaritas, Palomas, or Tequila Sunrises, or serve neat over ice with a wedge of lime. Also an excellent base for spicy Mexican food pairings.

Nose :

On the nose, Jack Daniel's Black Label offers gentle aromas of caramel, vanilla, and butterscotch. A hint of wood is apparent and toffee notes begin to appear in the glass as it opens up.

Body and Palate :

Since switching to its 80 proof formula from 86 proof, some claim Jack Daniel's Black Label has lost some of its luster. I disagree but I do think that the 80 proof formulation results in a lighter mouthfeel, which isn't unpleasant, just different.

On the palate, Jack Daniel's has a smooth smoky sweetness. One reviewer has descibed it as having a corn syrup note to it and I agree. Jack Daniels has a reputation for a light licorice note to all of their whiskies and its readily apparent in Black Label. The licorice note isn't overwhelming and lends a nice background note to flavors of vanilla, toasted oak, fresh brewed coffee, nuts and English toffee. What is most interesting about Jack Daniel's Black Label to me is the number of flavors that come into play over the course of drinking it. I challenge you to sit down and think about the flavors appearing on your palate. You'll be surprised at just how much complexity and depth there is hidden under the blanket of vanilla at the front of the palate.

Finish :

Jack Daniel's Black Label offers a quick, sweet finish. Nice oak notes, a hint of spices, and perhaps a touch of cedar mingle with a warm caramel finish that gives way to white pepper and walnuts.

name: Johnnie Walker  Black Label (1 L)
desc_en_short: A true icon, recognised as the benchmark for all other deluxe blends. Created using only whiskies aged for a minimum of 12 years from the four corners of Scotland, Johnnie Walker Black Label has an unmistakably smooth, deep, complex character. An impressive whisky to share on any occasion.
desc_en_full:
STORY

In 1909, 'Extra Special Old Highland Whisky' was renamed Johnnie Walker Black Label. Johnnie Walker Black Label was created to represent the very best from all four corners of Scotland and was quickly recognised as a new benchmark in blending excellence. Embraced by the great and the good, it has been immortalised in a painting by none other than Winston Churchill and on film in the cult classic Blade Runner. Today, it is one of the world's best-selling Deluxe Scotch Whiskies.

FLAVOURS

Johnnie Walker Black Label is rich, complex and incredibly well-balanced, full of dark fruits, sweet vanilla and signature smokiness.

Blended exclusively from whiskies matured for at least 12 years, it brings together flavours from the four corners of Scotland to create an experience that is complex, deep and rewarding. Johnnie Walker Black Label rolls decadently over the tongue, releasing intense sweet vanillas that give way to orange zest and aromas of spice and raisins. The finish is unbelievably smooth and layered with rich smoke, peat and malt.

SERVE

Enjoy Johnnie Walker Black Label in any way you like - on its own, with a dash of water, or along with your favourite mixer.

We recommend Johnnie Walker Black Label and soda. Here's the perfect serve.

I Fill a tall glass with cubed ice

II Add 25 ml Johnnie Walker Black Label

III Add 75 ml soda water, or fill to the top

IV Garnish with a wedge of lime

V Enjoy

name: Johnnie Walker  Red Label (1 L)
desc_en_short: The character of the whisky is defined by intense, spicy, zingy, edgy flavours. RED LABEL bursts onto the palate with the freshness of spray from a crashing wave, followed by the zing of aromatic spices and finally a long, lingering, smoky finish. The sensation in the mouth is complex – ‘sweet chilli’ is a good description of this taste and the tingling sensation on the tongue.
desc_en_full:
"JOHNNIE WALKER RED LABEL is the world's best-selling Scotch Whisky."

The story of RED LABEL started with the pioneering vision of the Walker family. John Walker and his sons saw an opportunity to take their whisky far beyond the borders of Scotland. They strove to make RED LABEL the world's favourite whisky and didn't give up until they achieved that goal, becoming one of the world's first truly global brands.

Alexander Walker created the distinctive square bottle in 1860, with a label angled at precisely 24°. In a world that predominately uses rounded bottles, it soon became the most recognised whisky globally – it's an iconic design for an iconic whisky.

The intrepid merchants and engineers who set sail from Scotland over 100 years ago taking RED LABEL with them are responsible for its rapid growth in popularity worldwide. As the Walker family once said, "We sailed wherever ship could sail", a pioneering spirit that continues today.

name: Bowmore  12 Years Old (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Puffs of peat smoke and pools of honey, sharpened by lemon zest. Breathe in subtle lemon and honey, balanced beautifully by Bowmore's trademark peaty smokiness. Sip sweet and delicious heather honey and gentle peat smoke. Savour the long, mellow finish.

Awards :

Gold Scotch Single Malt - Islay - 2019 : International Wine & Spirit Competition

Silver Independents’ Single Malts 21 years old and over - 2019 : International Spirits Challenge

Gold Single Malt Scotch - to 12 Yrs - 2013 : San Francisco World Spirits Competition
desc_en_full:
The Bowmore Balance

Complex yet perfectly balanced, our 12 Years Old reflects the raw essence of Bowmore - thrashing waves, windswept landscapes and generations of tradition. Bowmore 12 Years Old has received much critical acclaim; the late, great whisky writer Michael Jackson proclaimed it "remarkably long and complex". We like to think of it as vanilla ice cream at a beach bonfire.

Tasting Notes

On the eye warm amber.

Breathe in subtle lemon and honey, balanced beautifully by Bowmore's trademark peaty smokiness.

Sip sweet and delicious heather honey and gentle peat smoke.

Savour the long, mellow finish.

A Match Made In Heaven

Try some smoked salmon, langoustines, scallops or oysters with a little fresh lemon and a generous measure of our 12 Years Old to see for yourself just how perfectly they complement each other.

name: Johnnie Walker  Gold Label Reserve (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Johnnie Walker Gold Label Reserve is an indulgent, luxurious and multi-layered blend with a smooth balance of sweet fruits and creaminess that evolves into deeper honeyed tones before finishing in lingering waves of wood, fruit and light, sweet West-coast smoke.

Awards :

Winner - World's Best Blended Whisky - 2018 : World Whiskies Awards
desc_en_full:
When John Walker first established the family business in 1820 he began a lineage of blending expertise that continues unbroken to this day. Carefully honed skills have been handed down from generation to generation, with the result that today's JOHNNIE WALKER® GOLD LABEL RESERVE™ is testament to almost two centuries of blending excellence.

JOHNNIE WALKER® GOLD LABEL RESERVE™ has been personally crafted by the JOHNNIE WALKER® Master Blender as a celebration of his art. He has handpicked prized casks from selected reserves – each matured to perfection – to create a bold blend of intense flavours which is every bit as luxurious and indulgent as its name suggests.

Combining the big bold flavours of JOHNNIE WALKER with well matching ingredients results in a true flavour explosion. The world's best mixologists have been creating these perfect serves. Let's start your flavour adventure here.

name: Compass Box  The Peat Monster (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
PROCESS

Layer curated whiskies from distilleries across the peat spectrum to achieve balance and complexity of flavour. Blended carefully so aromatic, sweet smoke takes centre stage, accented by heavier, medicinal peatiness.

FLAVOUR PROFILE

Peaty and smoky, with a creamy and fruity character on the palate.

SERVING RECOMMENDATIONS

A fantastic late-night whisky that also works beautifully in a classic Scotch whisky Highball.

TECHNICAL DETAILS

Bottled at 46%.

Natural colour.

Not chill filtered.

Blended Malt Scotch Whisky.
desc_en_full:
PEAT WITH ELEGANCE

This is one monster you'll want to invite in.​ Fantastic. In every sense. And once those​ tentacles get a hold, you won't want to escape. ​A bewitching beast from the peat bogs of the​ Scottish coast, discovered warming itself by fires​ of driftwood. A true wonder.​ ​

Frightening at first glance? Maybe. But look​ beyond that show of teeth and this behemoth is​ revealed to be a bit of a sweetie. Which poses the​ question: How does a creation with such intensity,​ so much bite, still caress with a gentle touch?​

​Well, this is no lumbering Goliath. Instead, you'll find a complex character with hidden depths that​ confound expectation. A graceful colossus. A​ leviathan with a sensitive soul. Approach carefully so you don't spook it, get up close, and you may even see a tear in its eye. Because this isn't your typical fiery presence, hellbent on setting everything aflame.​

​This monster is a refined chimaera combining​ fabulous elements of the creations that came​ before it. A sublime blend ushered into reality by the most imaginative of minds. And that tear? It’s not a reflection of sadness, but an appreciation of beauty. Because this beast was conjured into existence to be loved. Step into its universe and You won't be able to resist.

name: Johnnie Walker  Gold Label XR 21 (750 ml)
desc_en_short:
Johnnie Walker XR 21 is a tribute to the legacy of Sir Alexander Walker II, grandson of the brand’s founder, who was honored with the Royal Warrant in the 1920s. Inspired by his private blending notes, XR 21 is crafted with a selection of rare malt and grain whiskies, each aged for at least 21 years.

Some of the whiskies used in this blend come from now-silent distilleries, such as Brora, adding complexity and rarity to its profile. The result is a whisky that reflects the finest blending traditions of John Walker & Sons.
desc_en_full:
Key Character

Smooth, elegant, and luxuriously layered, XR 21 delivers a harmony of honey, ripe fruits, vanilla, roasted nuts, and subtle oak spice. Its regal profile makes it ideal for celebration, gifting, or savoring slowly in refined moments.

This is a whisky designed not just for drinking—but for remembering.

Tasting Notes

Color: Deep amber gold

Nose: Rich honey, ripe peaches, vanilla, cinnamon, toasted almonds, and polished oak

Palate: Silky and balanced with flavors of dried fruit, brown sugar, creamy vanilla, and warm spice

Finish: Long, smooth, and graceful with gentle wood smoke and lingering sweetness

Monkey Shoulder's easy-drinking blend of three single malts - Balvenie, Glenfiddich and Kininvie. name: Suntory  Whisky Kakubin (700 ml)
desc_en_short: The Suntory Kakubin is a budget blend from Suntory. Kakubin means square bottle and althou "Kakubin" is stated nowhere on the bottle it is it's official name. It was originally introduced in 1937 and now bottled at 40% vol. Very drinkable blend for a very low price, no mixer needed. Very clean and simple, really nothing wrong with it.
desc_en_full:
Suntory Kakubin is the most popular whiskey in Japan since its appearance in this country. This whisky contains with spirits, matured in bourbon casks at least 8 years. Master Blender at his own discretion creates a blend of about ten distillates. He could remove one or two spirits, in order to keep from year to year unique Kakubin'sflavours. Whiskey Suntory Kakubin has an amazing soft taste and excellent aroma. Its shape bottle "Kakubin" reminds a turtle shell. Japanese borrowed from Scotland manufacturing technology of making whisky: double distillation in copper pot still alembics; blending with grain whisky; maturing in bourbon and sherry casks. Japan has perfect environment for creating and aging whiskey, but nevertheless, manufacturers prefer to bring a large batch of Scottish malt whiskey, which is then added into the alcohol mixtures. Thus the preserved traditions, so revered in Japan. The result is a great soft pure malt whiskey with distinctive notes of peat, with a clean taste and exquisite harmonious flavors. Father" of Japanese whiskey is rightly considered MasatakaTaketsuru who arrived in Scotland in 1918 specifically to learn the art of distillation. Two years later, he and his Scottish wife returned to his homeland, where he became the only specialist in this field. Using acquired knowledge, he has assisted in the construction and launch of the distillery "Yamazaki", whose founder is Shinjiro Tori - head of "Suntory". The main brands of Japanese whiskey are Suntory Old, Suntory Royal, Suntory Reserve, Suntory Imperial, Suntory Yamazaki, Suntory Hibiki, SuntoryKakubin.

• Nose: Very floral, light, a bit malt and honey, hint of alcohol • Mouth: Mild, honey, a tad "grainy", tad alcohol • Finish: Sweet honey, tad pepper, rather short but very clean

desc_en_full:
History

Distilled in the Dufftown distillery of Speyside, where fine single malts have been produced since 1896, exceptional care is taken at every stage of producing The Singleton to create the best taste possible. Perfectly Balanced, Naturally Rich and Smooth' is the wording on the label, and it is the ideal summary of this striking malt. There is a local saying in Dufftown: "Rome was built on seven hills and Dufftown stands on seven stills. Is it any wonder then that the Speyside region is widely revered around the world for its sublime malt whiskies? This fertile triangle of land between mountain and sea has long been known as the Garden of Scotland. Bang in the centre, this geologically stunning landscape, split by rivers and valleys, and dominated by high eroded peaks, is like paradise for the enthusiastic walker. With time to explore, you can visit a particularly beautiful spot where the River Dullan bubbles past a distillery. Look carefully and you'll see kingfishers diving for fish. Welcome to the picturesque home of the Singleton™ of Dufftown. Following those early attempts by rival distillers to divert the course of the crystal-clear spring waters used by the Dufftown Distillery, there has been little for those at the distillery to worry about, allowing them to focus on the delivery of this magnificent spirit. Longer fermentation and slower distillation than many competitors ensures exceptional smoothness and depth of flavour. The liquid is then matured in a combination of American and European oak casks (with only the finest casks hand selected for a richer taste) to ensure extra flavour and perfect balance. The unique bottle shape of The Singleton of Dufftown is inspired by a traditional hipflask while the colour of the glass reflects the blue flint glass used at the turn of the previous century. The Singleton is regarded by many to be 'the single best tasting single malt whisky'.

Testing Note:

Colour: Reddish gold

Smell: Malty with cereal/barley sweetness, buttery toast, wood shavings, hay and walnut.

Taste: Orange zest spiciness perks up a malty core of nuts, oak and toffee, hints of cut grass.

Finish: Oaky, rich with good length, some fruit lingers.

name: Lagavulin  8 Years (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Released to commemorate the 200th anniversary of the founding of Lagavulin distillery by John Johnston in 1816, this eight-year-old is big and smoky, with sweet spice that becomes apparent with a drop of water. Inspired by the visit of Alfred Barnard to the distillery in the 1880s when he tried an 'exceptionally fine' eight-year-old from the distillery, this is a fitting tribute for the milestone.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Note :

Nose:

Immediately quite soft with clean, fresh notes, faint hints of milk chocolate and lemon and then developing fragrant tea-scented smoke alongside nose-drying, maritime aromas, with subtle cereal. A prickliness seen earlier now develops, while the trademark Lagavulin dryness emerges as fresh newsprint. Softly sooty. Softer, fuller and more rounded with water: it's not hugely fruity but there's just a trace of red berry preserve, perhaps, beneath the smokiness, which comes sharply into focus.

Palate:

A soothing light texture, with a magnificently full-on Lagavulin taste that's somehow even bigger than you expect; sweet, smoky and warming, with a growing, smoky pungency, then dry, with more smoke. Charred, with minty, dark chocolate. Beautifully balanced mid-palate then salty, oven-charred baked potato skins and smoke. Water rounds things, the taste still mighty yet more succulent, sweeter, spicier and now tongue-tingling, mint-fresh and warming.

Finish:

Lovely; clean, very long and smoky. Smoothly, subtle minted smoke surrounds chocolate tannins, leaving a late drying note to emerge in time. It's warming, soft and still smoky with water, not as long or intense now, yet still leaving the palate dry as sweet smoke lingers on the breath.

name: Douglas Laing's  The Gauldrons (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
Part of Douglas Laing's Remarkable Regional Malts series, which also includes the likes of Big Peat and Rock Oyster, The Gauldrons is a small-batch blended malt from Campeltown, named after the area in the region whose name literally means 'bay of storms'. Both maritime and with a sweet, honeycomb character typical of the region, this is made from a marriage of the independent bottler's best casks of Campeltown single malt. The bottle's spiderweb design is a nod to the local legend that King Robert the Bruce, having been defeated by his enemies, was inspired by the sight of a spider building his web in the bay to carry on fighting.

PALATE : Initially dry (beachy and maritime) warming to crunched sugar, barley and oak.

name: Jack Daniel's  Old No.7 (375 ml)
desc_en_short: Jack Daniel’s® Tennessee Whiskey is made with the careful selection of the finest corn, rye and barley malt. These choice grains are mixed with the iron-free water that flows from the Cave Spring found on Distillery grounds. Our process includes the unique step of charcoal - mellowing, which give Jack Daniel’s its trademark smoothness and character and makes it a Tennessee Whiskey.
desc_en_full:
Jack Daniel's Old No.7 Tennessee Whiskey

Nose :

On the nose, Jack Daniel's Black Label offers gentle aromas of caramel, vanilla, and butterscotch. A hint of wood is apparent and toffee notes begin to appear in the glass as it opens up.

Body and Palate :

Since switching to its 80 proof formula from 86 proof, some claim Jack Daniel's Black Label has lost some of its luster. I disagree but I do think that the 80 proof formulation results in a lighter mouthfeel, which isn't unpleasant, just different.

On the palate, Jack Daniel's has a smooth smoky sweetness. One reviewer has descibed it as having a corn syrup note to it and I agree. Jack Daniels has a reputation for a light licorice note to all of their whiskies and its readily apparent in Black Label. The licorice note isn't overwhelming and lends a nice background note to flavors of vanilla, toasted oak, fresh brewed coffee, nuts and English toffee. What is most interesting about Jack Daniel's Black Label to me is the number of flavors that come into play over the course of drinking it. I challenge you to sit down and think about the flavors appearing on your palate. You'll be surprised at just how much complexity and depth there is hidden under the blanket of vanilla at the front of the palate.

Finish :

Jack Daniel's Black Label offers a quick, sweet finish. Nice oak notes, a hint of spices, and perhaps a touch of cedar mingle with a warm caramel finish that gives way to white pepper and walnuts.

name: The Glenturret  10 Years old Peat Smoked Single Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Peat smoke has long drifted through our history. In years gone by, peat featured heavily in distilling traditions and our records show The Glenturret using peat dug from Octhertye Estate in the early 1800s. We are in the rare position of producing peated and non-peated spirit at different times of the year from the same small stills. This has allowed us to craft this mellow, sweet, smoky 10 years old single malt that shows its character off to the full at a strength of 50%.

name: Prakaan  Single Malt Whisky Tribura Series Select Cask (700 ml)
desc_en_short:
SELECT CASK

Aged in Ex-Bourbon Oak Cask

Kamphaeng Phet is one of the most ancient cities of Thailand, a prestigious connecting point in the prosperous era, famous for its impressive fortified walls. For centuries these walls have stood strong in defense of the Kingdom of Thailand. Honoring the social and cultural significance of this proud history, PRAKAAN single malt whisky pays respectful tribute to this iconic city with its venerable walls and keeps the legacy alive.

PRAKAAN single malt whisky was born in Kamphaeng Phet amidst unspoiled natural abundance where pure spring water is slowly filtered into underground reservoirs; an ideal environment for making fine quality whisky.

Our whisky is crafted by our proud craftsmen and women who combine their passion about nature and centuries old whisky making methods. PRAKAAN single malt whisky is expertly distilled and matured with great pride in the Kingdom of Thailand.
desc_en_full:
Tasting Notes

NOSE :

Soft tropical fruit and zesty citrus defined beautifully against lush honey sweetness, creamy vanilla, and comforting undertones of lemon cake.

PALATE :

Round sweetness of caramelized pineapple slices and chewy toffee, complemented with the rich perfume of freshly-ground spices and hints of candied lemon peel.

FINISH :

Mature and long-lasting with ripe fruitiness.

name: Johnnie Walker Blue Label Festive VAP 2025 – Decanter Edition (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Johnnie Walker Blue Label Festive VAP 2025 – Decanter Edition represents a special celebration of craftsmanship, rarity, and timeless elegance. This limited-release expression for 2025 brings together exceptional Scotch whisky with the refined beauty of a bespoke decanter—created to elevate both the drinking ritual and the visual experience.
desc_en_full: Johnnie Walker Blue Label Festive VAP 2025 – Decanter Edition is a special celebration that unites the mastery and artistry of world-class whisky blending in one remarkable release. This limited edition for 2025 is crafted as a piece of bar-top art, presented in a refined, crystal-clear decanter that reflects the elegance and precision synonymous with Johnnie Walker Scotch. Story & Inspiration This exclusive edition begins with the selection of exceptionally rare whiskies—drawn from only 1% of the casks resting in Johnnie Walker’s reserves. Many originate from legendary ghost distilleries such as Brora, Port Dundas, and other historic closed sites, lending remarkable depth, grace, and silky richness that define the iconic Blue Label character. Presenting the 2025 release in a bespoke decanter further elevates the experience, offering a new tier of sophistication beyond the classic bottle. Aroma & Taste The nose opens with notes of chestnut honey, delicate peat smoke, and ripe stone fruits like peach. The mid-palate reveals layers of dark chocolate, aromatic vanilla, and smooth creamy malt. It finishes with the signature Blue Label warmth—long, elegant, and gently smoky—with fine threads of sweetness woven throughout. The Beauty of the Decanter Edition The decanter has been meticulously designed exclusively for this release. Thick, polished crystal showcases the whisky’s amber-gold hue with exceptional clarity, while the engraved detailing symbolizes Johnnie Walker’s enduring journey forward. It is both a display piece and a companion for refined whisky moments. How to Enjoy – LIQ9 Style Best enjoyed neat or on the rock to allow the full expression of its layered aromatics and refined structure as intended by the Master Blender. The ideal serving temperature is 17–20°C, enabling the floral tones, gentle spice, and soft smoke to open beautifully. Perfect for Special Occasions This edition is an exceptional choice for collectors, whisky enthusiasts seeking a meaningful yearly release, or anyone looking to elevate festive evenings with a spirit that is smooth, deep, and memorable in every glass.

name: Johnnie Walker Blue Label Chinese New Year Of Horse (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Johnnie Walker Blue Label Chinese New Year – Year of the Horse celebrates strength, elegance, and forward momentum through a beautifully crafted limited-edition design. Featuring exceptionally rare whiskies from only 1% of Johnnie Walker’s reserves, it offers a silky, smoky, and deeply refined flavor—perfect for meaningful New Year celebrations and collectors.
desc_en_full:
Our Signature Craft

The Johnnie Walker Blue Label Lunar New Year Limited Edition Year of the Horse bottle has been created by one of the most influential fashion designers in the world, Robert Wun. It houses Blue Label Blended Scotch Whisky: a velvety smooth and vibrant Scotch Blended Whisky made with layers of incredible flavour, including fruit, spice and a long, luxurious smokiness. Give the gift of depth this Lunar New Year.

The Whisky

As with all Blue Label releases, this edition is crafted from exceptionally rare whiskies selected from less than 1% of the casks in Johnnie Walker’s reserves. Some of these originate from renowned “ghost distilleries,” adding layers of depth, rarity, and smooth complexity. The result is a whisky with remarkable balance and luxurious character.

Aroma & Taste

The nose opens with honeyed sweetness, dried fruits, and a gentle thread of clean peat smoke. The palate is rich and velvety—dark chocolate, spiced vanilla, roasted nuts, and silky malt weave together beautifully. The finish is long, warm, and elegantly smoky, featuring the unmistakable signature of Blue Label.

Design Highlights

The bottle’s intricate illustrations and gold detailing make it a true centerpiece for collectors. The flowing form of the horse, captured mid-stride, mirrors Johnnie Walker’s timeless philosophy of progress. Whether displayed on a bar shelf or gifted during New Year celebrations, it stands as a symbol of refinement and good fortune.

How to Enjoy

Savor it neat or with a single large ice cube to allow the nuanced layers to unfold gradually. For the best aromatic expression, serve at 17–20°C so the floral notes, delicate spice, and soft smoke can open gracefully.

Taylor’s Fine Tawny is drawn from wines selected for their smoothness and mellow character. These are aged for up to three years in oak casks in Taylor’s cellars in Vila Nova de Gaia. The individual wines are then blended together a few months before bottling for balance and completeness.

grape_variety: Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz
desc_en_short:
Taylor’s Fine Ruby a blend of full bodied Port wines aged for around two years in large oak vats in Taylor’s cellars, or ‘lodges’, in Vila Nova de Gaia.  

As they age, they gain smoothness and elegance while retaining their fruitiness, intensity and youthful ruby colour.  They are then blended together for balance and consistency. With its firm tannins and concentrated fruitiness, Fine Ruby is perfect for the classic port-and-cheese combination.

It is particularly good with blue-veined and richly flavoured soft cheeses.  It also pairs well with berry fruit and dark chocolate flavours and, like all red Ports, makes an excellent after dinner drink.

name: Banrock Station  Shiraz
desc_en_short: Banrock Station Shiraz from South Australia's Riverland delivers ripe plum and raspberry fruit layered with black pepper, aniseed spice, and a touch of vanilla oak — an approachable, medium-bodied red with genuine everyday charm and an environmental conscience behind every bottle.
desc_en_full:
Banrock Station has been a fixture on the banks of the Murray River in South Australia's Riverland since the 1960s, farming vines on fertile alluvial soils alongside one of the country's most significant wetland reserves. The estate's long-standing partnership with Landcare Australia means that every bottle sold contributes to the planting of native trees and shrubs across the continent — conservation and quality side by side.

This Shiraz is a textbook expression of warm-climate South Australian fruit: the glass opens with a deep ruby colour carrying vivid crimson edges, and the nose follows with generous red fruit — ripe plum and raspberry at the fore — lifted by subtle vanilla and mocha from careful oak treatment. On the palate, the medium body carries those fruit flavours with easy confidence, while black pepper and aniseed spice add the distinctive peppery signature that Australian Shiraz does so well. A thread of dried herb and savoury earthiness keeps the finish honest and food-friendly.

Tannins are soft and integrated, making this an effortless pour on any weeknight. It pairs naturally with a char-grilled rib-eye or slow-roasted lamb shoulder, but handles a hearty beef burger or lamb kebab just as comfortably. Serve slightly cool at around 16–17 °C for the best expression of its fruit and spice.

name: Hardys  Varietal Range Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Deep ruby (รูบี้เข้ม)
desc_en_short: Hardys Varietal Range Cabernet Sauvignon delivers classic Australian richness in a bold, approachable style — dark berry fruit, a hint of oak, and firm but friendly tannins that make it an everyday crowd-pleaser equally at home on a weeknight table or around a weekend barbecue.
desc_en_full:
Thomas Hardy founded his South Australian winery in 1853, and today Hardys stands as one of Australia's most recognised wine names — a producer with the scale to source exceptional Cabernet Sauvignon fruit right across the vast South Eastern Australia zone, blending regions to achieve consistency and character in every vintage.

The Varietal Range sits at the heart of the Hardys portfolio: honest, variety-driven wines built for everyday enjoyment without compromise. The Cabernet Sauvignon is crafted from carefully selected fruit, fermented and then matured in oak to build depth and structure while keeping the wine approachable from the moment the cork is pulled.

In the glass it shows a deep ruby hue. The nose opens with confident aromas of blackcurrant and dark plum, framed by subtle cedar and vanilla from oak maturation. On the palate the wine is full-bodied with well-integrated medium tannins, flavours of blackberry and a touch of dark chocolate, finishing with a clean, satisfying length.

This is a textbook crowd-pleasing Cabernet that earns its place at casual weeknight dinners and weekend gatherings alike. Pour it alongside a char-grilled rib-eye, slow-cooked beef brisket, a charcuterie board of cured meats, or let it shine at the barbecue next to lamb chops and smoky sausages. Serve at around 17–18 °C for the best expression of its fruit-forward character.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Deep ruby (รูบี้เข้ม)
desc_en_short: Robert Mondavi Private Selection Cabernet Sauvignon delivers classic California character with ripe blackberry and plum fruit, a touch of warming spice, and oak-aged depth — an approachable, full-bodied red that punches well above its everyday price point.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry more weight in the story of California wine than Robert Mondavi. A true pioneer of the Napa Valley and beyond, Mondavi spent decades proving that the Golden State could produce world-class wines, and that legacy lives on across every tier of the portfolio — including the widely loved Private Selection range, which draws fruit from premium vineyards across coastal and inland California.

This 100% Cabernet Sauvignon is a textbook example of what makes California Cab so universally appealing. The nose opens with generous aromas of blackberry and ripe plum, layered with a gentle lift of warming spice — think black pepper and a whisper of vanilla — courtesy of careful oak barrel ageing. On the palate it delivers a full, plush body with polished medium tannins and a fruit-forward finish that lingers satisfyingly long.

The wine is ready to enjoy the moment the bottle is opened, making it an effortless weeknight choice, yet it has enough structure to reward a few years of patient cellaring if desired.

At the table, pour this alongside a grilled ribeye or slow-braised beef short ribs for a classic match. It handles bold barbecue flavours with ease, stands up to aged hard cheeses, and works beautifully with a hearty Bolognese or cured charcuterie board — a dependable crowd-pleaser for casual dinners and relaxed entertaining alike.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Merlot
desc_en_short: A plush, fruit-forward California Merlot from Robert Mondavi's Private Selection range, drawing on Central Coast grapes to deliver ripe dark cherry, plum jam, and mocha notes over silky, well-rounded tannins — an everyday red that punches well above its price.
desc_en_full:
Robert Mondavi's Private Selection label has long served as the approachable face of one of California's most storied wine dynasties, sourcing fruit from carefully chosen coastal appellations to deliver consistent quality at an accessible price point. This Merlot-led blend sources primarily from Monterey County on the Central Coast, where temperate Pacific-influenced temperatures and moist alluvial soils allow Merlot to ripen slowly and evenly, building depth without losing freshness. Small additions of Syrah and Petite Sirah add backbone and a subtle dark-fruited intensity to the blend.

In the winery, a gentle crush and destem is followed by temperature-controlled fermentation, then an extended 12-day post-fermentation maceration to coax out soft, rounded tannins. Malolactic fermentation adds a creamy, supple texture, while ten months of ageing in a combination of French and American oak layers in just enough toasty richness without overwhelming the wine's vivid fruit character.

In the glass, expect an expressive nose of dark cherry, ripe plum, and milk chocolate, with a hint of roasted coffee. The palate echoes with cherry pie, plum jam, and mocha, all framed by a long, polished finish. Pour this alongside a slow-cooked lamb shoulder, a hearty spaghetti bolognese, or a weekend barbecue spread — it is equally at home with a wild mushroom risotto or a well-seasoned charcuterie board.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Classic varietal aromas and flavors in our Pinot Noir grapes, this vivacious red wine’s hallmark is fresh, vibrant fruit. On the nose, lovely rose petal, strawberry, and sour cherry aromas mingle with complementary scents of smoky oak. Soft and round with good length and richness on the palate, enjoy this delectable Pinot Noir with grilled salmon, herb-roasted chicken, baked ham, or lighter pork dishes.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING :

The grapes were destemmed and lightly crushed into open-top fermenters. The juice cold-soaked for 48 hours before it was warmed up to initiate a native yeast fermentation that completed in 8–10 days.During the fermentation, we punched down the cap of skins and seeds to achieve a gentle extraction of tannins and a softer mouthfeel. After the wine was pressed off the skins, it was transferred to French/American oak to complete its malolactic fermentation. During barrel aging, the wine was not racked until it was ready for bottling. This regimen added roundness and complexity to our Pinot Noir's vibrant fruit flavors.

name: Robert Mondavi   Woodbridge Cabernet Sauvignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 13% Syrah, 5% Petite Sirah, 2% Petit Verdot,1% Merlot And 3% Other Complementary Varieties.
desc_en_short: Robert Mondavi Woodbridge Cabernet Sauvignon delivers generous aromas of plum and cedar with deep blackberry and tobacco flavors, rounded out by smooth oak-aged tannins — an approachable, food-friendly California red built for everyday enjoyment.
desc_en_full:
Robert Mondavi is one of California's most storied names in wine, and his legacy traces back to 1966 when he established his namesake winery at the legendary To Kalon Vineyard in Oakville — a first-growth site in the heart of Napa Valley recognized worldwide for producing benchmark Cabernet Sauvignon. The Woodbridge label extends that heritage into an accessible, everyday expression crafted for generous drinking without compromise.

This non-vintage California red is a thoughtfully blended wine led by 76% Cabernet Sauvignon, with Syrah, Petite Sirah, Petit Verdot, and Merlot rounding out the blend. Each variety contributes depth and complexity — the Syrah lends dark fruit and spice, Petite Sirah adds inky structure, and Petit Verdot provides a floral, tannic backbone. The result is a full-bodied red with medium acidity and soft, approachable tannins shaped by careful oak aging.

On the nose, ripe plum mingles with fresh herb and warm cedar in an inviting, aromatic opening. The palate follows with concentrated flavors of blackberry and tobacco, while the oak influence brings a pleasing toasty, caramelized quality that carries through to a smooth, lingering finish.

Woodbridge Cabernet Sauvignon is effortlessly versatile at the table. Reach for it alongside grilled lamb chops or slow-cooked barbecued ribs, where its dark fruit and spice will echo the char and smokiness beautifully. It's equally at home with a rich, spicy Bolognese pasta or a charcuterie board of cured meats — a reliable, crowd-pleasing red for weeknight dinners and casual gatherings alike.

name: Antinori  Chianti Classico "Peppoli" DOC
wine_color: Ruby red in color with purple highlights, the wine shows intensely fruity notes of cherries and red currants
desc_en_short: Peppoli is one of the most celebrated Chianti Classico estates, now managed by the Antinori family. This Sangiovese-dominant blend delivers the hallmark character of the appellation — sour cherry, dried herbs, and earthy depth — with an open, generous texture.
desc_en_full:
Peppoli is an historic estate in the Chianti Classico zone, acquired by the Antinori family and managed with the same dedication to quality that defines the entire Antinori portfolio. The vineyard sits at altitude in the hills between Florence and Siena, where the combination of galestro and alberese soils and cool nights preserves the elegance and freshness that Chianti Classico is celebrated for.

Peppoli Chianti Classico is a Sangiovese-dominant blend, complemented by a small percentage of Merlot and Syrah, aged in both barriques and large oak casks. The result is a wine of genuine charm: ruby-garnet in colour, with a nose of fresh cherry, dried violets, wild herbs, and a hint of tobacco. The palate is medium-bodied with vivid acidity, smooth tannins, and a clean, savoury finish.

A versatile food wine, equally at home with pizza, pasta al ragù, grilled meats, charcuterie, and aged Italian cheeses.

name: "Tormaresca" By Antinori  Torcicoda Salento IGT
desc_en_short: Torcicoda is Tormaresca's flagship Primitivo — a rich, full-bodied southern Italian red bursting with dark plum, blackberry, chocolate, and warm spice, crafted from old-vine Primitivo grown in the sun-baked soils of the Salento peninsula.
desc_en_full:
Torcicoda takes its name from the local word for the Primitivo grape — literally 'twisted tail', referencing the grape's distinctive bunch formation. Sourced from vineyards on the Salento peninsula in the deep south of Puglia, where Primitivo thrives in the intense heat and calcareous clay soils, this is one of Tormaresca's most acclaimed wines.

The grapes are harvested at full ripeness and fermented before ageing in French oak barriques. The result is a wine of considerable depth and warmth: deep ruby-purple, with a rich nose of blackberry, dark plum, dried fig, cocoa, cinnamon, and a subtle leather undercurrent. On the palate, it is full-bodied, velvety, and generous, with smooth, ripe tannins and a long, spiced finish.

Perfect alongside grilled lamb chops, beef short rib, aged hard cheeses, slow-cooked game, and hearty pasta dishes with meat-based sauces.

name: Antinori  Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
wine_color: An intense ruby red in color.
desc_en_short: Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva is one of the historic wines from Tuscany's most famous dynasty — a concentrated, structured Sangiovese from the heartland of Chianti Classico, aged to develop complexity while maintaining the grape's vivid cherry-fruit character.
desc_en_full:
The Antinori family has been making wine in Tuscany for over 600 years, and Villa Antinori Chianti Classico Riserva represents one of their most traditional expressions. Sourced from estate vineyards in the Chianti Classico zone, this Riserva is dominated by Sangiovese with a small complement of Cabernet Sauvignon, aged in French and Hungarian oak barriques before extended bottle maturation.

Deep ruby in the glass, it offers an expressive nose of morello cherry, plum, dried rose petal, and Mediterranean scrubland, layered with cedar, tobacco, and a hint of leather. On the palate, it is structured and generous — full-bodied with Sangiovese's characteristic high acidity and firm but refined tannins, leading to a long, warming finish.

Classic accompaniments include bistecca alla Fiorentina, Chianina beef, wild boar ragu, aged Pecorino Toscano, and truffle-based dishes.

name: Bodegas Salentein  Portillo Malbec
wine_color: Ruby-Red Wine With Very Intense Purplish Tints
desc_en_short: Portillo Malbec from Bodegas Salentein delivers classic Mendoza fruit — ripe red and black berries laced with warming spice — wrapped in soft, silky tannins and a generous, crowd-pleasing finish that punches well above its entry-level price point.
desc_en_full:
Bodegas Salentein is one of Mendoza's most respected names, a Dutch-owned estate that transformed the high-altitude Uco Valley into a benchmark for Argentine fine wine. The Portillo label sits at the approachable end of their portfolio, but it is no afterthought — it draws on the same winemaking philosophy and Andean terroir that defines the house, delivering genuine Malbec character at an everyday price.

This 100% Malbec pours a deep, intense ruby that signals the variety's characteristic concentration. On the nose, aromas of ripe red cherry, dark plum, and blackberry mingle with an intriguing thread of spice — a hallmark of Mendoza Malbec grown with the cooling influence of altitude. The palate follows through with a generous burst of fruit, while the tannins remain notably soft and silky for a full-bodied red, making it immediately approachable without requiring any cellaring.

Recognised with 86 points from Wine Advocate, Portillo Malbec is a reliably satisfying everyday pour. It shines alongside grilled beef and lamb — the natural pairing for Argentine Malbec — and holds its own at a weekend barbecue. Beyond the classics, its fruit-forward profile works equally well with slow-roasted pork, hearty pasta dishes in light tomato or meat sauces, and semi-hard cheeses like manchego or aged gouda. Keep a bottle or two on hand for any occasion that calls for a rich, approachable red.

name: Errazuriz  Estate Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: A classic Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon from Errazuriz, sourcing grapes across the prestigious Maipo and Aconcagua valleys to deliver ripe cherry fruit, tobacco spice, and a well-structured palate with satisfying juiciness and smooth, medium tannins.
desc_en_full:
Errazuriz is one of Chile's most storied wine producers, with roots stretching back to 1870 in the Aconcagua Valley. The Estate Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon draws on fruit from two of the country's most celebrated growing regions — the Valle de Maipo and the Valle de Aconcagua — blending the best of both terroirs into a reliably satisfying, everyday-luxury red.

In Maipo, cool mountain breezes descend each morning from the Andes, moderating daytime temperatures and allowing the Cabernet Sauvignon to ripen gradually while retaining natural freshness. The stony, alluvial soils provide superb drainage and concentrate flavour without excess vigour. Aconcagua contributes its own intensity through dramatic day-to-night temperature swings of up to 20°C in the weeks before harvest, driving deep colour and anthocyanin concentration that gives the wine its vivid ruby hue.

In the glass, expect a classic ruby-red with aromas of tart cherry and ripe strawberry layered with tobacco leaf, cracked black pepper, and a gentle toasted oak note. On the palate it is well-structured yet approachable — medium tannins and lively acidity frame a juicy, fruit-forward core that finishes cleanly and satisfyingly.

This is a natural partner for a weekend asado, whether that means a grilled ribeye, slow-smoked brisket, or a platter of cured charcuterie. Equally at home poured generously at a casual dinner or taken as a reliable house red for any beef-centric occasion.

name: Marques De Riscal  Reserva, DOC Rioja
desc_en_short: One of Rioja's most storied names, Marqués de Riscal Reserva blends Tempranillo with Graciano and Mazuelo for a classically structured red brimming with dark berry fruit, balsamic spice, and the signature elegance of long oak aging.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Spanish wine as Marqués de Riscal. Founded in Elciego in 1858, the estate is credited with introducing Bordeaux-inspired winemaking methods to Rioja, and its Reserva remains one of the benchmarks against which all Riojan reds are measured. Sourced from estate vineyards in the Rioja Alavesa subzone, the blend is led by Tempranillo — the backbone of great Rioja — with Graciano and Mazuelo adding aromatic intensity and freshness. After fermentation, the wine ages in American and French oak barrels for a minimum of 24 months before further bottle refinement, earning its Reserva classification under DOC Rioja rules.

In the glass it pours a deep cherry-red with a vivid, youthful robe. The nose is immediately complex: ripe plum and blackberry meld with balsamic lift, dried herbs, and a gentle thread of toasted vanilla from the oak. On the palate, the wine is full-bodied and generously structured, with polished, well-integrated tannins and a lively acidity that keeps everything in balance. The finish is long, clean, and satisfying, trailing off with a refined whisper of fine oak.

This is a wine built for the table. It shines alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb, chargrilled beef sirloin, or a platter of aged Manchego and Ibérico ham — classics of the Spanish table that mirror its spice and depth. Decant for 30 minutes to let the full complexity unfold.

name: Donnafugata  Sedàra Sicilia
style: Nero D’avola (The Primary Grape) Along With Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah And Other Grapes
grape_variety: Nero d'Avola, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Syrah
desc_en_short: Donnafugata's Sedàra is a sun-drenched Sicilian red led by Nero d'Avola, offering a vibrant bouquet of cherry and plum with a whisper of black pepper — a soft, well-structured everyday wine that pairs beautifully across the table.
desc_en_full:
Donnafugata, the celebrated Sicilian estate whose name evokes the island's wild, windswept beauty, crafted Sedàra as their approachable yet character-driven entry into the world of Sicilian red wine. Rooted in the sun-baked soils of western Sicily, this blend is anchored by Nero d'Avola — the island's noble indigenous grape — given depth and dimension through the addition of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Syrah. The result is a wine that captures the warmth and generosity of Sicily without sacrificing freshness or finesse.

In the glass, Sedàra pours a vivid ruby red, immediately signalling its vibrant, fruit-forward character. The nose opens with inviting aromas of ripe cherry and dark plum, lifted by a gentle, enticing whisper of black pepper — a hallmark of Nero d'Avola's spice-laced personality. On the palate, the wine delivers good structure through suave, polished tannins and a pleasing streak of natural acidity that keeps everything lively and refreshing through to the finish.

Sedàra's charm lies in its versatility. This is a wine made for the table — equally at home alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder, a plate of pasta al ragù, a grilled ribeye, or a generous board of soft cheeses. Its medium tannins and balanced body make it an excellent midweek companion, but its genuine Sicilian character means it holds its own at more convivial gatherings too. Serve slightly cool at around 16–17 °C for best results.

name: Marques De Riscal  Reserva Rioja DOC (375 ml)
desc_en_short: A historic Rioja Reserva from Marqués de Riscal, blending 90% Tempranillo with Graciano and Mazuelo for a full-bodied red layered with dark berries, balsamic spice, and elegantly rounded tannins — classic Spanish craftsmanship in a convenient half-bottle format.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in Spanish wine as Marqués de Riscal. Founded in 1858 in the town of Elciego in the heart of Rioja Alta, the estate is widely credited with introducing modern winemaking techniques — including Bordeaux-trained expertise and oak-ageing — to the region. Today, the winery remains a benchmark for what a classic Rioja Reserva should be, with the Reserva tier representing the ideal balance of fruit purity and structured oak influence.

Crafted predominantly from Tempranillo (90%), with Graciano and Mazuelo adding aromatic lift and structural backbone, this wine is aged in American and French oak to meet Rioja's strict Reserva requirements. The result is a beautifully layered red that speaks confidently of its Rioja DOC origins.

In the glass, it pours a deep cherry-red with a vivid, full robe. The nose is generous and complex — ripe dark berries, balsamic herb, and warm spice woven together with subtle toasted oak. On the palate, full-bodied and well-structured, the tannins are polished and round, carrying the wine through to a long, fresh finish with a lingering whisper of fine oak.

This half-bottle (375 ml) is an ideal companion for a solo dinner or a paired tasting. Serve with slow-roasted lamb, grilled beef, cured Ibérico meats, or aged Manchego. A timeless expression of Rioja, available in a perfectly sized format.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Graves Rouge
desc_en_short: The Graves Grands Terroirs is characterised by elegance and finesse. The wine boasts deep, dark appearance and a delicate red-fruit bouquet. On the palate, it is full bodied and well balanced, with a nice long aromatic finish.
desc_en_full: The Graves terroir, to the south of Bordeaux, is similar to that of the Medoc (made up gravely ridges exposed to the sun), with, however, the presence of sand and clay mixed in alternately with the gravel. These round pebbles, deposited by the Garonne river over the centuries, have an important role to play in the temperature control of the grapes : throughout the night they give off the heat accumulated during the day. The Grands Terroirs Graves is the result of a particularly strict selection procedure carried out by the Dourthe oenologists. From among hundreds of batches, only the most interesting wines are selected, those with a well-balanced structure and a personality typical of its appellation. The Graves Grands Terroirs is characterised by elegance and finesse.

name: Crane Lake  Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: A soft, approachable California Pinot Noir from the Franzia family's Crane Lake label, offering aromas of raspberry and violet with flavours of cherry and plum that glide to a velvety, easy-drinking finish.
desc_en_full:
Crane Lake is the value-focused label from one of California's most storied winemaking dynasties — the Franzia family, whose roots in California viticulture stretch back more than a century. Their philosophy is straightforward: quality wine should not require a premium price tag, and Crane Lake Pinot Noir delivers on that promise with every pour.

Sourced from California's broad, sun-warmed growing regions, this Pinot Noir is crafted for immediate enjoyment. The ruby-red colour is bright and inviting, hinting at the fruit-driven character within. On the nose, lifted aromas of fresh raspberry and violet set a floral, elegant tone that is quintessentially Pinot Noir in style. The palate follows through with ripe cherry and plum, while restrained oak adds just a whisper of structure before the wine settles into its signature soft, velvety finish. High natural acidity keeps the mouthfeel lively and the finish clean.

The wine's low tannins and medium body make it one of the most versatile reds on the shelf. Serve it slightly chilled at around 14–16 °C alongside a wedge of Brie or Camembert, a plate of smoked salmon blinis, or a simple roast leg of lamb. It is equally at home next to roast duck, grilled swordfish, or a slow-cooked beef dish. An honest, crowd-pleasing Pinot Noir that punches well above its price point.

name: Caymus  Conundrum Red
desc_en_short: Conundrum' s original began at the dinner table. Charlie Wagner Sr., who co-founded Caymus Vineyards in 1972 with his wife, Lorna, and son, Chuck, would mix wines to find the perfect glass to pair with his meal. No one blended wines back then, so his experiment was pretty radical. They source Conundrum in places that are both famed and off the beaten track – in Napa, Monterey, Santa Barbara, San Benito, Solano and Tulare Counties.
desc_en_full:
Intensely colored and fragrant, the wine opens up with evocative aromas of blackberry, raspberry, rhubarb and cola. On the palate, the wine is full bodied and exuberantly fruity, pairing black cherry, plum and pomegranate notes with more soulful cinnamon, lavender and cocoa nuances. The tannins are firm yet supple, and the finish is long with enticing notes of brown spice, bramble fruits, vanilla and toasty oak.

This wine tastes great with many dishes; best with highly flavored foods, especially spicy tomato based sauces, dark spices or meats enhanced with BBQ sauce….and really…anything that will fit on your grill. Serve slightly to well-chilled. -Winemaker, Charlie Wagner II

wine_color: Deep ruby red wiith purple tones.
desc_en_short: Deep ruby red wiith purple tones, generous aromas of ripe blackberry, plum, green olives, with touches of vanilla and sweet smoke from the barrel ageing.With a round and juicy feeling in mouth,this wines shows tannins that give it a charming grip in the mouth. desc_en_full:
Montgras Antu Ninquen

ANTU - "Sun" in the Mapuche native language-, is MontGras' new exploratory range of wines.

With the eagerness and curiosity to explore new places and grape varieties, ANTU represents the adventurous spirit of our Winemakers. In these wines, the identity and character of a very special terroirs, along with a signature winemaking style, is transferred to each bottle.

Our detailed vineyard mapping has enabled us to carefully select specific vineyard Blocks and lots within them, that give us limited yields of world-class grapes.

We carry out segmented harvests, hand-picking grapes at their optimum moment of ripeness to create wines that are full bodied, robust with vibrant fruit.

name: Pio Cesare  Barolo DOCG
desc_en_short: A classic style Barolo. Excellent structure, harmony, elegance. Soft tannins and balanced fruit. Approachable, but with a very long ageing potential. Barolo is such a great wine which should not be described as a “basic” or “regular” Barolo, simply because it does not have any additional indication on the label.
desc_en_full:
Pio Cesare Barolo DOCG

96 points in Wine Enthusiast - "Enticingly fragrant, this wine offers alluring scents of iris, rose, perfumed berry and wild herb. It’s full bodied and elegant, delivering crushed raspberry, juicy Marasca cherry, star anise and orange zest set against a backbone of taut, fine-grained tannins. Bright acidity keeps it balanced and lends intensity. Drink 2024– 2036. Editors' Choice"

95 points in The Wine Advocate - "Traditionalists happily fall back to this wine from Pio Cesare. This historic winery, now in its fifth generation and located within the city limits of Alba, takes care to shape a classic interpretation without too many extra bells or whistles. The fact that Pio Cesare now has the flashier Barolo Ornato and the newly created Barolo Mosconi, only gives more importance to this wine. The Pio Cesare 2016 Barolo offers deep, dark fruit with a mingling of spice, smoke, tar and smoke. This wine represents a blend of fruit from five comuni, or villages: Cascina Ornato, La Serra and Briccolina in Serralunga d'Alba; Gustava and Garretti in Grinzane Cavour; Roncaglie in La Morra; Ravera in Novello; and starting with the 2015 vintage, Mosconi in Monforte d’Alba."

95 points in James Suckling - "Pretty sweetness and ripeness to this with cherries, dried strawberries and chocolate. It’s full-bodied with round, firm tannins. Very long and intense. A little tight now. Complex and subtle at this stage. Give it two or three years to come around. Try after 2022."

91 points in Wine Spectator - "Lean and taut, showing macerated cherry, eucalyptus and iron flavors. It's ripe and the tannins are well-integrated. Just a bit shy on the finish today, but a year or two in the bottle should take care of that. Best from 2022 through 2036."

name: Montes  Alpha Syrah
wine_color: Deep ruby-red with a purplish tone.
desc_en_short: 92 points in Wine Enthusiast - "Deep purple in color, this cool-vintage Syrah excels via smooth cherry, cassis, cedar and charcoal aromas. A full-bodied palate is balanced by firm acidity, while plum, berry, cassis and chocolate flavors finish in harmony. Drink through 2020."
desc_en_full:
Montes Alpha Syrah

From our La Finca estate, in the Apalta Valley, the 1999 vintage was our first Syrah. This is the scarcest of our Alpha wines. Yields are very low at only 6 tonnes per hectare. The wine is aged in French oak barrels for one year and has only one soft filtering. In the manner of Rhone producers, Aurelio adds a small percentage of Cabernet Sauvignon, also originating from Apalta, and some Viognier, to the final mixture. Of intense ruby red color, it has a great aroma with floral, tobacco and leather notes. Strong and full-bodied on the palate, with soft and mature tannins. A very long, elegant and satisfying aftertaste. A superb Syrah.

name: Jacob's Creek  Reserve Shiraz (750 ml)
desc_en_short: This premium, full bodied wine is an outstanding example os South Australian Shiraz, displaying an intensity of ripe plum with pepper spice, balanced by soft grape and oak tannins, leading to a lingering smooth finish. An ideal accompaniment to most red meats, game and cheeses. A wine that will develop further with cellaring.
desc_en_full:
This premium, full bodied wine is an outstanding example os South Australian Shiraz, displaying an intensity of ripe plum with pepper spice, balanced by soft grape and oak tannins, leading to a lingering smooth finish. An ideal accompaniment to most red meats, game and cheeses. A wine that will develop further with cellaring.

THE STORY OF JACOB CREEK

Jacob Creek from Australia In 1847, along with the banks of Jacob's Creek, Johann Gramp planted his first vineyard. His vision was to create great tasting wines that reflect the true character of the grapes and the land. To this day we live and breathe this vision by producing wines that showcase how each grape variety expresses itself when shaped by the soil and the sun of Australia.

Awarded with 3 gold medals from international wine and spirits competition. Such as, 2014 WSA Wine Challenge, 2013 China Wine & Spirits Awards and 2014 Pacific Rim International Wine Compettition.

WINE MAKING PROCESS OF JACOB CREEK RESERVE

1 ) THE FIRST TASTE, ON THE VINES

Our viticulturists and winemakers visit every the vineyard to taste the fruit and determine the optimal time to start harvesting.

2 ) THE HARVEST

Grapes are picked. Most are picked with a harvester to minimise the time it takes to get the grapes to the winery to best retain freshness of the fruit. Some are picked by hand during the day, such as the old delicate vines or where the site is too steep.

3 ) THE TRANSFER

Time is critical. We fill the grape bins in the vineyard and take the grapes by truck to our winery as soon as possible.

4 ) THE DE-STEMMER

The de-stemmer gently removes the stalks from the grapes

5 ) THE FERMENT

Reds are fermented on skins, as it's the skins that contribute most of the colour and tannin to the wine. Yeast is added and fermentation takes place under the careful watch of our winemakers. Individual parcels are fermented separately, so the timing varies. Typically, a parcel destined for our Reserve Shiraz spends around 10-12 days fermenting before it is ready to be pressed.

As the juice ferments, bubbles rise to the surface and carry grape skins with them. They can form a 'cap' which needs to be broken up using processes such as 'pumping over' (pumping the wine from the bottom to the top) and occasionally 'pushing down the caps' (with a plunger). This also maximises the extraction of colour and tannins.

6 ) THE PRESS

After primary fermentation, red grapes are then pressed to separate out the wine from the skins and pulp.

7 ) MALOLACTIC FERMENTATION

All of our red wines undergo malolactic fermentation, a natural process where malic acid is converted to softer lactic acid. Some parcels of our premium red wines are selected for barrel maturation to add oak nuances and texture, and to soften tannins.

8 ) OAK MATURATION

Depending on the desired wine style, the wine is left to mature on oak for a period of 6-24 months. Aromatic-styles, such as Pinot Noir with its inherently softer tannins spends less time on oak, while more assertive styles such as, young Cabernet Sauvignon spends more time on oak.

9 ) FILTRATION AND BOTTLING

Through filtration, the wine takes its final, clear and bright form. This is done with the lightest touch possible to deliver you a wine that showcases the character of our regions.

We bottle our reds mostly in screw cap for preservation of quality, and a small quantity also in traditional cork for the few countries which prefer it.

10 ) BOTTLE MATURATION

After bottling, some premium reds are left for a further period, to mature perfectly before being released for sale. The timing is determined by the winemaker who considers when the flavours have become well-integrated and the wine has entered the start of its ideal drinking window.

With the premium nature of our Reserve range, it is well suited to medium-term bottle maturation. With a few years of careful cellaring, the wine can evolve, during which the overt fruit flavours will become subdued and more savoury complexity will develop.

THE STORY OF CAMPO VIEJO

name: La Linda  Malbec
desc_en_short: La Linda Malbec from Mendoza, Argentina, delivers classic varietal character with bright cherry and blackberry fruit, delicate violet florals, and a silky full-bodied palate shaped by three months of oak ageing — approachable, elegant, and built for the table.
desc_en_full:
Luigi Bosca's entry-level label La Linda has earned a devoted following across South America and beyond by doing something deceptively simple: bottling honest, expressive Malbec from Mendoza's sun-drenched vineyards at a price that invites everyday enjoyment. The La Linda range sits under the Luigi Bosca umbrella — one of Argentina's most storied family wineries, farming in Mendoza since 1901 — and channels the house's winemaking discipline into a thoroughly modern, crowd-pleasing style.

In the glass, expect a vivid violet-red hue that signals the grape's inky depth. The nose opens with ripe cherries and blackberries lifted by a characteristic whiff of violet and a gentle spice note. Winemaking is straightforward and precise: hand-harvested clusters are de-stemmed and fermented in temperature-controlled stainless steel at 28 °C, then transferred to oak barrels for three months of ageing — just enough time to smooth the tannins and add a subtle toasty backbone without overshadowing the fruit.

On the palate, La Linda Malbec is plush and generous, with silky texture and ripe, well-integrated tannins. The finish is clean and fruit-forward, making it one of the most versatile reds in its category.

Pour it alongside a wood-fired Argentine asado, a slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a board of aged hard cheeses. It also holds its own against robust barbecue and grilled game. Serve at 16–18 °C to let those floral and spice notes fully express themselves.
