name: Luigi Bosca  Malbec
wine_color: A deep red color with violet hints.
desc_en_short: Luigi Bosca Malbec from Mendoza delivers a lush, full-bodied expression of Argentina's signature grape — deep ruby with violet hints, bursting with ripe plum, warming spice, and coffee notes from careful oak ageing, finishing with elegance and impressive length.
desc_en_full:
Luigi Bosca is one of Mendoza's most storied wine dynasties, founded in 1901 by Italian immigrant Luigi Bosca and now entering its fourth generation of family winemaking. Rooted in the high-altitude vineyards of Mendoza — where intense Andean sunshine, cool nights, and well-drained soils push Malbec to its most expressive heights — the estate has built a reputation for wines that honour the grape's bold character while delivering genuine finesse.

This 100% Malbec pours a striking deep red with alluring violet hints, immediately signalling the concentration and vibrancy within. The nose is generously expressive: ripe plums and dark berries take centre stage, threaded with warming spice and a seductive hint of coffee that speaks to the wine's thoughtful ageing in oak. On the palate, it arrives compact and powerful, then opens up with a rich, juicy fruitiness that feels both indulgent and well-structured. Sweet, polished tannins and balanced acidity give the wine a satisfying grip without harshness, making it approachable now yet capable of further development in the cellar. The finish is elegant and impressively persistent — a hallmark of quality Mendoza Malbec done right.

Pour this alongside a perfectly grilled Argentine-style asado, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a hearty Bolognese. It is equally at home at a weekend barbecue with friends or anchoring a more formal dinner table. Serve at 16–18 °C to let its full aromatic complexity unfold.

name: Bread & Butter  Pinot Noir
wine_color: Light ruby (รูบี้อ่อน)
desc_en_short: Bread & Butter Pinot Noir from California is an approachable, silky red delivering gentle cherry fruit, subtle earthy notes, and soft tannins — an everyday crowd-pleaser that rewards both casual sipping and relaxed dinner tables alike.
desc_en_full:
Bread & Butter has built its reputation on a simple but compelling promise: fine California wine that feels as comfortable as a home-cooked meal. The brand sources Pinot Noir fruit from across California's cool-climate growing regions, blending parcels to achieve a consistent, fruit-forward style that speaks to the grape's naturally elegant character without demanding ceremony from the drinker.

In the glass, this Pinot Noir opens with inviting aromas of ripe cherry, fresh strawberry, and a whisper of dried rose petal, followed by a gentle undercurrent of forest floor and subtle spice — the kind of complexity that makes Pinot Noir so captivating. The winemaking team employs a gentle fermentation approach, preserving the variety's delicate aromatics and delivering a palate that is smooth, medium-bodied, and refreshingly lively, with bright natural acidity carrying the fruit through to a clean, soft finish. Tannins are deliberately light, making each sip effortlessly drinkable.

This is the bottle to open when the occasion calls for something laid-back yet genuinely satisfying. Pour it slightly chilled alongside roasted lamb chops, a grazing board of soft cheeses such as Brie or Camembert, or a simple herb-crusted beef tenderloin. It also shines as a relaxed evening sipper on its own. Bread & Butter Pinot Noir is best enjoyed young and fresh, so there is every reason to open it tonight.

name: Jacob's Creek  Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Jacob's Creek Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from South Australia delivers ripe plum, cracked pepper, and warming cinnamon spice, with approachable tannins shaped by careful oak maturation — a crowd-pleasing full-bodied red that punches well above its price point.
desc_en_full:
Jacob's Creek traces its roots to 1847, when Bavarian immigrant Johann Gramp planted his first vines along the banks of Jacob's Creek in South Australia — a region that would go on to define Australian winemaking for the world. Nearly 180 years later, the Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon stands as a testament to that founding philosophy: let the grape variety and the sun-baked Australian terroir do the talking.

The Reserve tier represents a meaningful step up in winemaking intent. Fruit is hand-selected at peak ripeness, fermented on skins to build structure and colour, then guided through malolactic fermentation to soften the natural acidity before extended oak maturation — up to 24 months — adds layers of spice, texture, and complexity that define the Cabernet Sauvignon character at this level.

In the glass, expect a deep, garnet-rimmed red. The nose opens with generous ripe plum and dark berry fruit, lifted by a dusting of cracked black pepper. On the palate, those fruit-forward flavours settle into a warm, spicy mid-palate of cinnamon and vanilla oak, while medium tannins remain soft and approachable rather than grippy or austere. The finish is clean and satisfying with a pleasant savoury edge.

This is a reliable, versatile red wine that shines alongside a grilled ribeye, slow-cooked beef short ribs, or a classic charcuterie board loaded with cured meats. It also handles the grill beautifully — open a bottle at the next barbecue and it will not disappoint.

From winemaker

Robert Mondavi established his namesake winery in 1966 with a vision to create Napa Valley wines that would stand in the company of the world's finest. He chose To Kalon Vineyard in the heart of the Napa Valley as the home for Robert Mondavi Winery. This first-growth vineyard, located in Oakville, California, is renowned for producing some of the finest Cabernet Sauvignon wines in the world, as well as for its Sauvignon Blanc grapes.

name: Chateau Lagrezette  Seigneur de Grezette Malbec
wine_color: The wine is dark red in color.
desc_en_short: From one of Cahors' most storied estates, Seigneur de Grezette is a characterful Malbec shaped by centuries of history and the guiding hand of legendary consultant Michel Rolland — delivering ripe dark fruit, supple structure, and unmistakable southwest French terroir in every glass.
desc_en_full:
Few châteaux in southwest France carry as much history as Château Lagrézette, a property whose roots stretch back to a 12th-century medieval fortress in the heart of Cahors, the ancestral home of Malbec long before the grape found international fame in Argentina. The estate's vineyards were among the earliest to cultivate this dark, brooding variety, and though phylloxera and decades of abandonment nearly erased that legacy, a passionate revival beginning in 1980 under proprietor Alain Dominique Perrin restored Lagrézette to its former glory — and then some. Enlisting the celebrated consulting oenologist Michel Rolland, the château now produces some of Cahors' most acclaimed Malbec-based wines.

Seigneur de Grezette sits as a beautifully approachable expression of that ambition. Expect a deep ruby-purple colour leading into aromas of ripe blackberry, damson plum, and a whisper of violet, underscored by notes of dark chocolate, toasted oak, and subtle earthy garrigue. On the palate, it is full-bodied yet polished, with medium tannins that frame the fruit generously and a long, satisfying finish that speaks to classic Cahors character.

This is a wine built for the table. Pour it alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb with herbes de Provence, a chargrilled côte de boeuf, or smoky barbecue ribs for a pairing that feels effortless. Decant for 30 minutes to let the wine fully open and reveal its layered complexity.

name: Vasse Felix  Filius Range Cabernet Suavignon
desc_en_short: The West Australian Wine Guide 2018 - Ray Jordan 93 points - "Such an elegant and stylish cabernet that captures the regional stamp or Margaret River. Blackcurrant and black olive tapenade on the nose. The palate has good oak integration and firm sinewy tannins. Controlled and integrated. Its a wine that clearly over-delivers at this price."
desc_en_full:
Vasse Felix Filius Range Cabernet Suavignon

'Son of' the Vasse Felix Cabernet Sauvignon. It carries the structure, power and tannins of classic Cabernet in an earlier drinking, more forward style from fruit sourced from each of the Vasse Felix vineyards.

Winemaker's Notes

The structural elements of fine Margaret River Cabernet are embraced with a hint of the Malbec's dark fruit to make a classic Vasse Felix style Cabernet Sauvignon. All vineyard sections are fermented separately, accentuating the natural beauty of each parcel of fruit. After 12 months matured in barrique, the Cabernet was blended with the ideal percentage of Malbec to create a savoury fine wine with medium weight, where big is not considered the best.

name: Campo Viejo  Winemaker's Art (750 ml)
wine_color: Ruby with a hint of garnet
desc_en_short: Campo Viejo's Winemaker's Art is a rich, expressive Tempranillo from Rioja Alta, delivering layers of red and dark fruit, polished tannins, and a clean, lingering finish — a bold statement of Spanish winemaking craft at its finest.
desc_en_full:
Campo Viejo has long been one of Rioja's most celebrated producers, and the Winemaker's Art represents the label's most personal expression — a cuvée built around the finest hand-selected Tempranillo grapes sourced from estate vineyards in the prestigious Rioja Alta subregion. This elevated corner of Rioja, with its clay-limestone soils and continental climate, consistently yields fruit of exceptional concentration and character.

In the glass, the wine opens with an inviting depth of colour and a generous nose of ripe red cherry, blackcurrant, and plum, threaded with subtle hints of dried herbs, warm spice, and toasted oak. On the palate, it lives up to its artistic ambition — full-bodied and expressive, with dark fruit flavours that build layer by layer. Firm but well-integrated tannins lend structure, while the wine's high natural acidity keeps everything fresh and precise through to a long, clean finish.

This is a wine that rewards the table. Pair it with a slow-roasted leg of lamb seasoned with rosemary and garlic, or a classic Spanish chuletón (bone-in ribeye) straight from the grill. It's equally compelling alongside a generous spread of Ibérico and Serrano ham, or aged Manchego and Idiazábal cheeses. Confident, structured, and unmistakably Riojan — the Winemaker's Art earns its name.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection  Vint Bourbon Barrel-Aged Cabernet Sauvignon
wine_color: Deep, rich ruby red in color
desc_en_short: Robert Mondavi Private Selection Bourbon Barrel-Aged Cabernet Sauvignon is a bold California red finished in Kentucky bourbon casks, delivering a richly layered experience of blackberry, praline, vanilla custard, and mocha, framed by soft, chewy tannins and a smoky oak finish.
desc_en_full:
Robert Mondavi's Private Selection label has long championed imaginative winemaking within California's coastal valleys, and the Bourbon Barrel-Aged Cabernet Sauvignon stands as one of its most distinctive expressions. Sourced from Monterey County vineyards — where cool Pacific breezes preserve vibrant fruit character — the grapes are harvested at peak ripeness before the winemaking team applies a genuinely original finishing technique.

A portion of the blend is transferred into a combination of new and used bourbon barrels, the latter sourced from a renowned Kentucky distillery. New barrels contribute deep, resonant layers of toasted oak, while the seasoned casks carry whispers of vanilla, dried herbs, and the ghost of fine bourbon that once aged within their staves. The result is a Cabernet Sauvignon that sits at a compelling crossroads of California fruit power and Southern American barrel craft.

In the glass, aromas of blackberry cobbler, graham cracker, brown sugar, milk chocolate, coffee, and smoke build a seductive opening act. The palate follows with blueberry pie, praline, sweet vanilla custard, caramel, and mocha — all underpinned by soft, chewy tannins and a lingering finish of toasted oak. Accolades from the Ultimate Wine Challenge and San Francisco International Wine Competition affirm what the glass already tells you.

name: San Marzano  Talo Primitivo di Manduria DOP
wine_color: Ruby red colur enriched with violet hues
desc_en_short: From the sun-drenched heel of Italy's boot, San Marzano's Talò Primitivo di Manduria is a richly structured Puglian red delivering opulent aromas of ripe cherry, plum, cocoa, and vanilla — a warm, generously flavoured wine with a seductively sweet, lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Few corners of southern Italy produce red wine with the sheer, sun-soaked generosity of Primitivo di Manduria, the DOC heartland of Puglia where the indigenous Primitivo grape — the same variety known as Zinfandel in California — ripens to extraordinary depth under an unrelenting Adriatic sun. The Talò bottling from Cantina San Marzano is a tribute to the small-plot growers whose families have tended these gnarly, low-trained vines for generations, each plot treated as carefully as a kitchen garden.

In the glass, Talò pours a deep ruby lit with violet highlights, a visual cue to the wine's ripe, primary character. The bouquet is opulent and immediately inviting — dark cherries, plum compote, and a whisper of dried violets framed by layers of cocoa and vanilla that speak to measured oak ageing. On the palate, the wine's full body and plush, high tannins are softened by the characteristically warm, almost velvety texture that Manduria's old vines deliver, while low acidity amplifies the sensation of ripeness and a finish that trails off with that signature gentle sweetness Primitivo devotees prize.

Talò is the kind of bottle that shines at the table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised lamb ragù over pappardelle, grilled beef ribs fresh off the barbecue, or a board of aged Pecorino and Parmigiano. Its spice-friendly structure also makes it an unexpected partner for richly seasoned, slow-cooked dishes from any cuisine. Serve at 16–18 °C to let its full aromatic expression unfold.

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci  Villa Da Vinci S.to Ippolito
desc_en_short: A bold Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Syrah, and Merlot from the historic vineyards surrounding the Villa da Vinci estate, crafted using the proprietary Metodo Leonardo® to deliver rich blackberry, warm spice, and balsamic depth with seamless oak integration.
desc_en_full:
Rooted in one of Tuscany's most storied landscapes, Villa da Vinci Santo Ippolito from Cantine Leonardo da Vinci draws its identity from the very countryside once owned by Leonardo da Vinci's family near the hilltop town of Vinci. The estate's vineyards — designated as Cru di Villa da Vinci — supply fruit for this flagship red blend, produced in strict accordance with the house's proprietary Metodo Leonardo®, a philosophy centred on expressing the highest quality potential of each variety grown on this distinctive Tuscan terroir.

The blend marries Sangiovese (40%) with equal shares of Syrah (30%) and Merlot (30%), an inspired combination that fuses Sangiovese's vibrant cherry-tinged acidity and structural backbone with the dark, peppery depth of Syrah and the plush, velvety fruit of Merlot. Fermentation in temperature-controlled stainless steel preserves aromatic purity before the wine spends 12 to 18 months maturing in predominantly new oak, which lends layers of vanilla, cedar, and subtle menthol alongside the wine's signature balsamic lift.

In the glass, expect concentrated aromas of blackberry jam, ripe plum, and warm spice, giving way to a palate that is full-bodied yet well-balanced, with medium tannins and a long, spice-kissed finish. This is an ideal partner for a slow-braised Florentine beef stew, grilled lamb chops, or a generous board of aged Pecorino and Tuscan cured meats. Equally at home at a convivial dinner table or as the centrepiece of a celebratory evening.

desc_en_short: Deep intense bright cherry colour with coloured tears. Intensely expressive nose laced with spicy and mineral notes. Juicy and fresh flavour. Smooth drinkable and balanced with a lovely fruity finish.
desc_en_full:
Region

The great plain of La Mancha, to the south of Madrid and east of Toledo, represents the largest viticultural area of Spain. The climate is arid, extreme in both summer and winter, tempered by strong prevailing winds. The widely planted white Arien grape variety is slowly losing favour to Tempranillo, Shiraz and Garnacha and the old wineries are giving way to gleaming modern facilities heralding a revolution for the region`s winemaking.

Producer

Dominio de Punctum is situated in the town of Las Pedroneras at the north end of La Mancha. It was founded in 2005 and run by the Fernandez family, the estate currently includes over 150 hectares of vineyards. The winery is located adjacent to the vineyards allowing to keep the maximum quality in the production of the whites, reds, young and aged wines. Since the beginning they have focused on organic and biodynamic production.

Tasting Notes   Deep intense bright cherry colour with coloured tears. Intensely expressive nose laced with spicy and mineral notes. Juicy and fresh flavour. Smooth drinkable and balanced with a lovely fruity finish.

name: Bertinga  Sassi Chiusi Rosso Tuscany IGT
desc_en_short: Bertinga's Sassi Chiusi is a vibrant Tuscan IGT blending 85% Sangiovese with 15% Merlot, vinified in stainless steel and concrete for freshness, delivering expressive red fruit, iris, violet, and a lively, spice-laced finish of impressive length.
desc_en_full:
Bertinga is a boutique estate in the heart of Tuscany, crafting IGT wines that champion terroir-driven expression over formula. The Sassi Chiusi Rosso Tuscany IGT is a testament to that philosophy — a Sangiovese-led blend that trades heavy oak influence for pure, energetic fruit character, making it one of the more refreshingly honest interpretations of central Tuscany's red wine identity.

The blend of 85% Sangiovese and 15% Merlot was harvested entirely by hand into small 10-kilogram boxes, then meticulously sorted on the selection table before each variety was vinified separately in temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats and concrete tanks. The wine then spent 18 months in those same vessels, preserving aromatics and natural vibrancy, followed by a minimum 12 months of bottle ageing before release — a patient, unhurried process that speaks to the estate's commitment to quality.

In the glass, Sassi Chiusi is immediately expressive: strawberry, black cherry, and blackcurrant form the fruit core, while Sangiovese's signature florals — iris and violet — lift the nose with elegance. On the palate, the wine is full-bodied yet animated by bright, refreshing acidity that carries a long finish tinged with citrus zest, green pepper, and fresh spice. Tannins are medium and well-integrated, offering structure without austerity.

Pair with Florentine-style bistecca, slow-braised lamb shanks, or aged Pecorino for a quintessentially Tuscan table experience.

name: Orapiena  Montepulciano D'Abruzzo
wine_color: Intense ruby red in colour with purple hues.
desc_en_short: Orapiena's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo pours a deep ruby with purple hues, delivering ripe red fruit, vanilla, cocoa, and warming spice on a full-bodied, velvety palate — a generous, food-friendly red from one of southern Italy's most reliable appellations.
desc_en_full:
Hailing from the sun-drenched hills of Abruzzo on Italy's Adriatic coast, Orapiena's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a dependable expression of one of central Italy's most characterful indigenous grapes. Montepulciano — not to be confused with the Tuscan town of the same name — thrives in Abruzzo's continental climate, where warm days and cool nights allow the thick-skinned berries to develop both generous fruit concentration and the structure to carry it gracefully.

In the glass, this wine is an inviting deep ruby shot through with vivid purple hues, hinting at its youthful vitality. The nose opens with a generous basket of ripe dark cherries, plum, and blackberry, layered with comforting notes of vanilla and cocoa that suggest careful oak integration. A dusting of dried herbs and warming spice rounds out the bouquet, adding complexity without weight.

On the palate, the wine lives up to its full-bodied promise — plush and velvety in texture, with medium tannins that feel polished rather than grippy, and medium acidity keeping everything fresh and lively to the finish. It is the kind of bottle that feels equally at home poured generously at a long Sunday lunch or opened thoughtfully alongside a quiet weeknight dinner.

Pair it with slow-braised lamb shoulder, a hearty Bolognese, grilled ribeye, or aged Pecorino for a classically Italian experience. Adventurous drinkers will also find it handles richly sauced pasta dishes with ease.

name: Xige Estate  Jade Dove Red (Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot)
desc_en_short: From the Helan Mountain wine region of Ningxia, Xige Estate's Jade Dove Red blends Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot into an elegant, fruit-forward red with silky tannins, ripe berry aromas, and a polished structure that punches well above its category.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the Dove Mountains at the heart of Ningxia's celebrated Helan Mountain Wine Region, Xige Estate has become one of China's most ambitious and technically accomplished fine wine producers since its founding. The estate sprawls across more than 2,000 hectares of self-owned vineyard and has distinguished itself as the first Chinese winery to implement a smart agriculture control system — earning BRCGS international certification, the world's highest food and beverage quality standard. Over 100 international awards in just five years speak to just how seriously Xige takes its craft.

The Jade Dove Red is a classic Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend that elegantly showcases what Ningxia's continental climate and gravelly loam soils can achieve. Expect an inviting nose of ripe red cherries, blackcurrant, and a whisper of dried violet, underpinned by a fresh, lively lift that keeps the fruit energetic rather than heavy. On the palate, the wine is round and generous — Merlot lending plush, velvety texture while Cabernet Sauvignon provides backbone and a satisfying depth of structure. Tannins are notably silky, making this an approachable yet complete red wine that rewards both casual and considered drinking.

A natural companion to slow-roasted lamb shoulder with cumin and garlic, grilled ribeye, or Sichuan-spiced braised beef — the Jade Dove Red bridges Chinese culinary tradition with international wine style effortlessly. Serve at 16–18°C.

name: Noble Vines  "181" Lodi Merlot
desc_en_short: Vine types perform at their best when they are put to work in the type of soil and climate from which they were born. The clay soil and temperate Mediterranean climate of our Clay Station Vineyard on a breezy knoll in Lodi make this a perfect California home for the vaunted Merlot 181 vine stock from Pomerol, France. 181 Merlot vine stock is used to thick, silty soils that hold their moisture from winter rains into the growing season. Like Bordeaux, Lodi offers stone “cobbles” over a layer of clay red soil. These soils allow for deep root penetration and a stable vine during times of low moisture and heat. This allows the roots to “reach out” and establish a balanced vine.

desc_en_full: Founded in 1997, Wine Company Shumi produces a wide range of grape-based alcoholic beverages, including some created using patented technology and others found nowhere else in the world. Under the direction of top Georgian winemaker, Giorgi Khatiashvili, consulting with renowned French winemaker Jean-Michel Ferrandez, Shumi’s products are acclaimed for their quality and have earned more than 450 prestigious international awards. The concept, which was basement of the company in 1997, focused not only on the quality production, but also on restoring forgotten traditions, planting vineyards in controlled microzones and finding and restoring the Georgian vine gene pool.

name: Reguta Carant Tre Venezie I.G.P.
style: Cabernet Sauvignon , Merlot , Refosco Dal Peduncolo Rosso
desc_en_short: A richly perfumed Tre Venezie blend from Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Reguta Carant weaves Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Refosco dal Peduncolo Rosso into a velvety, full-bodied red with enticing notes of cherry, spice, and nutmeg — structured yet approachable.
desc_en_full:
Nestled in the heart of Friuli-Venezia Giulia, the Reguta estate has long championed the rich viticultural heritage of northeastern Italy's Tre Venezie zone — a vast I.G.P. territory stretching across Friuli, Veneto, and Trentino-Alto Adige that rewards blenders with exceptional versatility. The Carant bottling is a confident expression of that philosophy, marrying the international gravitas of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with the distinctly indigenous character of Refosco dal Peduncolo Rosso, a native Friulian grape prized for its deep colour, rustic backbone, and bright acidity.

In the glass, Carant pours a deep garnet with a luminous ruby rim. The nose is generous and perfumed — ripe morello cherry and dark plum lead the way, underpinned by warm baking spice, a whisper of nutmeg, and a subtle earthy note that betrays Refosco's Friulian roots. On the palate, the wine delivers on its aromatic promise: full-bodied yet polished, with velvety tannins that wrap around the mid-palate and a persistent, spice-tinged finish that lingers pleasingly.

This is a crowd-pleasing red built for the dinner table. Pour it alongside a slow-braised beef short rib, a herb-crusted rack of lamb, or a hearty Bolognese. Its balanced structure also holds its own against aged hard cheeses. Serve at 16–18 °C to let its aromatic complexity fully open up.

name: Villa Sandi  Prosecco IL Fresco Bio (Organic)
desc_en_short: The organoleptic examination highlights the valuable characteristics of this Prosecco from Villa Sandi starting from the color which is pale straw yellow with a fine and persistent perlage. The bouquet is fruity and floral with notes of ripe Golden apple and small mountain flowers. In the mouth it is dry, soft and savory with a fruity and aromatic finish.
desc_en_full:
PRODUCTION AREA

The vineyards are located within the Prosecco DOC area, cultivated in medium-to-alluvial soils, conducted under organic viticulture. The biological value becomes even more a profound meaning of the quality linked to the peculiar characteristics of the Terroir, a link always protected and valued by the company

TRAINING SYSTEM

Single espalier, double canopy and radiating systems with 2300 vines per hectare.

YIELD PER HECTARE

Maximum 180 quintals of grapes.

SECONDARY FERMENTATION

The whole grapes are put into the pneumatic press for soft pressing and the resulting must, after a first racking, is put into controlled temperature tanks where it remains until it is used for the production of the sparkling wine.

WINEMAKING

Added with yeast cultures selected by Villa Sandi, the must is then put into vessels where fermentation takes place at a controlled temperature of 15-16°C to enhance all the harvest fragrances. Upon reaching the required alcohol and sugar content fermentation is stopped by refrigeration. After a short settling period the sparkling wine is ready for bottling. The refermentation is carried out in such a way as to preserve all the aromatic heritage and develop floral and fruity aromas typical of the variety and the territory.

PAIRING

An excellent aperitif, perfect with shellfish or with fried fish.

name: Champagne  Alain Navarre Cuvée Tradition Brut
wine_color: Clear color with brilliant straw gold nuances.
desc_en_short: Clear color with brilliant straw gold nuances. Beautiful fine bubbles. Elegant nose of dried fruits and white fruits and tangy mirabelle plum enhanced by fresh citrus notes. Gourmet mouth, round and ample. Long, balanced finish.
desc_en_full:
ALAIN NAVARRE is above all an Independent Champagne Winegrower where only the production of the 10 hectare (25 acres) family vinevard is used for the different cuvees.

The estate is located in the Marne valley, more precisely in Passy-sur-Marne, a charming little village of 150 inhabitants between Epernay and Chateau-Thierry. The vines are located on the right bank of La Marne (the river) in the villages of Passy-sur-Marne and Trelou-sur-Marne. They benefit from exceptional south-facing terroirs where the 3 Champagne grape varieties (Chardonnay, Meunier, Pinot Noir) are represented.

Four generations of passionate winegrowers have succeeded each other on the family estate. In the second half of the 19th century, it was Felix Dubois, from Champagne, who began the activity of winegrower in Passy-sur-Marne and lived in what remains to this day the family house and farm. His son Daniel Dubois was born there in 1899. He joined his father Felix and was also the first winemaker in the village to have his own wine press. The first Champagne brand of the family was founded : Champagne Daniel Dubois. Later, in 1960, Jean-Marie Dubois (Daniel's son), also born in the family home, took over from his father and created Champagne Jean-Marie Dubois. One of his three daughters, Joelle, married a coppersmith (Alain) and they decided to take over the business by creating their own brand : Champagne ALAIN NAVARRE. The couple is developing the activity of the estate and will greatly contribute the reputation that their wines enjoy today. Since 2015, they have been joined by Marjorie (daughter of Alain and Joelle Navarre) and her husband Arnaud, himself a Champagne winemaker from the neighboring town. They merged their two estates in 2019 and are now the new managers. They work together with enthusiasm to produce the ALAIN NAVARRE cuvees and perpetuate the family tradition.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Chardonnay
desc_en_short: Golden straw in color and opens with aromas of key lime pie, pineapple, crème brûlee, and vanilla bean.Rich and creamy flavors of baked apples, poached pears, pineapple, baking spices, and toasty oak with a balanced acidity and finish. Enjoy our Chardonnay with roasted turkey and rosemary stuffing, chicken cordon bleu, fish dishes, and fettucine Alfredo.
desc_en_full:
WINEMAKING :

We pressed the grapes as whole clusters to fully capture the purity and freshness of the juice, 30% of which we barrel-fermented and aged for 10 months in French/American oak to enhance texture and complexity. The balance was tank-fermented to accentuate the wine's fruitiness. Thirty percent of the blend also underwent malolactic fermentation to foster a rich, creamy mouthfeel, while 100% aged on the yeast lees for 10 months, with periodic stirring, to promote even greater richness.

name: Montes  Alpha Chardonnay
desc_en_short: 93 points in James Suckling - "This is a very serious chardonnay with pie-crust and cooked-apple character. Full body, tight and focused. Beautiful and long. Small percentage of new wood and malolactic fermentation. Excellent. Drink now."
desc_en_full: Delicious, full-bodied, complex and creamy in the mid-palate, our Alpha Chardonnay is one of the few Chilean whites wines that will improve with age. The 1998 vintage was selected as ‘World Champion Chardonnay’ by Slow Food of Italy, after simultaneous tastings in 26 cities around the world, covered live by BBC-TV in London. Grapes are sourced from the renowned white wine area, Casablanca Valley. Fermented and aged in French oak barrels for one year; 40% of the wine undergoes secondary ‘malolactic fermentation’ to enhance buttery flavors. A monthly stirring of the wine or batonage enhances flavor and aroma complexity.

name: "Tormaresca" By Antinori  Chardonnay IGT
desc_en_short: Tormaresca Chardonnay is a fresh, approachable white from Antinori's southern Italian estate in Puglia — showing ripe tropical fruit, citrus zest, and a hint of toasty oak, with generous texture and a clean, lively finish.
desc_en_full:
Tormaresca is the Antinori family's venture into Puglia's sun-drenched heel of Italy, where the warm climate and maritime influence of the Adriatic and Ionian seas create conditions for rich, characterful wines. Their Chardonnay reflects both the warmth of the south and the winemaking precision that the Antinori name demands.

Vinified and partially aged in oak, the wine shows ripe yellow apple, mango, and citrus fruit alongside a subtle butterscotch and toasted almond note from the wood contact. The palate is broad and textured, with good weight and refreshing acidity that keeps the wine lively. It finishes clean and fruity with just enough length to satisfy.

An ideal partner for seafood pasta, grilled prawns, mild curries, roasted chicken, and soft fresh cheeses.

name: Elena Walch  Pinot Grigio Alto Adige D.O.C.
desc_en_short: Elena Walch's Pinot Grigio is one of Alto Adige's finest expressions of the variety — precise and Alpine in character with crisp green apple, pear, mineral slate notes, and a distinctive floral elegance that sets it apart from flatter interpretations of the grape.
desc_en_full:
Elena Walch is one of Alto Adige's most celebrated wine producers, farming steep mountain vineyards in the Caldaro and Tramin area where the combination of Alpine altitude, intense sun, and cool nights creates ideal conditions for aromatic, high-quality white wines.

Her Pinot Grigio is a benchmark for the Alto Adige style — a more precise, mountain-influenced interpretation than you find further south. Pale straw with green highlights, it opens on fresh pear, crisp Granny Smith apple, citrus zest, and white flowers, backed by a clean, stony mineral note that speaks clearly of the region. The palate is dry, taut, and refreshing, with vibrant acidity, moderate body, and a clean, mineral finish.

Excellent with grilled fish and seafood, light pasta dishes with cream sauces, chicken, goat's cheese, and charcuterie. Equally enjoyable as an aperitif.

name: Springfield Estate  The Work of Time
style: Merlot, 31% Cabrnet Franc, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon,  14% Petit Verdot
grape_variety: Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: It was our desire to produce a Bordeaux Blend and so we planted the classic varieties of Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Petit Verdot. These vines were planted on carefully selected sites and took 9 years to reach a level of maturity which we felt comfortable to use for the maiden vintage (2001). The grapes were fermented whole with native yeasts (true to our ancient custom) and left for 5 weeks on their skins. A slow 2 years of barrel maturation followed and a further 4 years of bottle maturation. Finally, we released this wine rich, classic and complex from age – of vines and wine. This long wait, justified only by our passion, does bear fruit. It’s called-“The Work of Time”.

name: Chateau De Pez  Saint-Estèphe 2021
wine_color: A deep color and harmonious composition.
desc_en_short:
Winemaker Notes

The Château de Pez winery remains resolutely faithful to wood. The blend is composed in December, then the wine is stored in barrels (40% new wood, 40% in "Premier vin" casks and 20% in "Deuxième vin" casks) and racked every three months. It is to be noted that the wine undergoes absolutely no filtration. Château de Pez exhibits deep color and harmonious composition. This complete, rich, dense wine merits prolonged ageing. Thanks to the quality of its production, Château de Pez is counted among the most highly reputed crus of the Médoc. Despite all of the surprises, fears and doubts, 2015 turned out to be a remarkable vintage. The growing cycle took place in perfect conditions up until some extremely dry weather in July, leading to worries over low yields. The rain and associated fungal diseases that we usually fear turned out to work for us from mid-July to mid-September, enabling the vine to reach optimum ripeness levels. The wines are distinctive and exceptionally precise yet also dense and restrained. They remind us of the extremely dense yet ethereal Bordeaux’s of the 1980s, with an excellent ageing potential yet able to be enjoyed in their youth.

name: Dal Forno Romano  Valpolicella Superiore DOC
desc_en_short: Valpolicella offers hints of blueberry, blackberry, cherry and chocolate which emerge gradually as the wine breathes. The potent tannins, which give structure to this product, are in perfect symbiosis with the velvety aromas of sweet spices and jam which envelop the palate and excite the senses.
desc_en_full: Valpolicella, as we conceive it to be, is a wine which can be defined from a multitude of related wines by the particular methodology we use to produce it. In fact, we faithfully follow the same qualitative and procedural criteria that we use for Amarone. It is enough to just think that, since the 2002 vintage, all grapes that go into the making of Valpolicella are subjected to a light but advantageous drying process which lasts around one and a half months. This process allows the wine to achieve an optimal balance between organoleptic concentration and olfactory freshness.

name: Wither Hills  Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Rich brooding aromatics full of dark wild berry fruits, alluring toasty spice and subtle earthy undertones blend together in a concentrated hedonistic bouquet.
desc_en_full:
Winemaking

Our two stunning Pinot Noir vineyards, Benmorven and Taylor River, make up the majority of this blend with the fruit for this Pinot Noir coming from Marlborough's famed 'Southern Valley's' sub-region. Each vineyard was hand-harvested at optimum ripeness and kept separate throughout the winemaking process to provide the greatest possible individual expression. The fruit was cold soaked in square fermenters, inoculated with selected yeast strains, basket pressed and left to relax for 14 months in French oak barriques, during which time the wine underwent malolactic fermentation. The wine was then blended, egg white fined, filtered and bottled, resulting in a classically elegant yet opulent mouth-filling Marlborough Pinot Noir.

Testing Nose

Rich brooding aromatics full of dark wild berry fruits, alluring toasty spice and subtle earthy undertones blend together in a concentrated hedonistic bouquet.

name: Henschke  Henry's Seven, Barossa Valley
desc_en_short: Deep crimson with ruby hues. Attractive aromas of spiced plum, red currant and blue and black berry fruits are met with floral nuances of violets and black pepper spice. The round, supple palate is filled with juicy blue and red fruits, spiced plum, liquorice and notes of crushed tarragon, with beautifully textured svelte tannins and a long, peppery finish.
desc_en_full:
Vintage Description

Above average 2021 winter rainfall helped to refill soil profiles and the cool, slow start to the growing season resulted in budburst occurring slightly later than average. A frost event occurred on 11 October, followed by an extreme hail event on 28 October, and the cold and wet weather mid-November coincided with flowering, and impacted fruit-set and potential yields. December and January provided a burst of summer heat, however at the beginning of vintage another severe weather event dealt a deluge of rain and even more hail to some parts of the region. While mild and dry conditions followed, the cumulative impacts of the season’s weather events resulted in lower yields across the region. The 2022 wines show great aromatics, balance of acidity and a bright core of flavour.

name: Tenuta di Biserno  Insoglio Del Cinghiale Toscana IGT
style: Syrah, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot Blend
grape_variety: Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Pecorino
desc_en_short: Insoglio del Cinghiale is the second wine of Biserno, the prestigious Tuscan estate co-founded by Lodovico Antinori — a bold, elegant Bolgheri-area red blending Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon with impressive depth, warmth, and Mediterranean character.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta di Biserno was established by Lodovico Antinori, one of the pioneering figures of the Super Tuscan movement, in the coastal hills of the Etruscan Coast near Bolgheri. Insoglio del Cinghiale — 'the boar's puddle' — is the estate's second wine, crafted from the same approach and terroir that produces the flagship Biserno, but designed for earlier drinking.

The blend, typically Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon, reflects the estate's continental-Mediterranean terroir: warm, generous, and complex. The wine is aged in French barriques to develop structure and nuance while preserving its fresh fruit core. Deep ruby-purple in colour, it offers an expressive nose of blackberry, cassis, warm spice, dried herbs, cedar, and earthy minerals. On the palate, it is medium-full bodied with polished tannins, vibrant acidity, and a long, savoury finish.

Best served with roasted lamb, beef short rib, wild boar ragu, aged Pecorino, and hearty pasta dishes with meat sauces.

name: El Coto Rioja  Crianza
wine_color: Dense cherry red with orange highlights
desc_en_short: The nose is fresh and pronounced with concentrated red fruits and notes of vanilla and toasted oak. On the palate, it is silky with a core of strawberry fruit combined.
desc_en_full:
The nose is fresh and pronounced with concentrated red fruits and notes of vanilla and toasted oak. On the palate, it is silky with a core of strawberry fruit combined with sweet spicy flavours from American oak. The finish is juicy and long.

The grapes for this wine are sourced mainly from Rioja Alavesa (65%) with some from Rioja Alta (25%) and Rioja Baja (10%). Soils are mainly limestone and clay.

The must is fermented in stainless-steel, temperature-controlled tanks at 20-26ºC over a period of 20-25 days. Once fermentation is complete, malolactic fermentation occurs. The wine is aged for a minimum of 12 months in American oak and a further 6 months in bottle before release.

A stunning wine and a great match with red meats and cheeses. Exceptional with lamb.

name: Chateau Leoville Poyferre Saint-Julien Grand Cru Classé 2017
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 27% Merlot, 3% Cabernet Franc And 2% Petit Verdot,
desc_en_short:
The blessing of a great terroir

As the devastating effects of frosts have a serious impact on the viticultural landscape of Bordeaux, Saint-Julien can give praise thanks to Saint Vincent, patron Saint of the vineyard workers, blessed by his protection from the worst of the exceptional frosts and safeguarded for the duration of the vintage.

2017, not too dissimilar to its companions of misfortune in sevens, started off on the wrong foot. A very dry winter and late severe spring frosts were then turned around with a warm pleasant summer.

This beautiful weather coupled with our unique terroir really raised the bar for our 2017 vintage. The result is a wine that is dense, marked with an exquisite aromatic expression, all wrapped in a vintage with surprising cellaring potential. An outsider, quite contrary to its reputation!

name: Villa Calappiano Prosecco DOC Treviso Extra Dry
wine_color: Beautiful pale yellow with a hint of green.
desc_en_short: Elegant Prosecco from Treviso with fresh flowers, apple, and white peach notes — crisp, refreshing, with soft, persistent bubbles. Perfect for seafood and celebrations.
desc_en_full:
Villa Calappiano Prosecco DOC Treviso Extra Dry is a stylish and versatile sparkling wine from the heart of Treviso, Italy, crafted entirely from the native Glera grape. This Prosecco offers a perfect balance of elegance and freshness, making it an ideal choice for any joyful occasion.

Tasting Notes: Beautiful pale yellow with a hint of green, this Prosecco greets you with an enchanting bouquet of fresh white flowers, crisp green apple, and juicy white peach. On the palate, it is clean, bright, and refreshing, with a fine mousse of soft, persistent bubbles that deliver a smooth, elegant finish.

Winemaking & Terroir: Crafted using the traditional Charmat method, which preserves the natural freshness and fruity expression of the Glera grape. The grapes are sourced from select vineyards in Treviso, known for their mineral-rich soils and cool climate, contributing to the wine’s vibrant acidity and aromatic complexity.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: An ideal pairing with shellfish, seafood risotto , and sushi. It also makes a perfect aperitif for celebrations and casual gatherings. Serve well chilled at 10°C to highlight its lively bubbles and crisp flavors.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Best enjoyed young to capture its fresh and vibrant character. Drink now for maximum pleasure.

name: Ruppertsber Riesling Dry Pfalz
desc_en_short: Elegant and crisp Riesling from Pfalz, Germany — showcasing bright citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. A dry, refreshing white wine perfect for seafood, spicy dishes, and light cuisine.
desc_en_full:
Ruppertsberger Riesling Dry Pfalz is a beautifully crafted dry Riesling (รีสลิง) from the sun-kissed vineyards of the Pfalz (พาลซ์) region in Germany, known for producing some of the finest and most balanced Rieslings in the world. This wine reflects the perfect harmony of fruit, acidity, and minerality that makes Pfalz Rieslings so special.

Tasting Notes: Bright pale yellow in color, this Riesling opens with invigorating aromas of lime zest (เปลือกมะนาว), green apple (แอปเปิ้ลเขียว), and white peach (ลูกพีชขาว), with a subtle hint of wet stone minerality (แร่ธาตุ). On the palate, it is crisp, linear, and refreshing with lively acidity, offering flavors of citrus, orchard fruits, and a clean, mineral-driven finish. Bone-dry (Trocken) style but beautifully aromatic and fruit-forward.

Winemaking & Terroir: Produced by Ruppertsberger Winzerverein, a respected cooperative with a long tradition of quality winemaking. Grapes are grown on limestone-rich soils in the Pfalz, benefiting from the region's sunny yet cool climate that preserves acidity and aromatic intensity. Fermented in stainless steel to retain freshness and purity.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: This dry Riesling is a versatile food wine, pairing exceptionally well with seafood (especially shellfish and oysters), sushi, Thai and Asian cuisine with a touch of spice, goat cheese, and light salads. Serve chilled at 8-10°C to enjoy its crispness and vibrant character.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Although wonderfully fresh and ready to drink now, this Riesling can develop additional complexity over the next 3-5 years.

name: Nonino  Grappa Il Merlot (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Pure, soft and round with scents of rose petals and cherry stones. On the nose it recalls the smell of grape must being pressed.
desc_en_full:
Il Merlot

Pure, soft and round with scents of rose petals and cherry stones. On the nose it recalls the smell of grape must being pressed.

FEATURES

Primary product

The pomace, collected when it is fresh and soft, is selected from Merlot grapes from vocated areas.

Alcoholic content

41% vol

Fermentation

In stainless steel tanks under vacuum and in a controlled temperature environment. This is followed immediately by distillation in order to avoid harmful secondary fermentations.

Artisanal Distillation

Batch method with the removal of the heads and the tails in artisanal batch steam stills renovated by Benito Nonino.

Production

Limited and strictly dependent on the individual year's harvest.

Maturation

From six months to a year in a stainless steel tank.

Bottling

700ml bottled in transparent glass (not hand-made).

Optimum Temperature

About 12° C or in an ice glass with an open tulip shape. The aged distillates should be served at room temperature in wide balloon-type glasses.

Tasting

Before tasting, Grappa should be allowed to rest in the glass for some minutes: on exposure to oxygen the perfumes are enhanced and the nose/palate harmony is perfected.

When to drink it

Thanks to its particular elegance and persistency, drunk, sipped or lingered over at the end of a meal or at any moment of the day it is a source of real pleasure!

In cooking

One or two spoonfuls of Grappa Nonino Monovitigno® can be added after toasting the rice, to enhance the aroma of a risotto or when roasting meat you can pour in a small glass of Grappa Nonino half-way through the cooking."

name: Midori  Melon Liqueur (700 ml)
desc_en_short: MIDORI is the original melon liqueur enhanced by premium Japanese musk melons that can be enjoyed with juices, spirits and other liqueurs. A fruity sensation of fresh melons.
desc_en_full:
Simple Pleasures, Little Luxuries, and Tini Triumphs.

Discovering new hideaways, a party dress just because. It's stepping out with friends. Dancing anywhere. It's the sunshine on your face, your best friend's laugh.

It's about being together with friends – a thousand shared smiles. It's where you are and where you want to be. MIDORI celebrates your everyday moments.

Midori is a sweet, bright-green-coloured, muskmelon-flavored liqueur made by Suntory. It is manufactured in Japan, Mexico, and France, although it was made exclusively in Japan until 1987. Midori is usually 20–21% alcohol by volume. Its name is the Japanese word for "green". French-made Midori is sweeter than the original Japanese version.

As it is extremely sweet, Midori is not usually taken "straight"; it is generally used in a mixed drink (i.e., a cocktail), such as a Japanese slipper. It is usually used in a range of long drinks – with lemonade, fresh lemon juice, lime juice, pineapple juice, or orange juice. Sour flavours are often combined with it to balance its sweetness.

name: Donnafugata  Rosa Dolce & Gabbana
desc_en_short:
The pleasure of the family that meets at the dining table and the conviviality that survives even in this delicate moment: Dolce&Gabbana and Donnafugata renew their collaboration by giving life to Rosa, a new rosé wine with a fruity and floral personality. A message of common positivity and rebirth, starting from its roots: Sicily.

Love for their native land, attention to detail and craftsmanship: united by the desire to make the colours, scents and history of Sicilian culture known to every corner of the world, these two excellences of Made in Italy strengthen their partnership in the name of the tradition and expertise of the Bel Paese.
desc_en_full:
THE GRAPES

Nerello Mascalese is produced by Donnafugata on the northern slopes of Mount Etna, between Randazzo and Castiglione di Sicilia, where the volcanic soil and the particular microclimate give birth to wines of mineral notes laced with floral delicacy. Indeed, Rosa is distinguished by a refined bouquet of jasmine, enriched by delicate hints of wild strawberry, peach and bergamot.

The captivating fruity component derives instead from the Nocera grapes produced on the Contessa Entellina Estate where, after years of experimentation, Donnafugata has planted this ancient, almost forgotten variety that has proven to give excellent results.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Merlot
desc_en_short: Duckhorn's Decoy Merlot delivers a lush, fruit-forward expression built on black cherry, blueberry, and plum, framed by dark chocolate, cedar, and a mineral backbone — a polished, accessible take on the varietal from one of California's most celebrated Merlot houses.
desc_en_full:
Duckhorn Vineyards has spent more than three decades establishing itself as one of California's foremost authorities on Merlot, and the Decoy label is where that deep expertise meets everyday approachability. Crafted by the same winemaking team responsible for Duckhorn's celebrated Napa Valley bottlings, Decoy Merlot draws on fruit sourced across premium California growing regions, allowing the team to select for optimal ripeness, balance, and varietal character in every vintage.

The blend is anchored by 98% Merlot, with small additions of Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot adding structural depth and a subtle lift of colour. The result is a wine that feels simultaneously generous and composed — layers of black cherry, blueberry, and ripe plum unfold on the nose, underpinned by enticing notes of dark chocolate, cedar, and cool mineral. On the palate, medium acidity keeps everything bright and focused, while the tannins — firm but polished — carry the wine through to a concentrated, fruit-driven finish.

This is a bottle that earns its place at the dinner table just as readily as it suits a relaxed evening pour. Pair it with a slow-braised lamb shoulder, a Bolognese ragu, or a board of aged hard cheeses and charcuterie. Those who enjoy a more classic pairing will find it sings alongside a well-seasoned ribeye or grilled beef tenderloin.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Cabernet Sauvignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 13% Merlot, 4% Zinfandel, 2% Petite Sirah, 2% Syrah, 1% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: Duckhorn's Decoy Cabernet Sauvignon is a lush, crowd-pleasing California red built on layers of blackberry, black cherry, and dark chocolate, with refined tannins and a hint of spearmint that keep every sip fresh and satisfying.
desc_en_full:
Duckhorn Vineyards has been one of Napa Valley's most respected names since 1978, and its Decoy label carries that winemaking philosophy into a more approachable, everyday-drinking tier without sacrificing character. Sourcing fruit from California's premium growing regions — with Sonoma County's cooler coastal influence playing a key role — the Decoy Cabernet Sauvignon is crafted to be enjoyed from the moment the cork is pulled, yet it rewards those who give it a little time to open up in the glass.

The 2022 vintage is a classically structured blend anchored by 78% Cabernet Sauvignon, with Merlot lending plushness, Zinfandel adding a wild-berry brightness, and small additions of Petite Sirah, Syrah, and Petit Verdot deepening the colour and complexity. The result is a wine that feels both generous and focused: rich waves of blackberry, black cherry, and blueberry are layered with dark chocolate and a lift of fresh spearmint, all framed by polished, firm tannins that carry through to a long, rewarding finish.

This is the kind of bottle that earns its place at the dinner table time and again. Pour it alongside a slow-roasted rack of lamb, a well-seasoned prime rib, or a board of aged hard cheeses and cured meats. With its full body and medium acidity, it strikes the balance between bold and refined — making it equally at home at a casual weeknight grill or a more celebratory occasion.

name: Robert Mondavi   Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 8% Merlot, 7% Petit Verdot, 3% Cabernet Franc, 1% Malbec
desc_en_short: Fruit for our Napa Valley Cabernet comes from sub-appellations throughout the Valley, each of which have been specially chosen for their contributions to a combined blend that is a true reflection of place, a gorgeous expression of Napa Valley.  Nearly half of this year’s blend is sourced from the deep, well-drained soils of Oakville, which give us gorgeously dark, supple wines framed by fine tannin. Complementary portions of the blend come from Stags Leap District, Oak Knoll, Yountville and Rutherford.
desc_en_full:
Robert Mondavi established his namesake winery in 1966 with a vision to create Napa Valley wines that would stand in the company of the world's finest. He chose To Kalon Vineyard in the heart of the Napa Valley as the home for Robert Mondavi Winery. It is said that the wines produced here. Taste wine from the region of Bordeaux in France compared to one.

This first-growth vineyard, located in Oakville, California, is renowned for producing some of the finest Cabernet Sauvignon wines in the world, as well as for its Sauvignon Blanc grapes. ( 985% Cabernet Sauvignon, 7% Petit Verdot, 5% Merlot, 3% Cabernet Franc)

A classic Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon, Dark fruits of black cherry, plum and blackberry are layered seamlessly with sweet spice, herbal, dark earth and a touch of sweet tobacco and accompany the rich concentration, silky tannins and long finish.

VINEYARD NOTES :

Fruit for our Napa Valley Cabernet includes two of the valley's most renowned sub-appellations and vineyards: our historic To Kalon vineyard in Oakville located on the well-drained alluvial bench surrounding the winery; and a smaller percentage from our Wappo Hill Vineyard in the Stag's Leap district. The varying mesoclimates and soils of these two vineyards elicit a wonderfully concentrated, expressive, and classic Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon and are combined with fruit from a number of the area's finest vineyards for the perfect expression of the region.

WINEMAKER NOTES :

(85% Cabernet Sauvignon, 7% Petit Verdot, 5% Merlot, 3% Cabernet Franc) The grapes were hand harvested into small bins and carefully sorted in our gravity-flow cellar. The clusters were gently de-stemmed directly into traditional oak tanks for cold soak, fermentation and extended maceration-a total of 24 days of wine to skin contact--maximizing the extraction of varietal character and complexity while keeping the tannins fleshy and supple. The new wine was drained and gently pressed into French oak barrels (19% new) for malolactic fermentation, assuring seamless integration of fruit and oak. The final blend was assembled through repeated tasting trials over the 17 months of barrel aging. The wine was bottled in May 2018.

name: Antinori  Tignanello IGT
wine_color: Intensely ruby red in color with purple hues.
desc_en_short: Tignanello is one of Italy's most iconic Super Tuscans, crafted by the legendary Antinori family from their Santa Cristina estate in Chianti Classico. A bold Sangiovese-led blend with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, it pioneered the use of non-traditional varieties and French barriques in Tuscany.
desc_en_full:
Tignanello is not merely a wine — it is a landmark in the history of Italian viticulture. When Marchese Piero Antinori first released it in the early 1970s, he broke decisively from tradition: no white grapes, no large Slavonian oak casks, no DOC classification. What he created instead was a blend of Sangiovese with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, aged in small French barriques, that would go on to define an entire category — the Super Tuscan.

The wine comes from the Tignanello estate in Chianti Classico, where the unique combination of galestro and alberese soils, combined with altitude and optimal sun exposure, creates the conditions for extraordinary red wines. The Sangiovese provides the structural backbone and sour-cherry vivacity characteristic of the region, while the Cabernet grapes add depth, dark fruit, and cedar complexity.

In the glass, Tignanello presents a deep garnet with violet highlights. The nose is expressive and layered — plum, blackcurrant, violet, tobacco, and warm spice with hints of leather and graphite emerging as it opens. On the palate, it is full-bodied with firm but polished tannins, excellent acidity, and a long, mineral finish. This is a wine built for the cellar but deeply pleasurable with a decade's patience. Pair with bistecca alla Fiorentina, roast lamb, aged Parmigiano, or rich game dishes.

name: Continuum 2019
wine_color: A dense purple robe and vibrant violet rim.
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 37% Cabernet Franc, 7% Petit Verdot, 6% Merlot
desc_en_short: A landmark Napa Valley Bordeaux blend from the Mondavi family's Sage Mountain Vineyard, the 2019 Continuum showcases a record proportion of Cabernet Franc alongside Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering volcanic mountain-fruit intensity wrapped in silky tannins, dark mulberry, wild herbs, and ferrous minerality.
desc_en_full:
Continuum is the legacy project of the Mondavi family — Tim Mondavi and his sister Marcia — dedicated to crafting a single, estate-grown Bordeaux-style blend from their dramatic Sage Mountain Vineyard on Pritchard Hill in Napa Valley. Farmed entirely on volcanic, rocky terrain high above the valley floor, the estate's extreme site imparts an unmistakable mineral tension and mountain-fruit concentration that sets Continuum apart from the valley's broader canon.

The 2019 vintage marks a milestone: the highest proportion of Cabernet Franc ever used in the blend — 37% — amplifying the wine's lifted aromatic character and savory complexity alongside 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 7% Petit Verdot, and 6% Merlot. Every cluster is hand-harvested at night and gravity-fed into French oak and concrete tanks, where a three-day cold soak precedes fermentation with multiple pump-overs and délestage. After 20–40 days on skins, the lots are basket-pressed, then aged 22 months across 66% new French oak barrels and a single concrete amphora — bottled unfined and unfiltered.

On the nose, hibiscus, dried rose petals, and lifted red cherry give way to concentrated black mulberry and dark plum on the palate. Layers of graphite, wild sage, exotic spice, and iron-rich minerality emerge mid-palate, while velvety fine tannins and vibrant acidity carry the wine to a long, graceful finish. Pair with a prime-cut ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or aged hard cheeses. While approachable now, the 2019 Continuum will reward a decade or more of cellaring.

name: Penfolds  Bin 23 Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Penfolds Bin 23 Pinot Noir is a multi-regional cool-climate expression drawing fruit from Tasmania's Tamar Valley, Adelaide Hills, and Henty — delivering generous strawberry, framboise, and subtle herbal complexity matured in the storied Cellar 23 at Magill Estate.
desc_en_full:
Named for Cellar 23 at Magill Estate — the very space where it quietly matures — Penfolds Bin 23 Pinot Noir carries both place and purpose in its name. First produced in 2009, it was conceived to bring Penfolds' signature fruit generosity and flavour length to the notoriously demanding Pinot Noir variety, drawing on the winery's long-standing relationships with Australia's finest cool-climate growing regions.

The wine is a deliberate blend of perspectives: fruit sourced across Tasmania's Tamar Valley, the Adelaide Hills, and Henty in Victoria captures what each region does best. The Tamar Valley delivers bright varietal precision; Adelaide Hills contributes intensity despite its sun-soaked challenges; Henty adds structure and elegance from its windswept southern latitude. The diversity of Pinot Noir clones used across these sites adds further textural and aromatic complexity.

On the palate, Bin 23 opens with an inviting rush of strawberry cream and framboise, followed by a wisp of fraises des bois and delicate herbaceousness — fresh tobacco, sage, and red currant pastille weaving through the mid-palate. Spicy oak from Magill Estate's Cellar 23 frames the wine with subtle confidence rather than dominance. Light-bodied and lively, it pairs beautifully with duck breast, roasted lamb rack, or a well-aged hard cheese. Scores of 93–94 points from critics including Tyson Stelzer and Andrew Caillard MW affirm this as serious, benchmark Australian Pinot Noir.

name: Tommasi  Amarone Della Valpolicella Classico DOC
desc_en_short: Tommasi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico is a benchmark expression of the appassimento tradition — opulent and deeply structured, with concentrated cherry, dried plum, and dark spice from a renowned multi-generational Valpolicella family estate.
desc_en_full:
Few wines in Italy command the same reverence as a well-made Amarone, and the Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOC stands as one of the appellation's most trusted benchmarks. The Tommasi family has been cultivating vines in the Valpolicella Classica zone since 1902, and their mastery of the appassimento method — the ancient practice of drying harvested grapes on bamboo racks for three to four months before fermentation — is evident in every glass.

The blend of Corvina Veronese (50%), Rondinella (30%), Corvinone (15%), and Molinara (5%) delivers the signature complexity that defines great Amarone. In the glass it presents a deep ruby red with warm garnet reflections, signalling the concentration that slow drying imparts. The nose is intense and refined: ripe dark cherry, dried fig, and plum meld seamlessly with notes of dark chocolate, tobacco, and a whisper of warm spice. On the palate, it is full-bodied and commanding, yet remarkably smooth for its structural weight, with high, polished tannins and vibrant acidity providing the backbone for long cellaring.

This is a wine made for occasion. Pour it alongside a slow-braised short rib, a Florentine-style bistecca, or a rack of herb-crusted lamb. It holds its own with aged Parmigiano Reggiano or a bold hard pecorino. Equally, as the source notes suggest, it rewards those who simply pour a generous glass and let conversation take its course. Serve at 18–20°C, and decant for at least an hour to unlock its full expressive range.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Volnay
desc_en_short: Joseph Drouhin's Volnay is a benchmark expression of Côte de Beaune elegance — silky Pinot Noir from marl-limestone soils delivering violet, blackcurrant, and gentle spice in a wine prized for its velvety texture and refined, long-lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Volnay sits on the southern slopes of the Côte de Beaune, nestled between Beaune and Pommard, and has been celebrated for centuries as one of Burgundy's most refined red wine communes. According to local legend, the pure hillside air made Volnay a favoured retreat for the ladies of the Burgundian ducal court — a fitting origin story for a village whose wines have always been defined by grace rather than brute force. Joseph Drouhin, one of Beaune's most respected négociant-éleveurs, captures that spirit with quiet authority.

The vineyards lie on a patchwork of marl and limestone punctuated by small pebbles — soils that lend Volnay its characteristic mineral lift and fine-grained tannin structure. Drouhin's commitment to gentle winemaking preserves every nuance the terroir has to offer, resulting in a Pinot Noir that feels effortlessly complete rather than manufactured.

In the glass, expect a vivid, luminous ruby. Young vintages open with lifted aromas of violet and fresh blackcurrant, underscored by an earthy, stony minerality. With time in the cellar, those primary notes evolve into candied cherry, mushroom, and warm gentle spices — the classic arc of a well-made village Volnay. The palate is rounded and supple with a velvety texture that is distinctly Volnay, finishing with echoes of candied fruit and a whisper of vanilla.

Pour alongside a rack of lamb with herbes de Provence, or a slow-braised beef cheek — dishes that match the wine's depth without overwhelming its inherent delicacy. A confident, timeless choice for any Burgundy lover's table.

name: Chateau Montelena  Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: Chateau Montelena's Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon is a cool-boned, perfumed red delivering cassis, menthol, and fennel alongside briary earthy minerality — a structured, personality-driven Cab that rewards patience and a proper decant.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in American wine history as Chateau Montelena. The Calistoga-based estate — immortalised by its 1973 Chardonnay's victory at the legendary 1976 Judgment of Paris — has spent decades proving that Napa Valley can produce wines of genuine restraint, complexity, and age-worthiness. The Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon is the estate's flagship statement of that philosophy, drawing fruit from across the valley floor and benchland sites to deliver a wine that leans cooler, more classically structured, and more European in character than many of its Napa peers.

In the glass, this 2019 Cabernet Sauvignon opens with a bright, deep ruby hue and a perfumed nose that immediately sets it apart — cassis and fresh herbs give way to menthol, anise, fennel, and a whisper of dried flowers. It is an aromatic profile that speaks of careful farming and minimal intervention rather than extraction and new oak. On the palate, the wine is juicy and focused, with a pungent spine of acidity threading through spiced dark fruit. Briary, earthy minerality adds texture and intrigue, while firm but well-integrated tannins signal genuine cellaring potential. The 2019 vintage in Napa delivered a long, even growing season, and Montelena's winemaking team made the most of it.

Decant for at least 90 minutes if opening now, or cellar through the mid-2020s to let the structure fully soften. Pour alongside a dry-aged ribeye, house-cured charcuterie, or slow-cooked short ribs off the grill.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Merlot
style: Merlot, 22% Cabernet Sauvignon, .5% Malbec, .5 % Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: Duckhorn's Napa Valley Merlot is a benchmark American Merlot — lush and generously textured, weaving Bing cherry, ripe plum, and blueberry with cedar, vanilla, and a long coffee-tinged finish underpinned by polished, full-bodied tannins.
desc_en_full:
Few producers have done more to elevate the reputation of Merlot in the United States than Duckhorn Vineyards, and their flagship Napa Valley Merlot remains the standard by which American Merlot is judged. Since the winery's founding in 1976 in St. Helena, Duckhorn has championed Merlot as a grape capable of genuine complexity and age-worthiness — a conviction this wine delivers vintage after vintage.

The 2022 bottling blends 77% Merlot with 22% Cabernet Sauvignon and small touches of Malbec and Petit Verdot sourced from across Napa Valley's finest growing districts. The result is a wine of immediate appeal yet real structure. It opens with a fleshy, inviting entry — layers of Bing cherry, fresh raspberry, and ripe plum unfold alongside hints of blueberry, adding depth and dimension. Oak-driven notes of cedar, vanilla, and clove weave seamlessly through the mid-palate, reflecting careful barrel aging that integrates rather than dominates.

The texture is the hallmark: polished tannins give way to a silky, almost seamless mouthfeel, carrying the fruit to a long, generous finish where freshly ground coffee and a final burst of ripe red cherry linger with elegance.

Pour this alongside a slow-braised short rib, a rosemary-crusted rack of lamb, or a plate of house-made tagliatelle with a rich Bolognese. It's equally at home anchoring a dinner-party table or making a weeknight feel special.

name: Duckhorn  'Goldeneye' Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Goldeneye's flagship Anderson Valley Pinot Noir from Duckhorn delivers layers of fresh raspberry, Bing cherry, and bramble berry, underpinned by silky tannins and whispers of leather, breakfast tea, and baking spice across a long, lingering finish.
desc_en_full:
Duckhorn's Goldeneye label is dedicated entirely to Pinot Noir, and its spiritual home is California's Anderson Valley — a cool, fog-kissed appellation in Mendocino County where Pacific maritime influence and well-drained soils coax exceptional depth and aromatic complexity from the grape. This 2020 bottling draws fruit from seven vineyard sites across the valley, a multi-vineyard approach that captures the full expressive range Anderson Valley has to offer.

In the winery, the fruit is vinified in 100% French oak Burgundy-style barrels sourced from the prestigious Allier, Vosges, and Tronçais forests, with 65% new oak and a 16-month aging regime lending structure and spice without overwhelming the wine's natural elegance. The result is a textbook example of California Pinot Noir executed with Burgundian discipline.

On the nose, raspberry purée and fresh oatmeal cookies open to more intricate notes of black cherry, blackberry pie, pennyroyal, and a distinctive sea spray salinity — a hallmark of Anderson Valley's coastal terroir. The palate is generous yet precise: bright, balanced acidity lifts flavors of Bing cherry and sun-warmed bramble berries, while a hint of smoke and cracked peppercorn at entry transitions into silky, supple tannins. Coffee, baking spice, and leather weave through the finish, which lingers with real persistence.

Pair Goldeneye with roasted duck breast, lamb rack with herb crust, or mushroom risotto. It is equally at home on a dinner table set for a special occasion as it is uncorked on a relaxed weekend evening.

name: Francis Coppola  Director's Cut Cinema Red Blend
style: Zinfandel, 39% Cabernet Sauvignon, 5% Petit Sirah, 4% Merlot
desc_en_short: Francis Coppola's Director's Cut Cinema is a bold Sonoma red blend built around Zinfandel and Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering lavish layers of dark berries, spiced plum, toasted oak, and a velvety finish framed by firm, age-worthy tannins.
desc_en_full:
Named after the filmmaker's ultimate creative statement, Francis Ford Coppola's Director's Cut Cinema is the flagship wine of the Director's Cut range — a project conceived by winemaker Corey Beck as his personal auteur expression of Sonoma County's finest fruit. Just as a director's cut reveals a bolder, more personal vision of a story, this wine goes beyond the conventional, drawing on appellation-specific vineyard sourcing, rigorous fruit selection, and exhaustive blending trials to capture the full character of Sonoma's diverse microclimates.

The blend — anchored by 52% Zinfandel and 39% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting roles from Petit Sirah and Merlot — is recomposed vintage by vintage from dozens of evaluated lots, ensuring Cinema remains a genuine one-of-a-kind bottling. The result is a full-bodied, deeply concentrated red with remarkable textural richness and sophistication.

On the nose, ripe blackberries, raspberries, and tangy pomegranate set the stage, woven with aromatic threads of cinnamon, coffee bean, cocoa, and cracked black pepper. The palate unfolds with sweet cherries, spiced plum, warm vanilla, and toasted oak, building toward a long, lush finish with a mineral undercurrent. Firm tannins provide structure and exceptional cellaring potential.

Cinema is a natural companion for the table — pour it alongside a slow-roasted prime rib, dry-aged steak, aged hard cheeses, or a hearty Bolognese. It will equally impress at a weekend barbecue or beside bold, spiced dishes.

name: Silver Oak  Cabernet Sauvignon Alexander Valley
desc_en_short: Silver Oak's Alexander Valley Cabernet Sauvignon is a California icon — lush with red cherry, blackberry, vanilla, and warm baking spices, offering an elegant, fruit-forward style with remarkable aging potential through 2047.
desc_en_full:
Few names carry as much weight in California Cabernet Sauvignon as Silver Oak Cellars. Founded in 1972 in the Napa Valley and later expanding into Sonoma County's Alexander Valley, Silver Oak built its reputation on a singular, unwavering commitment to American oak aging — a choice that defines the house style and sets it apart from virtually every other serious Cabernet producer in the state.

The Alexander Valley appellation, nestled within Sonoma County along the upper reaches of the Russian River, delivers a warmer, more generously fruited expression of Cabernet Sauvignon than Napa's benchland sites. The valley's alluvial soils and long, sun-drenched growing season coax ripe blackberry, red cherry, and raspberry from the vine, while the signature American oak maturation — typically 24 months — weaves in layers of vanilla, clove, and warm baking spices with a seamless, polished touch.

In the glass, this Cabernet Sauvignon shows a deep ruby hue and an immediately inviting nose of iris and red fruit. On the palate, bright acidity lifts the mid-palate beautifully, preventing any sense of heaviness despite the wine's full body. Black currant and spice linger well into a long, fruit-driven finish. The structure here supports effortless drinking now yet promises to reward patience for decades to come.

Pour this alongside a bone-in ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or an aged hard cheese board. It is equally at home at a celebratory dinner or opened on a quiet evening with a well-marbled cut from the grill.

name: Pegasus Bay  Pinot Noir
desc_en_short: Pegasus Bay Pinot Noir from Canterbury, New Zealand, is a beautifully crafted, Burgundian-inspired red vinified with whole berries, natural fermentation, and 18 months in French oak barriques — delivering silky tannins, vibrant red fruit, and genuine depth of character.
desc_en_full:
Pegasus Bay is one of New Zealand's most celebrated family estates, situated in the Waipara Valley within the broader Canterbury region — a cooler, inland growing area that coaxes Pinot Noir of remarkable elegance and complexity. With a winemaking philosophy rooted firmly in Burgundian tradition, the estate has earned a devoted following among lovers of serious, terroir-driven New Zealand red wine.

This Pinot Noir is made entirely without shortcuts. Grapes harvested in mid-to-late April are destemmed and placed into small open-top fermenting vats, preserving as many whole berries as possible to encourage a lifted, perfumed character. A cool pre-fermentation soak draws out soft, silky tannins before a completely natural, uninoculated fermentation begins — no added yeasts, no intervention. Twice-daily hand plunging keeps the cap integrated and healthy throughout. Once dry, the vats are sealed and the skins left to steep for several additional weeks, extracting structure and savoury depth, with the precise duration guided entirely by daily tasting.

Ageing follows in French oak barriques sourced from artisan Burgundian coopers — 30% new — for a full 18 months, during which a natural malo-lactic fermentation softens the wine's bright acidity into a rounder, more textural profile. The finished wine is blended from multiple individual plots, each assessed on its own merits before assembly.

Expect aromas of dark cherry, dried rose petal, and a whisper of toasty oak, with a palate that is structured yet graceful. A superb match for roasted lamb rack, duck confit, or a ripe wedge of aged Comté.
