name: Jim Barry  The Lodge Hill Shiraz
wine_color: This wine is a deep red with a bright magenta hue.
desc_en_short: Jim Barry's Lodge Hill Shiraz from Clare Valley is a medium-bodied, fruit-driven red delivering juicy plum, blackberry, and sweet spice with fine powdery tannins — an approachable, expressive Shiraz that punches well above its price point.
desc_en_full:
Jim Barry Wines has been shaping the identity of Clare Valley since 1959, and the Lodge Hill label stands as one of the estate's most beloved expressions of what this cool-climate South Australian appellation can achieve with Shiraz. Sourced from the Lodge Hill vineyard — planted at higher elevation than many Clare Valley sites — the grapes benefit from warm days and cool nights that preserve natural acidity and intensify aromatic complexity, hallmarks that set Clare Valley Shiraz apart from its warmer-climate Australian counterparts.

The 2017 vintage delivered excellent growing conditions across Clare Valley, allowing the Shiraz fruit to ripen fully while retaining elegance and freshness. In the glass, Lodge Hill Shiraz opens with inviting aromas of dark berry fruit layered with violet florals and a dusting of warm spice — classic varietal character done with restraint and purpose. On the palate, a lively burst of plum and blackberry fruit takes centre stage, framed by fine, powdery tannins that give the wine a polished, approachable texture. Sweet spice and floral notes linger through a clean, persistent finish.

This is a wine built for the table. Pair it with slow-braised lamb shoulder, a wood-fired beef brisket, or a fragrant Thai red curry for a pairing that highlights the wine's fruit generosity and spice-friendly character. Equally enjoyable on its own as a relaxed evening pour, Lodge Hill Shiraz is an outstanding introduction to Jim Barry's portfolio and to Clare Valley at its most expressive.

name: Tenuta Guado al Tasso By Antinori  COn't Ugo Bolgheri DOC
desc_en_short: Con't Ugo is the second wine of Antinori's prestigious Guado al Tasso estate in Bolgheri, offering Bordeaux-style depth and elegance at a more accessible level. Expect lush dark fruit, cedar, and Mediterranean spice with smooth, well-integrated tannins.
desc_en_full:
Tenuta Guado al Tasso is one of the jewels of the Antinori portfolio — a coastal Bolgheri estate where the proximity to the Tyrrhenian Sea and the unique terroir of gravelly alluvial soils create ideal conditions for Bordeaux varieties. Con't Ugo is the estate's second wine, drawing on the same approach and philosophy as the flagship but crafted for earlier accessibility.

The blend, typically Cabernet Sauvignon-led with Merlot and Syrah, offers a distinctly Mediterranean character: warm, ripe, and generously fruited, but never heavy. Aged in French oak barriques, it develops notes of dark plum, blackberry, dried herbs, cedar, and a whisper of tobacco — all framed by smooth, polished tannins and vibrant acidity that keeps it lively.

This is a wine that performs well young but rewards a few years' patience. Serve with roasted meats — lamb shoulder, beef tenderloin, wild boar — or rich pasta dishes with ragu.

wine_color: garnet ruby red with fleeting orange reflections
desc_en_short: At the eye, Liste appears in its intense ruby red color. At the nose, it expresses notes of wild berries, liquorish, tobacco, and cocoa. At the palate, it is dry, full-bodied, has a persistent flavor, and is austere and velvety.
desc_en_full:
Damilano is one of the oldest wineries in Barolo. The family business dates back to 1890 when Giuseppe Borgogno started cultivating and producing wine from the grapes from his own vineyards. Giuseppe improved the general quality of the vinification system and of the wines, turning the winery into a "jewel" of the Langhe area. In 1997 Giuseppe passed the winery on to his grandchildren Paolo, Mario and Guido. Together, they are working to keep and improve the level of the quality of the Damilano wines.

Tasting Notes

At the eye, Liste appears in its intense ruby red color. At the nose, it expresses notes of wild berries, liquorish, tobacco, and cocoa. At the palate, it is dry, full-bodied, has a persistent flavor, and is austere and velvety.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2009 (Premiers Crus)
wine_color: The wine has a very deep, almost black colour with glittering highlights.
desc_en_short: Campo Viejo's Winemaker's Art is a rich, expressive Tempranillo from Rioja Alta, delivering layers of red and dark fruit, polished tannins, and a clean, lingering finish — a bold statement of Spanish winemaking craft at its finest.
desc_en_full:
Campo Viejo has long been one of Rioja's most celebrated producers, and the Winemaker's Art represents the label's most personal expression — a cuvée built around the finest hand-selected Tempranillo grapes sourced from estate vineyards in the prestigious Rioja Alta subregion. This elevated corner of Rioja, with its clay-limestone soils and continental climate, consistently yields fruit of exceptional concentration and character.

In the glass, the wine opens with an inviting depth of colour and a generous nose of ripe red cherry, blackcurrant, and plum, threaded with subtle hints of dried herbs, warm spice, and toasted oak. On the palate, it lives up to its artistic ambition — full-bodied and expressive, with dark fruit flavours that build layer by layer. Firm but well-integrated tannins lend structure, while the wine's high natural acidity keeps everything fresh and precise through to a long, clean finish.

This is a wine that rewards the table. Pair it with a slow-roasted leg of lamb seasoned with rosemary and garlic, or a classic Spanish chuletón (bone-in ribeye) straight from the grill. It's equally compelling alongside a generous spread of Ibérico and Serrano ham, or aged Manchego and Idiazábal cheeses. Confident, structured, and unmistakably Riojan — the Winemaker's Art earns its name.

name: Shafer  Hillside Select Cabernet Sauvignon 2018
desc_en_short: Shafer Hillside Select 2018 is a benchmark Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon from one of the appellation's most celebrated single-vineyard sites, delivering a sumptuous layering of blackberry, berry compote, baking spice, and mocha underpinned by firm, cellar-worthy tannins.
desc_en_full:
Few Cabernet Sauvignons in California carry the same weight of expectation — or consistently meet it — as Shafer Hillside Select. Sourced entirely from Shafer's steep, rocky Stags Leap District hillside blocks in Napa Valley, this is a wine that has defined estate-grown, single-vineyard Cabernet for over three decades. The vines here dig deep into volcanic and alluvial soils that stress the fruit in exactly the right way, concentrating flavour while preserving the natural tension that separates great wine from merely impressive wine.

The 2018 vintage delivered exceptional growing conditions across Napa — warm days, cool nights, and a well-timed harvest — and Hillside Select responded with characteristic generosity. The nose opens with vivid blackberry, black cherry, and warm earth, layered with baking spice and the subtle suggestion of cedar. On the palate, the wine unfolds with impressive breadth: summer briar fruit and berry compote give way to lively red and black plum, cinnamon, and a long mocha-tinged finish. 100% Cabernet Sauvignon, aged in French oak, the structure here is both muscular and refined.

This is a wine built for the cellar — structured tannins and medium acidity promise a graceful evolution over the next 15–20 years — but it is already accessible to those who decant generously. At the table, pair it with a dry-aged ribeye, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or an aged hard cheese. A landmark bottle for a landmark occasion.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2019
wine_color: The wine has a rich colour with a garnet hue.
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 22% Merlot, 4% Cabernet Franc, 2% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: Château Clerc Milon's 2019 Pauillac is a fifth growth of uncommon elegance, delivering layered aromas of blackcurrant, violet, and licorice alongside creamy, close-knit tannins and a saline, minty finish that lingers with impressive persistence.
desc_en_full:
Château Clerc Milon occupies a privileged corner of Pauillac, its vineyards nestled between the legendary estates of Mouton Rothschild and Latour on the Left Bank's Médoc peninsula. A Fifth Growth in the 1855 Classification, the estate has been owned by the Baron Philippe de Rothschild family since 1970, and under their stewardship it has steadily punched well above its classified rank. The 2019 vintage is widely regarded as one of Bordeaux's finest in recent memory — a warm, dry growing season that delivered exceptional ripeness and natural concentration without sacrificing the freshness that defines great Pauillac.

The blend is anchored by a dominant 72% Cabernet Sauvignon, enriched by 22% Merlot for mid-palate generosity, with Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot providing aromatic lift and structural depth. In the glass, it pours a vivid crimson-garnet, immediately signalling its ambition. The nose opens with refined floral notes — violet and dried rose petal — before airing reveals classic Pauillac blackcurrant and a streak of licorice root. On the palate, a full, smooth entry gives way to a complex layering of ripe dark fruit, toasted cereal, and subtle oak spice, all framed by high-quality, creamy tannins that feel both generous and precise. The finish is long, saline, and faintly minty, with a whisper of dark chocolate.

This is a wine built for the cellar — ideally enjoyed from 2026 onwards — though it rewards patient decanting now alongside slow-roasted rack of lamb, a well-aged côte de boeuf, or a platter of hard aged cheeses like Comté or aged Manchego.

name: Le Colombier De Brown Pessac-Leognan AOC
desc_en_short: Le Colombier de Brown is the approachable second wine of Château Brown in Pessac-Léognan, blending Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon into an elegant, fruit-forward expression of Graves terroir — ready to enjoy now yet structured enough to reward short-term cellaring.
desc_en_full:
Named after an eighteenth-century stone dovecote standing in the grounds of Château Brown — a symbol of aristocratic wealth in feudal France — Le Colombier de Brown carries the heritage of one of Pessac-Léognan's distinguished estates into an approachable, pleasure-first bottle. The dovecote itself remains a quiet witness to centuries of winemaking on this storied Graves terroir, and that sense of history informs every vintage of this second wine.

Sourced from carefully selected plots within the Château Brown vineyard, the blend of 79% Merlot and 21% Cabernet Sauvignon is crafted to deliver the estate's characteristic mineral signature in a fruitier, lighter register than the Grand Vin. The same meticulous viticulture and cellar attention applied to the flagship label extends fully to this cuvée, ensuring genuine quality rather than a simple declassified overflow.

In the glass, the 2019 vintage shows a deep, lively ruby. Ripe black cherry and plum from the Merlot lead the nose, framed by a hint of graphite and damp earth that speaks unmistakably of the Pessac-Léognan gravel and clay soils. On the palate it is polished and medium-bodied in feel despite its full designation, with fine-grained tannins, bright medium acidity, and a finish that lingers on dark fruit and subtle cedar.

Pour this alongside a slow-roasted rack of lamb with herbes de Provence, a grilled côte de bœuf, or a cheese board anchored by aged Comté. It drinks beautifully now and will hold comfortably for another four to six years.

It is a wine of both immediate pleasure and long-term potential, offering elegance and structure in perfect balance—ideal for special occasions or cellaring over the next two decades.
desc_en_full:
Château Cantenac Brown, classified as a Third Growth (1855 Grand Cru Classé) from Margaux, is one of the most architecturally unique châteaux in Bordeaux, featuring a stunning Tudor-style estate and deep winemaking heritage since the early 19th century.

The vineyard is planted on deep gravel soils, ideal for cultivating Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The 2018 vintage was an exceptional year in Bordeaux—marked by concentration, ripeness, and age-worthy structure, making this a standout for collectors and connoisseurs alike.

name: Robert Mondavi   Napa Valley Red Blend
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 13% Cabernet Franc, 9% Merlot, 4% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: On the nose, Ripe plum, black cherry, cedar, and graham cracker. On the palate, sweet tobacco, dried herbs, blackberries, rich dark chocolate, and toast. Juicy and plush, with fine tannins but a layer of rich creaminess here also carries through to the finish - enjoyable now and for years to come.
desc_en_full:
Robert Mondavi Winery was founded in 1966 by winemaking pioneer Robert Mondavi, whose vision was to create Napa Valley wines that could stand in the company of the world’s finest. It was the first major winery built in Napa Valley in the three decades following the repeal of Prohibition, Robert Mondavi Winery created the foundation for the modern-day California wine industry. Today, Robert Mondavi Winery continues to pursue the fullest expression of Napa Valley terroir in wines that are also varietally authentic. Mondavi chose to situate his estate next to To Kalon Vineyard, one of the most coveted wine growing parcels in the world, known for producing award-winning Cabernet Sauvignon and for its Sauvignon Blanc.One of the first goals Robert Mondavi set for himself was recreating Sauvignon Blanc, which was, at the time a sweet, unnuanced wine. He sought inspiration in Loire Valley Pouilly Fumé, enlivening it with a uniquely California profile. He named the delightfully rich and refined yet bright, fresh wine he created Fumé Blanc. It’s Sauvignon Blanc, plus so much more. Released in 1968, Fumé Blanc remains Mr. Mondavi’s signature and one of Robert Mondavi Winery’s most popular wines.

The first vintage (1966) of Robert Mondavi Winery's Cabernet Sauvignon is released. A shot heard throughout the Valley, it would inspire the waves of pioneers to follow. In time, the path Robert Mondavi cleared would lead to the creation of some of the finest Cabernet Sauvignons, Chardonnays, Merlots and Sauvignon Blancs in the world.

“Our mission at Robert Mondavi Winery is to produce wines of elegance and complexity that are recognized globally for their exceptional quality,” says Geneviève Janssens, chief winemaker at Robert Mondavi Winery.

Testing Nose

On the nose, Ripe plum, black cherry, cedar, and graham cracker. On the palate, sweet tobacco, dried herbs, blackberries, rich dark chocolate, and toast. Juicy and plush, with fine tannins but a layer of rich creaminess here also carries through to the finish - enjoyable now and for years to come.

name: Duckhorn  Canvasback "Red Mountain" Cabernet Sauvignon
style: Cabernet Sauvignon, 7% Merlot, 7% Malbec, 2% Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: This lush and alluring Cabernet Sauvignon begins with aromas of ripe strawberry, grenadine and Bing cherry that leap from the glass, followed by notes of sarsaparilla, root spices, black licorice cinnamon and star anise. On the palate it is both luxurious and energetic, with plush tannins and ripe red berry flavors balanced by enlivening energy and sophisticated hints of cigar wrapper, granitic minerality and sweet baking spices.
desc_en_full: Home to some of Washington State’s most celebrated vineyards, Red Mountain is renowned for Cabernet Sauvignon. With ideal southwest-facing slopes and significant day and nighttime temperature swings, this small but highly prized area in Eastern Washington produces exceptional wines. Named for the Canvasback duck, which is native to the Pacific Flyway, this wine was crafted from grapes cultivated by some of Red Mountain’s finest growers. With Canvasback, our aim is to express and develop the depth, structure and sophistication that define wines from this young and exciting appellation.

name: Pastourelle De Clerc Milon  "Le 2nd Vin De Chateau Clerc Milon" 2016
wine_color: A beautiful, radiant colour with glittering highlights.
desc_en_short: Discreet and elegant nose which opens up with airing to reveal succulent aromas of brandied morello cherries mingled with peppery, minty notes. From a substantial and powerful attack, the mid-palate reveals a close-knit, creamy texture set off by well-integrated, structured tannins. The succulence hinted at on the nose is expressed in black fruit and mocha notes, leading into an opulent finish which underlines the wine’s complexity.
desc_en_full:
The weather pattern in Pauillac in 2016 had two main features:

A very wet winter and spring, with more than half the annual rainfall occurring in the first four months of the year, including a record 240mm in January.

An extremely dry summer and autumn, without any significant rainfall, leading to a negative water balance at the end of the year.

The crop was large but the grapes remained rather small as a consequence of the summer drought, making for dense and concentrated wines.

The grapes ripened slowly in hot weather in August and September, achieving excellent maturity. The harvest started on 28 September with the Merlot parcels and continued in perfect conditions until 15 October.

Each grape variety and each parcel were vinified separately as usual, in order to allow them to express their brightest characteristics.

The wines offer remarkable colour, intense aromas of fruit and spice, and a dense mouthfeel enhanced by rich and well-rounded tannins.

By its density and consistency, the 2016 vintage compares with the finest vintages of the 21st century to date.

name: Chateau Pichon  Longueville Baron 2019
desc_en_short: The 2019 Pichon-Longueville Baron is a Bordeaux blend, predominantly comprising 87% Cabernet Sauvignon and 13% Merlot. Originating from the Pauillac region in Bordeaux, France, this red wine, with an alcohol content of 14.5%, has garnered exceptional reviews from various professional critics, highlighting its distinguished quality and aging potential.
desc_en_full:
Chateau Pichon-Longueville Baron Winery

Pichon-Longueville is located on beautifully gravelly soil in the southern part of the commune of Pauillac. In 1694, Jacques de Pichon, Baron de Longueville, married the daughter of Pierre de Rauzan, who originally created the property. Their descendants remained deeply involved with making fine wine, and Raoul de Pichon-Longueville built the present chateau in 1851.

In 1988, following an architectural competition organized by the Centre Georges Pompidou, the château and wine cellar were entirely renovated. A spectacular new winemaking facility enables an exquisite wine to be made under ideal conditions.

name: Chateau Cos D'Estournel  Saint-Estephe 2018
wine_color: Deep opaque ruby-black, signaling great concentration and long aging potential.
desc_en_short:
Cos d’Estournel 2018 is the embodiment of power wrapped in elegance. Predominantly made from Cabernet Sauvignon, its structure is bold and muscular, while Merlot contributes a plush texture and softness that balances the wine beautifully. It’s a wine of depth, opulence, and poise—with dark fruit, spice, and mineral nuances that evoke both intrigue and sophistication.

Despite its concentration, the wine maintains remarkable balance, showcasing vibrant acidity and refined tannins. It is a Bordeaux that feels like "strength draped in velvet"—confident yet composed.
desc_en_full:
Château Cos d’Estournel is one of the finest estates in Saint-Estèphe, celebrated not only for its exceptional wines but also for its unique identity. Founded by Louis-Gaspard d’Estournel, a visionary who fell in love with the East and traded his wines extensively with India, the estate earned him the nickname “The Maharajah of Saint-Estèphe.” The château itself is iconic, adorned with Moorish arches and carved wooden doors imported from Zanzibar—making it one of the most visually distinct châteaux in all of Bordeaux.

The vineyards lie on a gravel-rich southern-facing slope near the famous Château Lafite-Rothschild, within a highly prized terroir of the Médoc. The 2018 vintage benefited from near-perfect weather conditions, producing grapes of incredible intensity and balance.

name: Domaine Marchand -Grillot  Gevrey-Chambertin 'Enjouise'
desc_en_short:
This wine offers a textbook expression of Gevrey-Chambertin, with notes of wild cherry, dried flowers, and subtle earthiness, supported by mineral-driven tension and gentle oak integration.

Elegant and focused, this Pinot Noir will evolve gracefully over 5–10 years, revealing new dimensions with cellaring while already charming in its youthful precision.
desc_en_full:
Domaine Marchand-Grillot is a historic family estate based in Gevrey-Chambertin, with winemaking roots going back to the 19th century. Today, the 7th generation leads the domaine, crafting wines with respect for terroir and a minimal intervention philosophy.

The vineyard site “Enjouise” lies within the prestigious Gevrey-Chambertin appellation in Burgundy’s Côte de Nuits, known for producing structured and long-lived Pinot Noir of great complexity and depth.

name: Opus One  Vintage 2021
desc_en_short: The Opus One 2021 effuses seductive, dark-fruit aromas of crushed cassis, blackberry, and black cherry with subtle hints of violet, vanilla, and baking spices. Bright and fresh on the attack, concentrated flavors mirror the aromas and are coupled with an exceptionally plush, creamy texture in the mid-palate from the fine-grained tannins. Poignant notes of black tea, licorice, and dark chocolate lead to a long lingering finish. With attentive cellaring, the wine will delight for decades to come.
desc_en_full:
Opus One Vintage 2021 is a true testament to the artistry and precision of Napa Valley winemaking. Created through the collaboration of legendary producers, Opus One is the embodiment of elegance, depth, and complexity.

Tasting Notes: Deep garnet in color, this wine reveals an aromatic profile rich in dark fruit such as black currant , plum , and blackberry. The palate is full-bodied, with well-integrated tannins that offer a silky smooth texture. Layers of vanilla, spice, and subtle oak are elegantly woven into the fruit, creating a harmonious balance with a long, refined finish.

Winemaking & Terroir: The Opus One 2021 is crafted from a meticulous blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot and Malbec grapes. Sourced from the prestigious Napa Valley, the wine benefits from the unique terroir—warm days and cool nights—that allows for slow ripening and exceptional fruit character. Aged in French oak barrels to develop further complexity.

Food Pairings & Serving Tips: This luxurious blend pairs beautifully with grilled steaks, roasted lamb, or rich duck dishes. It also complements aged cheeses like Gruyère or Comté. Serve at 16-18°C for optimal enjoyment.

Aging Potential & Cellaring: Opus One 2021 has the potential to age for 10-15 years, developing even greater complexity and refinement over time.

name: Sena  2021
wine_color: A beautiful deep ruby colour with violet hues.
grape_variety: Carmenère, Malbec, Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot
desc_en_short: "Seña 2021 is a beautiful deep ruby colour with violet hues. The nose reveals delicate notes of strawberries, cassis and rose petals, framed by hints of cloves, spice and cocoa. Red fruit and blackcurrants, pastries and dark chocolate make for a delicious palate where the tension is perfectly balanced by the juicy acidity and ne-grained tannins, tracing a smooth and vertical backbone through the palate. is wine can be enjoyed for many years to come."
desc_en_full:
Seña 2021

Aconcagua Valley - Chile

Seña was created in 1995 as a joint venture between Eduardo Chadwick and Robert Mondavi, who shared a dream of making a wine that would show Chile’s full potential, a wine that would come to be welcomed, in time, among the world’s First Growths. Modelled on a Bordeaux style, it would have a Chilean soul given by the Carmenere variety, grown under biodynamic farming principles in the Aconcagua Valley.

Vintage 2021

An outstanding vintage due to moderately cool conditions that translated into a slow smooth ripening of the grapes. Pure, elegant and re ned wine.

The season saw average rainfall with 282mm falling between May and October and this enabled the aquifers in the valley to recover their levels ready for the coming season. With a total heat summation of 1,425 DD, slightly fresher than historical averages, that created an ideal slow growing season. In spring, the temperatures were within historical levels favouring a perfect fruit set. e summer was slightly cooler and cloudier than other years, with some refreshing rain (9mm) in late January, which ensured a gentle ripening of healthy grapes. During late March, the Seña vineyards were blessed with moderately warm days, allowing the grapes to reach perfect maturity.

The harvest got underway as expected with Malbec between late March and mid-April. Next came the Cabernet Sauvignon in the rst two weeks of April, overlapping with the Petit Verdot in the second and third weeks of April. We nalized the harvest with the Carmenere.

The 2021 vintage will be remembered as an exceptional year with ideal conditions resulting in a vibrant wine of great purity. A wine with high aromatic complexity, refreshing acidity and delicious linear palate with ne-grained tannins. A vintage with an extraordinary ageing potential.

name: Torbreck The Pict 2007
wine_color: Totally opaque, black-dark red with a deep brick-red hue.
desc_en_short: A bold and muscular Barossa Mataro, Torbreck The Pict 2007 offers savory intensity and layered complexity, crafted from ancient vines and aged to reveal the full depth of this noble Rhône variety.
desc_en_full:
A Warrior’s Wine from the Old Vines of Barossa

Torbreck’s inspiration lies in reviving ancient Rhône grapes through the sun-drenched soils of Barossa Valley. Among these, Mataro (also known as Mourvèdre) holds a powerful, rustic charm — one that the winery felt deserved its own spotlight. Thus, “The Pict” was born: a single-vineyard wine named after a fierce Celtic tribe known for their strength and endurance — qualities mirrored in this wine.

The 2007 vintage brought warm conditions and low yields, concentrating flavors in the old, dry-grown Mataro vines. Harvested at peak ripeness, the fruit underwent gentle fermentation and was matured in fine-grain French oak for two years, allowing tannins to soften while preserving the wine’s bold personality.

The result is a wine that speaks of place and time — an iron-fisted red wrapped in velvet, expressive of ancient vines and bold ambitions. “The Pict” isn't just a name; it’s a statement of power, purity and place.

name: Torbreck Factor 2007
wine_color: Deep, opaque crimson-purple with a dark, brooding core.
desc_en_short: Torbreck Factor 2007 is a bold, muscular Barossa Shiraz with deep, dark fruit character, spicy complexity and ripe tannin structure — a wine of power and refinement that showcases top‑tier vintage material.
desc_en_full:
The Iconic Barossa Shiraz with Bold Expression

Torbreck Vintners crafted Factor as one of their signature single‑vineyard Shirazes — a wine designed to express the fullness and intensity of old Barossa fruit in refined, structured form. Unlike blends that incorporate multiple varieties, Factor focuses purely on Shiraz, allowing the grape’s natural depth, spice and texture to be the central story.

The 2007 vintage brought warm conditions that encouraged phenolic ripeness and concentration in the Barossa fruit. The grapes for Factor were harvested with precision, fermented to retain purity of flavor and aged in premium French oak to add complexity while maintaining fresh fruit expression. This process resulted in a wine that combines Barossa’s hallmark generosity with meticulous winemaking discipline.

Factor 2007 stands as a benchmark of Barossa Shiraz for its balance of power and elegance. With dense fruit layers and a structure that supports aging, it rewards both immediate enjoyment and thoughtful cellaring — a testament to the region’s capacity for world‑class red wines.

name: Chateau Pichon Longueville Baron 2009
desc_en_short: Château Pichon Longueville Baron 2009 is a benchmark Pauillac from one of Bordeaux's most celebrated Second Growth estates — a powerful, age-worthy blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot shaped by the legendary 2009 growing season and the estate's storied gravelly terroir.
desc_en_full:
Few estates command the Médoc skyline — or the imagination of Bordeaux lovers — quite like Château Pichon Longueville Baron. Perched on the gravelly ridges of the southern Pauillac commune, this Deuxième Grand Cru Classé has roots stretching back to 1694, when Jacques de Pichon, Baron de Longueville, united the property through marriage with the de Rauzan family. Since charting its own course in the 19th century, Pichon Baron has become a byword for the muscular, aristocratic style that defines Pauillac at its finest.

The 2009 vintage is widely regarded as one of the greatest in modern Bordeaux history. A warm, generously sunny growing season delivered exceptional ripeness across the Médoc, and Pichon Baron's oldest vines — planted deep into the estate's signature iron-rich gravel over clay — responded with remarkable concentration and natural balance. The Grand Vin was aged in a high proportion of new French oak in the estate's state-of-the-art cellars, rebuilt after an architectural competition organised by the Centre Georges Pompidou.

In the glass, the 2009 presents a deep, saturated ruby. The nose layers dark cassis, crushed blackberry, and ripe plum alongside cigar box, graphite, and the unmistakable mineral intensity of Pauillac's terroir. The palate is full-bodied and structured, with the high, fine-grained tannins of Cabernet Sauvignon perfectly framed by the richer, velvety weight of Merlot. Earthy, cedar-spiced notes unfold with every sip, and the finish is long and authoritative.

Decant generously and pair with a châteaubriand, slow-braised lamb shoulder, or a well-aged hard cheese. This is a wine built for the cellar but magnificent now with patience.

Celebrate the holiday season with Bin 389, a benchmark Penfolds wine. The structure of Cabernet Sauvignon meets the richness of South Australian Shiraz. This 2023 vintage leaves a lasting impression of power and elegance, layered with dark fruit complexity, fine tannins, and a long, savoury finish.

Signed, sealed, delivered. From Penfolds... to the World.
desc_en_full: Bin 389 was often referred to as ‘Baby Grange’, in part because components of the wine are matured in the same barrels that held the previous vintage of Grange. First made in 1960 by the legendary Max Schubert, this was the wine that helped forge Penfolds reputation with red wine drinkers by combining the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon with the richness of Shiraz. Exemplifying the judicious balance of fruit and oak, Bin 389 highlights the generous mid-palate Penfolds is known for.

name: Penfolds FWT 585 Cabernet Merot Petit Verdot 2022 with Gift Box
wine_color: Deep crimson with a vibrant garnet core.
desc_en_short:
Celebrate the holiday season with Penfolds FWT 585, a French Winemaking Trial shaped by Bordeaux vineyards and honed by Penfolds signature House Style.

Encased in this limited-release gift box, is the 2022 vintage. Classic Bordeaux varietals of Cabernet, Merlot and Petit Verdot meet Penfolds signature fruit-forward, mid palate generosity. Explore black cherry, chocolate mint and delicate violet notes.

Signed, sealed, delivered. From Penfolds... to the World.
desc_en_full:
A Tale of Two Hemispheres

The "FWT" in the name stands for French Winemaking Trial, a project that represents Penfolds' ambitious venture beyond Australian shores to explore the celebrated terroirs of Bordeaux. Guided by the philosophy that a "House Style" transcends geography, Penfolds winemakers apply their time-honored techniques—focusing on multi-regional blending and precise oak maturation—to fruit sourced from exceptional vineyards across the Médoc. The result is a wine that feels familiar to Penfolds loyalists yet distinctly French in its geological DNA.

The 2022 vintage was shaped by a warm, dry growing season in Bordeaux, resulting in thick-skinned berries with immense concentration and color. This release features a blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot, matured for 12 months in a combination of French and American oak barriques. This dual-oak regime is a Penfolds signature, imparting layers of sweet spice and creaminess that wrap around the firm, structural tannins typical of the Haut-Médoc region.

While many Bordeaux wines are defined strictly by their estate boundaries, FWT 585 is defined by a flavor profile: bold, generous, and meticulously balanced. It bridges the gap between the Old World and the New, offering the savory complexity of French soil with the polished, fruit-forward accessibility that has made Penfolds a global icon. Ideally decanted before serving, it is a wine built to evolve gracefully in the cellar.

name: Penfolds Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz Lunar New Year Horse Edition (750 ml)
desc_en_short: Penfolds Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz arrives in a limited Lunar New Year Horse Edition, blending 51% Cabernet Sauvignon with 49% Shiraz for a South Australian classic that delivers dark fruit richness, exotic spice, and mouth-coating tannins in an elegant, collectible package.
desc_en_full:
Few Australian red wines carry the heritage and consistency of Penfolds Bin 389. Nicknamed the "Baby Grange" by enthusiasts, this South Australian benchmark has anchored Penfolds' portfolio for decades, earning its reputation through a masterful blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz sourced from across the state's finest growing regions. This special Lunar New Year Horse Edition honours the Year of the Horse — a symbol of curiosity, momentum, and new beginnings — making it as compelling a gift as it is a drinking experience.

The 2023 vintage opens with a deep plum-red colour and a generous nose of blackberries, ripe plums, and dried herbs, underscored by toasty, integrated oak and subtle savoury complexity. Hints of cinnamon and exotic spice keep the aromatics lively without overwhelming the fruit.

On the palate, the 51% Cabernet Sauvignon asserts classic structure and backbone while 49% Shiraz contributes warmth and mid-palate weight. Red currant and tart cranberry mingle with vanilla sweetness, black olive, and a touch of orange zest. Fine, mouth-coating tannins carry the wine through a long, elegant finish that speaks equally of power and restraint.

Ideal alongside a slow-roasted leg of lamb, grilled beef ribeye, a well-aged cheddar, or anything coming off the barbecue. Just as rewarding poured into a decanter on a celebratory Chinese New Year table as it is tucked away for a few years of cellaring.

name: Louis Roederer  Blanc de Blancs 2017
wine_color: Intense yellow hue with bright, luminous tints.
desc_en_short: Intense yellow hue with bright, luminous tints. An even flow of creamy, ultra-fine bubbles. Bouquet of harvest (almonds), spring flowers (honeysuckle) and citrus (lemon). The aromas are harmonious and deep, with delicate notes of roasted almonds and roasting. Upon aeration, powdery and chalky nuances emerge, creating a refined reductive nose typical of exceptional Chardonnays. Precise, juicy and deep on entry to the palate, this wine exudes a magnificent concentration of ripe fruit and floral aromas interwoven with a mineral freshness. The mouthfeel is creamy and chalky, almost powdery and drying, imparting a trim, finely honed quality to the palate. Juicy and rounded, the wine is drawn out into a fresh, powdery finish. The palate crescendos like a torrent of white mountain water, with a lingering sweetness from the wood ageing complementing the saline length and structure imparted by the white chalk of Avize.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Vaudon  Chablis
desc_en_short: "A dry and fruity wine, easy to drink. Its colour is pale gold with greenish hues. Very fresh aromas reminiscent of citrus (lemon or grapefruit); small pleasant touches of fern or coriander are found as well. On the palate, dry and fruity, with mineral notes. Pleasant and long aftertaste".
desc_en_full:
The Moulin de Vaudon, the property of Joseph Drouhin, is an 18th Century watermill straddling the Serein River, close to the Grand Cru vineyards of Chablis. Flowing gently past hillsides covered with vineyards, the river has always been closely identified with Chablis and its region. Because of its unique location at the heart of their 38 hectare vineyard estate (95 acres), the historical watermill is the headquarters of the Drouhin Domaine in Chablis.

Joseph Drouhin, a precursor and pioneer in this great wine region for 45 years, strengthens the identity of the prestigious Chablis Domaine. Starting with the 2008 vintage, the name "Vaudon" was associated with Joseph Drouhin for all its Chablis wines as a sign of the firm's allegiance to this historical terroir.

Harvested from our estates and elite vineyard sites in California’s North Coast, this rich and elegant Sauvignon Blanc was blended with Sémillon to add depth and complexity. This vibrant Sauvignon Blanc offers an enticing medley of tropical and citrus aromas, including pineapple, lemon verbena, lime and guava. The palate is lush and lively, combining a silky texture and energetic acidity with fresh melon, citrus, and pineapple flavors framed by subtle minerality throughout a long, bright finish.

name: Jermann  Pinot Grigio DOC
wine_color: It has an intense straw-yellow colour
desc_en_short: Silvio Jermann's Pinot Grigio is a benchmark example from Friuli-Venezia Giulia — a fresh, elegant white with crisp green apple, pear, and delicate floral notes, underpinned by clean minerality and a pleasing, food-friendly finish.
desc_en_full:
Jermann is one of Friuli-Venezia Giulia's most celebrated estates, and their Pinot Grigio stands as a classic example of the region's ability to produce white wines with both character and immediacy. Unlike flatter versions of this grape grown further south, Jermann's Pinot Grigio benefits from the cooling Adriatic influence and the estate's careful canopy management, resulting in a wine with genuine freshness and definition.

Pale straw in colour with golden glints, the wine opens on fresh green apple, ripe pear, and subtle white peach, lifted by delicate floral notes of acacia. The palate is crisp and clean, with refreshing natural acidity, a lightly saline mineral thread, and a dry, lingering finish. This is a wine of understated elegance — not showy, but deeply satisfying.

Versatile at the table, it excels with grilled fish and seafood — particularly sole, sea bass, and langoustines. It also works beautifully with light pasta dishes, risotto with vegetables, and fresh goat's cheese.

name: Heering  Cherry Liqueur (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Heering Cherry Liqueur is the original "Cherry Brandy". contains only natural ingredients including Danish grown cherries. No additives or artificial colouring. Swirl with ice, lean back and chill.
desc_en_full:
Since 1818, Heering Cherry Liqueur has been a fashion accessory, adding extravagance and civilization to the mix of some of the most legendary cocktails of all time, including the original.

name: Vigneti del Salento  `I Muri` Primitivo Puglia
wine_color: Deep ruby red colour with violet reflections.
desc_en_short: Deep ruby red colour with violet reflections. This wine has red berry fruit perfumes with a hint of spice. Full-bodied with firm tannins, this wine is well balanced with lots of ripe fruit flavours.
desc_en_full: This is the new solo venture in Puglia of the Farnese group (responsible for Gran Sasso, Zabu, Vesevo, Tufarello and others). The wines were made at two wineries in the province of Taranto on the western flank of Salento. The style of the wines - fresh whites, soft and generous reds - will be familiar to those who buy their other wines, as winemaker Filippo Baccalaro is the driving force behind this new label. Filippo, a native of Piemonte, has been working in Puglia for almost two decades. He was attracted by the rich, ripe flavours of the fruit, and the ability to capture these flavours with minimal use of technology and a lot of know-how. Filippo has the latter, as is illustrated by these wines. The star wines are, of course, those made from native grapes like Verdeca, Negroamaro and, of course, Primitivo.

desc_en_full: Marsannay is the village which marks the northern gateway to the Côte d'Or when leaving Dijon, the capital of Burgundy and home to the Ducs de Bourgogne. This village marks the beginning of the Route des Grands Crus which follows the N6 highway through the Côte d'Or. Wines from Marsannay are generally lively and robust; characteristics that come from the rich iron soil. Marsannay received its Appellation Contrôlée in 1987 in recognition of its consistently high quality wine.

A New Lucky Charm

For as long as we can remember, an intriguing black cat has shadowed us in our vineyards. For luck, our first winemaker fastened a number-nine amulet to its collar, representing the nine mythical lives of a cat.

This wine was borne of a journey across the World. The hope of the travellers all but ended when they reached the shore. Little did they realise that in the darkest of places beauty and brilliance thrive.

VINEYARD

Our vines grow strong and robust in the heartland of South Australia's vineyard regions. Bright, warm summers and deep sandy loam over limestone soils ensures they fl ourish to produce intensely fl avoured, perfectly ripened grapes.

WINEMAKING

Carefully selected parcels of Shiraz, Grenache and Malbec were picked in the cool light of a new dawn when perfectly ripe, the grapes were crushed and destemmed prior to fermentation under temperature controlled conditions to preserve their vibrant colours and fl avours. Each base wine was assessed and then unifi ed with the skill of a true artisan to create a luscious berry driven palate that intriguing and truly indulgent. A heady blend that sings with vibrancy and fi nishes with a dark, earthy swoon.

name: Belena  Shiraz
desc_en_short: A crowd-pleasing Australian Shiraz from the Calabria Family, delivering spicy dark forest fruit and plum on both nose and palate — approachable, alluring, and made with genuine heart.
desc_en_full:
Behind the Belena label lies a deeply personal story. The wine is crafted by the Calabria Family, one of South Australia's most respected multi-generational wine dynasties, and the name pays direct homage to Bill and Lena Calabria — the second generation who carried the family's winemaking ambitions through hard times with quiet determination and grace. That heritage of resilience shows in every bottle: honest, well-made wine designed to be shared.

This South Australian Shiraz opens with rich, inviting aromas of spicy dark forest fruit and ripe plum — the classic cool-to-warm climate Shiraz signature that Australia does so well. The palate follows through faithfully, with medium body and balanced tannins making it accessible without ever feeling thin. A gentle spice thread adds complexity and lifts the finish.

Certified vegan friendly, Belena Shiraz suits a wide range of tables and tastes. Pour it alongside a slow-braised lamb shoulder, a flame-grilled rib-eye, or a hearty beef stew — the fruit depth and moderate structure complement bold, savoury flavours beautifully. It also holds its own simply opened on a relaxed weeknight. Reliable, generous, and made with genuine family pride.

name: Finca Fella El Maso Garnacha Tintorera
desc_en_short: A bold, single‑variety expression from Almansa using old bush‑vines of Garnacha Tintorera at high altitude, offering remarkable intensity, purity and depth for its region.
desc_en_full:
High Altitude Heritage in Almansa

On the sun‑baked slopes of D.O. Almansa in southeast Spain, vines of over 60 years old of Garnacha Tintorera flourish at around 1,090 metres above sea level, rooted in red, iron‑rich soils over a limestone base. The estate behind El Maso embraced this uniquely local varietal to craft a wine that speaks of both terroir and tradition.

From vineyard to bottle, the grapes are selected with precision, undergo a maceration/fermentation of approximately 25 days, followed by malolactic fermentation and around 12 months’ ageing in American and French oak barrels. The result is a wine where power meets finesse, and where the altitude’s freshness tempers the heady fruit.

El Maso embodies a vision of modern Spanish fine wine: authentic, serious and reflective of place. It invites the drinker to explore Almansa’s lesser‑known heights and grape expression with confidence and pleasure.

name: Frangelico  (700 ml)
desc_en_short: Clear Pale Gold., Intense hazelnut aromas., Rich texture. Pronounced but delicate hazelnut flavour with hints of vanilla and dark chocolate.  Smooth, long finish with fragrant back-note.
desc_en_full: Tonda Gentile hazelnuts are known for being bigger, plumper and more uniform, with a skin that sheds easily, perfect for toasting without a bitter taste. Their sweet flavour is the reason they are so sought after by internationally known confectionery and worldwide popular gianduia chocolate creams. Tonda Gentile hazelnuts, grown in the Langhe, an area of rural hills and picturesque villages in southern Piedmont, are also used to obtain the distillate for Frangelico. The hazelnuts once toasted and distilled with alcohol, are married with other flavoring preparations including cocoa, coffee, vanilla according to the secret recipe and transformed into more than 3 millions bottles of Frangelico for enjoyment around the world, as well as in their native Italy.

name: Au Bon Climat  Santa Barbara County
desc_en_short: This wine is particularly focused and earthy, with a beautifully balanced and integrated acidity. Elegant and complex, this wine has a lovely spice component which becomes ever present while sipping.
desc_en_full:
It is difficult to mention Santa Barbara County Pinot Noir without bringing up the Alexander Payne movie Sideways. The release of the movie could not have come at a better time. Prior to Sideways, Santa Barbara County Pinot Noir was on its way to stardom. A new generation of vineyard managers had come on the scene and many understood what needed to be done in the vineyard to improve Pinot Noir and winemakers were getting a better idea of how to deal with Pinot Noir in the winery. The convergence of better grapes, better winemaking and a very popular and entertaining movie, led to an explosion in the popularity of Pinot Noir and mainly Santa Barbara County Pinot Noir. Even though the movie was a 2004 release, we still feel the effects over a decade later.

Au Bon Climat has been making Pinot Noir from Santa Barbara County grapes since 1982, 23 years before Sideways. In the 1980s and 90s the product mix was at Au Bon Climat was 75% to 80% Chardonnay to 20% to 25% Pinot Noir. Chardonnay was king and people had not discovered the joys of Pinot Noir. All this began to change in the late 90s and now Au Bon Climat makes an equal amount of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

Strawberries and blackberries aromas slowly rise out of the glass. Overtime, more of the anise and clove will mix with these other aromas. Currently, the wine is young with mouth filling intensity and suppleness. Complexity will increase as the wine ages, but this wine should stay "fresh" for 6 or 7 years. Like all Au Bon Climat wines, this is a food wine. In its youth, match this wine with lamb or dried meats. After a few years, as the flavors coalesce, it will pair better with salmon or mild red sauce with pasta. We have enjoyed this wine with many food choices from spicy Indian dishes to all-American sandwiches, and the wine worked well with most anything. The possible food pairings are almost endless.

name: Chateau Les Clavelines Saint Emilion Grand Cru
wine_color: Deep garnet red with violet reflections
desc_en_short: A refined Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2022 highlighting Merlot’s lush fruit and Cabernet Franc’s floral spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and elegant — a modern Right Bank Bordeaux with classic charm.
desc_en_full:
Château Les Clavelines Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2022 embodies the elegance and depth that define the Right Bank of Bordeaux. Crafted with precision and care, this wine reflects both modern winemaking expertise and the timeless allure of Saint-Émilion’s limestone terroir.

Tasting Notes: Deep garnet red with violet reflections, the nose opens with aromas of ripe black plum, blackberry, and hints of violet and sweet spice. The palate is round and full, offering layers of dark fruit, mocha, and soft vanilla from gentle oak aging. Smooth tannins and bright acidity create a velvety texture, leading to a long, polished finish with notes of cocoa and earth.

Winemaking & Terroir: A blend of 80% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Franc, sourced from clay-limestone slopes around Saint-Émilion. The grapes are hand-harvested at optimal ripeness and fermented in temperature-controlled vats before being aged in French oak barrels for 12–14 months. The 2022 vintage — warm and generous — produced wines of concentration, elegance, and freshness.

name: Larkmead Solari Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
desc_en_short: A legendary, single-vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Larkmead, known for its immense power, concentration, and long aging potential.
desc_en_full:
The Sun’s Light on the Valley Floor Larkmead is one of Napa Valley's oldest and most historically important estates, located in the northern Calistoga AVA. 'Solari' (Sun) is their single-vineyard flagship Cabernet, made from a special, intensely concentrated block.

The Calistoga area’s intense sun and volcanic soil yield wines of massive power and scale. The Solari is crafted with meticulous care, utilizing native yeast and extensive barrel aging to achieve profound complexity and a seamless texture.

Solari is a wine of monumental structure, perfect balance, and profound concentration. It is a defining statement of luxury, modern Napa winemaking, and the potential of the Calistoga terroir.

name: Chateau Montelena  Chardonnay
desc_en_short: There’s a wonderful fullness and weight on the palate punctuated by ripe melon, green apple, and fresh citrus. The oak contribution remains subtle with a hint of vanilla and baking spice.
desc_en_full:
Chateau Montelena Chardonnay

Nose : Ripe peach, pear and cloves dominate the nose amidst a flurry of floral and honeysuckle notes.

Palate : There's a wonderful fullness and weight on the palate punctuated by ripe melon, green apple, and fresh citrus. The oak contribution remains subtle with a hint of vanilla and baking spice.

Finish : The density of the wine is readily apparent as grilled pineapple, lychee, and a rich creaminess linger.

desc_en_full: Louis Henry Denis Jadot founded Maison Louis Jadot in 1859. The family operations by buying numerous vineyards, including Corton Charlemagne and Chevalier Montrachet Les Demoiselles. The next generation, under the guidance of Louis Auguste Jadot, developed new export markets, particularly those in England and the US, with the help of Rudy Kopf, the founder of the Kobrand Corporation.
